```python
    data_residency: list[str]    # ['US', 'EU']
    has_data_classification: bool
    employee_security_training: bool
    training_frequency: str


# Question keyword patterns to category mapping
CATEGORY_PATTERNS = [
    (QuestionCategory.ACCESS_CONTROL,
     re.compile(r'(?:mfa|multi.factor|sso|single sign|rbac|least privilege|access control|privileged)', re.I)),
    (QuestionCategory.DATA_ENCRYPTION,
     re.compile(r'(?:encrypt|cipher|tls|https|at.rest|in.transit|key management)', re.I)),
    (QuestionCategory.INCIDENT_RESPONSE,
     re.compile(r'(?:incident|breach|forensic|response plan|notification|escalat)', re.I)),
    (QuestionCategory.VULNERABILITY_MANAGEMENT,
     re.compile(r'(?:patch|vulnerability|scan|pentest|penetration test|cve|cvss)', re.I)),
    (QuestionCategory.BUSINESS_CONTINUITY,
     re.compile(r'(?:backup|recovery|rto|rpo|disaster|continuity|availability|uptime)', re.I)),
    (QuestionCategory.COMPLIANCE,
     re.compile(r'(?:iso 27001|soc 2|pci dss|gdpr|hipaa|compliance|audit|certif)', re.I)),
    (QuestionCategory.LOGGING_MONITORING,
     re.compile(r'(?:log|monitor|siem|alert|audit trail|detection|observab)', re.I)),
]


def classify_question(text: str) -> QuestionCategory:
    for category, pattern in CATEGORY_PATTERNS:
        if pattern.search(text):
            return category
    return QuestionCategory.ACCESS_CONTROL  # Default


class VendorRiskAutoFiller:
    """
    AI-powered security questionnaire auto-filler.
    Given an organization's security profile, generates accurate,
    consistent answers to vendor risk assessment questionnaires.
    Reduces the time to complete repetitive security questionnaires
    from hours to minutes while maintaining accuracy.
    """

    def __init__(self, profile: SecurityProfile):
        self.profile = profile

    def _answer_access_control(self, q: SecurityQuestion) -> GeneratedAnswer:
        text = q.text.lower()
        evidence = []

        if 'mfa' in text or 'multi' in text:
            ans = 'Yes' if self.profile.has_mfa else 'No'
            evidence = ['MFA Policy', 'Identity Provider Configuration']
            rationale = (
                f'{self.profile.org_name} enforces MFA for all user accounts accessing '
                f'production systems via our identity provider.'
            ) if self.profile.has_mfa else 'MFA is not currently deployed.'
            return GeneratedAnswer(
                question_id=q.question_id, answer=ans, confidence=0.95,
                rationale=rationale, evidence_references=evidence,
                requires_human_review=not self.profile.has_mfa
            )

        if 'sso' in text or 'single sign' in text:
            ans = 'Yes' if self.profile.has_sso else 'No'
            return GeneratedAnswer(
                question_id=q.question_id, answer=ans, confidence=0.95,
                rationale=f'SSO is {"implemented" if self.profile.has_sso else "not implemented"}.',
                evidence_references=['SSO Configuration Documentation'],
                requires_human_review=False
            )

        if 'least privilege' in text or 'rbac' in text:
            return GeneratedAnswer(
                question_id=q.question_id, answer='Yes', confidence=0.85,
                rationale='Role-Based Access Control is implemented per our Access Control Policy.',
                evidence_references=['Access Control Policy', 'ISO 27001 A.9'],
                requires_human_review=False
            )

        return GeneratedAnswer(
            question_id=q.question_id, answer='Yes', confidence=0.7,
            rationale='Access controls are implemented per our security policies.',
            evidence_references=['Access Control Policy'], requires_human_review=True
        )

    def _answer_encryption(self, q: SecurityQuestion) -> GeneratedAnswer:
        text = q.text.lower()
        algos = ', '.join(self.profile.encryption_algorithms)

        if 'at rest' in text or 'at-rest' in text or 'storage' in text:
            ans = 'Yes' if self.profile.encrypts_at_rest else 'No'
            return GeneratedAnswer(
                question_id=q.question_id, answer=ans, confidence=0.97,
                rationale=f'All data at rest is encrypted using {algos}.',
                evidence_references=['Data Encryption Policy', 'Infrastructure Security Standard'],
                requires_human_review=False
            )

        if 'transit' in text or 'tls' in text or 'transport' in text:
            ans = 'Yes' if self.profile.encrypts_in_transit else 'No'
            return GeneratedAnswer(
                question_id=q.question_id, answer=ans, confidence=0.97,
                rationale=f'All data in transit uses {algos}.',
                evidence_references=['Data Encryption Policy', 'Network Security Standard'],
                requires_human_review=False
            )

        if 'algorithm' in text or 'cipher' in text:
            return GeneratedAnswer(
                question_id=q.question_id, answer=algos, confidence=0.95,
                rationale=f'Encryption standards used: {algos}.',
                evidence_references=['Encryption Policy'], requires_human_review=False
            )

        return GeneratedAnswer(
            question_id=q.question_id, answer='Yes', confidence=0.9,
            rationale=f'Encryption is implemented using {algos}.',
            evidence_references=['Encryption Policy'], requires_human_review=False
        )

    def _answer_compliance(self, q: SecurityQuestion) -> GeneratedAnswer:
        certs = ', '.join(self.profile.certifications) if self.profile.certifications else 'None'
        for cert in self.profile.certifications:
            if cert.lower().replace(' ', '') in q.text.lower().replace(' ', ''):
                return GeneratedAnswer(
                    question_id=q.question_id, answer='Yes', confidence=0.99,
                    rationale=f'{self.profile.org_name} holds {cert} certification.',
                    evidence_references=[f'{cert} Certificate', f'{cert} Audit Report'],
                    requires_human_review=False
                )
        return GeneratedAnswer(
            question_id=q.question_id, answer='No', confidence=0.85,
            rationale=f'Current certifications: {certs}. Requested standard not held.',
            evidence_references=[], requires_human_review=True
        )

    def answer_question(self, question: SecurityQuestion) -> GeneratedAnswer:
        category = question.category
        dispatch = {
            QuestionCategory.ACCESS_CONTROL: self._answer_access_control,
            QuestionCategory.DATA_ENCRYPTION: self._answer_encryption,
            QuestionCategory.COMPLIANCE: self._answer_compliance,
        }
        handler = dispatch.get(category)
        if handler:
            return handler(question)

        # Generic fallback
        return GeneratedAnswer(
            question_id=question.question_id,
            answer='Please review manually.',
            confidence=0.3,
            rationale='This question requires manual review by security team.',
            evidence_references=[],
            requires_human_review=True
        )

    def complete_questionnaire(
        self,
        questions: list[SecurityQuestion]
    ) -> list[GeneratedAnswer]:
        return [self.answer_question(q) for q in questions]
```

instruction: Write a Python script to implement automated CVE impact analysis for a software bill of materials (SBOM).
output:
```python
import json
import re
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Optional
from enum import Enum


class CVSSRating(Enum):
    CRITICAL = 'CRITICAL'   # 9.0-10.0
    HIGH = 'HIGH'           # 7.0-8.9
    MEDIUM = 'MEDIUM'       # 4.0-6.9
    LOW = 'LOW'             # 0.1-3.9
    NONE = 'NONE'           # 0.0


@dataclass
class SBOMComponent:
    name: str
    version: str
    package_type: str   # 'npm', 'pypi', 'maven', 'cargo', 'go'
    purl: str           # Package URL: pkg:pypi/requests@2.28.0
    supplier: Optional[str] = None
    license: Optional[str] = None
    direct_dependency: bool = True
    transitive_depth: int = 0


@dataclass
class CVERecord:
    cve_id: str
    description: str
    cvss_score: float
    cvss_vector: str
    severity: CVSSRating
    affected_versions: str  # e.g., '< 2.29.0'
    fixed_version: Optional[str]
    published_date: str
    exploit_available: bool
    exploit_in_wild: bool
    cwe_ids: list[str]


@dataclass
class ComponentVulnerabilityReport:
    component: SBOMComponent
    vulnerabilities: list[CVERecord]
    highest_severity: CVSSRating
    exploitable_count: int
    in_the_wild_count: int
    recommended_action: str
    remediation: Optional[str]
    risk_score: float


@dataclass
class SBOMAnalysisReport:
    sbom_name: str
    analysis_timestamp: str
    total_components: int
    vulnerable_components: int
    critical_count: int
    high_count: int
    exploitable_count: int
    component_reports: list[ComponentVulnerabilityReport]
    top_risks: list[ComponentVulnerabilityReport]
    remediation_priority: list[str]
    overall_risk_score: float


def cvss_to_rating(score: float) -> CVSSRating:
    if score >= 9.0: return CVSSRating.CRITICAL
    elif score >= 7.0: return CVSSRating.HIGH
    elif score >= 4.0: return CVSSRating.MEDIUM
    elif score > 0: return CVSSRating.LOW
    return CVSSRating.NONE


# Simulated CVE database (in production: query NVD, OSV.dev, GitHub Advisory API)
SIMULATED_CVE_DB: dict[str, list[CVERecord]] = {
    'requests': [
        CVERecord(
            cve_id='CVE-2023-32681', description='Requests forwards Proxy-Authorization header to redirect targets.',
            cvss_score=6.1, cvss_vector='CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N',
            severity=CVSSRating.MEDIUM, affected_versions='< 2.31.0',
            fixed_version='2.31.0', published_date='2023-05-26',
            exploit_available=True, exploit_in_wild=False,
            cwe_ids=['CWE-200']
        )
    ],
    'pillow': [
        CVERecord(
            cve_id='CVE-2023-44271', description='Pillow uncontrolled resource consumption in PIL.ImageFont.',
            cvss_score=7.5, cvss_vector='CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H',
            severity=CVSSRating.HIGH, affected_versions='< 10.0.0',
            fixed_version='10.0.0', published_date='2023-11-03',
            exploit_available=False, exploit_in_wild=False,
            cwe_ids=['CWE-400']
        )
    ],
    'cryptography': [
        CVERecord(
            cve_id='CVE-2023-49083', description='NULL pointer dereference in PKCS12 parsing.',
            cvss_score=7.5, cvss_vector='CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H',
            severity=CVSSRating.HIGH, affected_versions='< 41.0.6',
            fixed_version='41.0.6', published_date='2023-11-29',
            exploit_available=False, exploit_in_wild=False,
            cwe_ids=['CWE-476']
        )
    ],
}


def is_version_affected(component_version: str, affected_range: str) -> bool:
    """Simple version comparison: supports '< X.Y.Z' and '<= X.Y.Z'."""
    m = re.match(r'(<|<=|>|>=|=)\s*([\d.]+)', affected_range.strip())
    if not m:
        return True  # Conservative: assume affected if can't parse
    op, ver_str = m.groups()
    try:
        def parse(v): return tuple(int(x) for x in v.split('.'))
        comp = parse(component_version)
        ref = parse(ver_str)
        return {'<': comp < ref, '<=': comp <= ref,
                '>': comp > ref, '>=': comp >= ref, '=': comp == ref}.get(op, False)
    except (ValueError, AttributeError):
        return True


def compute_risk_score(
    cves: list[CVERecord],
    component: SBOMComponent
) -> float:
    if not cves:
        return 0.0
    base = max(c.cvss_score for c in cves)
    exploitability_bonus = sum(2.0 for c in cves if c.exploit_available)
    wild_bonus = sum(3.0 for c in cves if c.exploit_in_wild)
    transitive_penalty = max(0.0, 1.0 - component.transitive_depth * 0.1)
    return round(min((base + exploitability_bonus + wild_bonus) * transitive_penalty, 10.0), 2)


class SBOMVulnerabilityAnalyzer:
    """
    Automated CVE impact analysis for Software Bill of Materials.
    Matches SBOM components against vulnerability databases,
    checks version ranges, and prioritizes remediation by exploitability
    and component criticality (direct vs transitive dependency).
    """

    def analyze_component(
        self,
        component: SBOMComponent
    ) -> ComponentVulnerabilityReport:
        pkg_name = component.name.lower().replace('-', '_')
        potential_cves = SIMULATED_CVE_DB.get(pkg_name, [])

        active_cves = [
            cve for cve in potential_cves
            if is_version_affected(component.version, cve.affected_versions)
        ]

        if not active_cves:
            return ComponentVulnerabilityReport(
                component=component, vulnerabilities=[],
                highest_severity=CVSSRating.NONE,
                exploitable_count=0, in_the_wild_count=0,
                recommended_action='monitor',
                remediation=None, risk_score=0.0
            )

        highest_score = max(c.cvss_score for c in active_cves)
        highest_severity = cvss_to_rating(highest_score)
        exploitable = sum(1 for c in active_cves if c.exploit_available)
        in_wild = sum(1 for c in active_cves if c.exploit_in_wild)

        risk_score = compute_risk_score(active_cves, component)

        fixed_versions = [c.fixed_version for c in active_cves if c.fixed_version]
        remediation = f'Upgrade to >= {max(fixed_versions)}' if fixed_versions else 'No fix available - consider alternatives'

        if in_wild > 0 or highest_score >= 9.0:
            action = 'IMMEDIATE: Patch within 24 hours'
        elif exploitable > 0 or highest_score >= 7.0:
            action = 'URGENT: Patch within 7 days'
        elif highest_score >= 4.0:
            action = 'PLANNED: Patch within 30 days'
        else:
            action = 'MONITOR: Patch in next release cycle'

        return ComponentVulnerabilityReport(
            component=component, vulnerabilities=active_cves,
            highest_severity=highest_severity,
            exploitable_count=exploitable, in_the_wild_count=in_wild,
            recommended_action=action, remediation=remediation,
            risk_score=risk_score
        )

    def analyze_sbom(
        self,
        sbom_name: str,
        components: list[SBOMComponent]
    ) -> SBOMAnalysisReport:
        reports = [self.analyze_component(c) for c in components]
        vulnerable = [r for r in reports if r.vulnerabilities]

        total_risk = sum(r.risk_score for r in reports)
        top_risks = sorted(vulnerable, key=lambda r: r.risk_score, reverse=True)[:10]

        priority = [
            f'{r.component.name}@{r.component.version}: {r.recommended_action} ({r.remediation})'
            for r in top_risks
        ]

        return SBOMAnalysisReport(
            sbom_name=sbom_name,
            analysis_timestamp=datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            total_components=len(components),
            vulnerable_components=len(vulnerable),
            critical_count=sum(1 for r in vulnerable if r.highest_severity == CVSSRating.CRITICAL),
            high_count=sum(1 for r in vulnerable if r.highest_severity == CVSSRating.HIGH),
            exploitable_count=sum(r.exploitable_count for r in reports),
            component_reports=reports,
            top_risks=top_risks,
            remediation_priority=priority,
            overall_risk_score=round(total_risk / max(len(components), 1), 2)
        )
```

instruction: Write a Python script to implement a privacy-preserving data synthesis engine using differential privacy for generating synthetic training data.
output:
```python
import numpy as np
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Optional
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.mixture import GaussianMixture


@dataclass
class DPSynthesisConfig:
    epsilon: float = 1.0          # Privacy budget (lower = more private)
    delta: float = 1e-5           # Failure probability
    n_synthetic_samples: int = 1000
    n_gmm_components: int = 5    # Gaussian Mixture components
    clip_bound: float = 1.0       # Sensitivity clipping
    n_histogram_bins: int = 32   # For marginal histograms
    max_categorical_values: int = 20


@dataclass
class SynthesisResult:
    synthetic_data: np.ndarray
    feature_names: list[str]
    epsilon_spent: float
    delta: float
    n_samples: int
    privacy_guarantee: str
    utility_metrics: dict


class DifferentiallyPrivateSynthesizer:
    """
    Differentially private data synthesizer.
    Uses the DP-GMM approach:
    1. Add Gaussian noise to sufficient statistics (means, covariances)
    2. Fit a Gaussian Mixture Model on the noisy statistics
    3. Sample synthetic data from the noisy GMM

    Privacy guarantee: (epsilon, delta)-DP via Gaussian mechanism.
    The synthetic data preserves statistical properties while providing
    formal privacy guarantees — real records are never released.
    """

    def __init__(self, config: Optional[DPSynthesisConfig] = None):
        self.config = config or DPSynthesisConfig()
        self.scaler = StandardScaler()
        self._fitted = False
        self._n_features = 0
        self._noise_scale = 0.0

    def _compute_noise_scale(
        self,
        n_samples: int,
        sensitivity: float
    ) -> float:
        """
        Gaussian mechanism noise scale: sigma = sensitivity * sqrt(2*ln(1.25/delta)) / epsilon
        """
        import math
        sigma = sensitivity * math.sqrt(2 * math.log(1.25 / self.config.delta)) / self.config.epsilon
        return sigma

    def _add_dp_noise(
        self,
        statistic: np.ndarray,
        sensitivity: float,
        rng: np.random.Generator
    ) -> np.ndarray:
        sigma = self._compute_noise_scale(1, sensitivity)
        noise = rng.normal(0, sigma, statistic.shape)
        return statistic + noise

    def fit(
        self,
        X: np.ndarray,
        feature_names: Optional[list[str]] = None
    ) -> 'DifferentiallyPrivateSynthesizer':
        n, d = X.shape
        self._n_features = d
        self._feature_names = feature_names or [f'feature_{i}' for i in range(d)]
        rng = np.random.default_rng(42)

        # Clip inputs to bound sensitivity
        X_clipped = np.clip(X, -self.config.clip_bound, self.config.clip_bound)
        X_scaled = self.scaler.fit_transform(X_clipped)

        # DP statistics: add noise to per-feature means and covariance
        # Sensitivity of mean = 2 * clip_bound / n
        mean_sensitivity = 2 * self.config.clip_bound / n
        noisy_means = self._add_dp_noise(X_scaled.mean(axis=0), mean_sensitivity, rng)

        # Sensitivity of covariance (upper bound)
        cov_sensitivity = 4 * (self.config.clip_bound ** 2) / n
        cov = np.cov(X_scaled.T)
        noisy_cov = self._add_dp_noise(cov, cov_sensitivity, rng)

        # Ensure positive semi-definite covariance
        eigvals = np.linalg.eigvalsh(noisy_cov)
        if eigvals.min() < 0:
            noisy_cov -= eigvals.min() * np.eye(d) * 1.01

        # Fit GMM with DP statistics
        # For a proper DP-GMM we'd add noise to EM sufficient stats
        # Here we use the noisy covariance as initialization
        self._noisy_means = noisy_means
        self._noisy_cov = noisy_cov
        self._n_samples_approx = n
        self._fitted = True
        self._noise_scale = self._compute_noise_scale(n, mean_sensitivity)
        return self

    def sample(
        self,
        n_samples: Optional[int] = None
    ) -> SynthesisResult:
        if not self._fitted:
            raise RuntimeError('Must call fit() before sample()')

        n = n_samples or self.config.n_synthetic_samples
        rng = np.random.default_rng()

        # Sample from multivariate normal with noisy parameters
        try:
            synthetic_scaled = rng.multivariate_normal(
                self._noisy_means, self._noisy_cov, size=n
            )
        except np.linalg.LinAlgError:
            # Fallback: diagonal covariance
            synthetic_scaled = rng.multivariate_normal(
                self._noisy_means,
                np.diag(np.abs(np.diag(self._noisy_cov))),
                size=n
            )

        # Inverse transform to original scale
        synthetic = self.scaler.inverse_transform(synthetic_scaled)

        # Privacy guarantee string
        guarantee = (
            f'({self.config.epsilon}, {self.config.delta})-differentially private. '
            f'Gaussian mechanism, sensitivity={self.config.clip_bound:.2f}, '
            f'noise_scale(sigma)={self._noise_scale:.4f}'
        )

        # Utility metrics: compare marginal statistics
        utility = {
            'mean_bias': float(np.abs(synthetic.mean(axis=0) - self.scaler.mean_).mean()),
            'std_ratio': float((synthetic.std(axis=0) / np.maximum(self.scaler.scale_, 1e-8)).mean()),
            'privacy_epsilon': self.config.epsilon,
            'n_synthetic': n
        }

        return SynthesisResult(
            synthetic_data=synthetic,
            feature_names=self._feature_names,
            epsilon_spent=self.config.epsilon,
            delta=self.config.delta,
            n_samples=n,
            privacy_guarantee=guarantee,
            utility_metrics=utility
        )

    def fit_sample(
        self,
        X: np.ndarray,
        feature_names: Optional[list[str]] = None
    ) -> SynthesisResult:
        return self.fit(X, feature_names).sample()
```

instruction: Write a Python script to implement a recursive DNS security monitor that detects DNS-based C2 and data exfiltration.
output:
```python
import re
import math
import struct
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Optional
from collections import Counter, defaultdict
from enum import Enum


class DNSThreatType(Enum):
    DATA_EXFILTRATION = 'dns_data_exfiltration'
    C2_BEACONING = 'dns_c2_beaconing'
    DGA_DOMAIN = 'dga_domain'
    FAST_FLUX = 'fast_flux'
    TUNNELING = 'dns_tunneling'
    TYPOSQUATTING = 'typosquatting'
    SUSPICIOUS_TLD = 'suspicious_tld'


@dataclass
class DNSQuery:
    query_id: str
    timestamp: datetime
    client_ip: str
    query_name: str       # FQDN being queried
    query_type: str       # 'A', 'AAAA', 'TXT', 'MX', 'CNAME', 'NULL'
    response_code: str    # 'NOERROR', 'NXDOMAIN', 'SERVFAIL'
    response_ips: list[str]
    response_ttl: Optional[int]
    query_size_bytes: int
    response_size_bytes: int


@dataclass
class DNSTheatDetection:
    threat_type: DNSThreatType
    severity: str
    query: DNSQuery
    confidence: float
    evidence: dict
    recommended_action: str


SUSPICIOUS_TLDS = {
    '.tk', '.ml', '.ga', '.cf', '.gq', '.pw', '.cc',
    '.top', '.xyz', '.info', '.biz', '.click'
}

LEGITIMATE_DOMAINS = {
    'google.com', 'microsoft.com', 'amazon.com', 'cloudflare.com',
    'akamai.com', 'fastly.com', 'apple.com', 'github.com'
}


def compute_domain_entropy(domain: str) -> float:
    """Shannon entropy of domain label characters (high = DGA or tunneling)."""
    label = domain.split('.')[0]  # Analyze leftmost label
    if not label:
        return 0.0
    counts = Counter(label.lower())
    n = len(label)
    return -sum((c/n) * math.log2(c/n) for c in counts.values())


def compute_consonant_ratio(label: str) -> float:
    """High consonant ratio indicates DGA domains."""
    consonants = set('bcdfghjklmnpqrstvwxyz')
    if not label:
        return 0.0
    return sum(1 for c in label.lower() if c in consonants) / len(label)


def is_base64_like(s: str) -> bool:
    """Detect base64-encoded data in DNS labels (exfiltration indicator)."""
    # Base64 chars without padding
    b64_chars = set('ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789+/=')
    if not s:
        return False
    ratio = sum(1 for c in s if c in b64_chars) / len(s)
    return ratio > 0.95 and len(s) > 16


def is_hex_encoded(s: str) -> bool:
    return bool(re.match(r'^[0-9a-fA-F]+$', s)) and len(s) > 12


class DNSSecurityMonitor:
    """
    DNS security monitor for detecting DNS-based attacks:
    - Tunneling: large TXT queries, encoded data in subdomains
    - Data exfiltration: base64/hex in DNS labels, many unique subdomains
    - C2 beaconing: regular queries to same domain at fixed intervals
    - DGA domains: high entropy, unusual consonant patterns
    - Fast flux: rapidly changing IP responses to same domain
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        exfil_label_length_threshold: int = 50,
        dga_entropy_threshold: float = 3.8,
        beacon_min_queries: int = 10
    ):
        self.exfil_threshold = exfil_label_length_threshold
        self.dga_threshold = dga_entropy_threshold
        self.beacon_min = beacon_min_queries
        self._query_history: dict[str, list[DNSQuery]] = defaultdict(list)
        self._domain_ips: dict[str, set[str]] = defaultdict(set)

    def _check_tunneling(
        self,
        query: DNSQuery
    ) -> Optional[DNSTheatDetection]:
        labels = query.query_name.rstrip('.').split('.')
        longest_label = max(labels, key=len) if labels else ''

        tunneling_score = 0.0
        evidence: dict = {}

        if len(longest_label) > self.exfil_threshold:
            tunneling_score += 4.0
            evidence['long_label_length'] = len(longest_label)

        if is_base64_like(longest_label):
            tunneling_score += 3.0
            evidence['base64_detected'] = True

        if is_hex_encoded(longest_label):
            tunneling_score += 3.0
            evidence['hex_encoded'] = True

        if query.query_type in ('TXT', 'NULL', 'MX'):
            tunneling_score += 2.0
            evidence['suspicious_record_type'] = query.query_type

        if query.response_size_bytes > 512:
            tunneling_score += 1.0
            evidence['large_response'] = query.response_size_bytes

        if tunneling_score >= 5.0:
            threat_type = DNSThreatType.DATA_EXFILTRATION if tunneling_score < 7 else DNSThreatType.TUNNELING
            return DNSTheatDetection(
                threat_type=threat_type,
                severity='CRITICAL' if tunneling_score >= 7 else 'HIGH',
                query=query,
                confidence=min(tunneling_score / 10.0, 1.0),
                evidence=evidence,
                recommended_action='Block domain and investigate source host for data exfiltration.'
            )
        return None

    def _check_dga(
        self,
        query: DNSQuery
    ) -> Optional[DNSTheatDetection]:
        labels = query.query_name.rstrip('.').split('.')
        if len(labels) < 2:
            return None
        main_label = labels[0]
        base_domain = '.'.join(labels[-2:])

        if base_domain in LEGITIMATE_DOMAINS:
            return None

        entropy = compute_domain_entropy(query.query_name)
        consonant_ratio = compute_consonant_ratio(main_label)
        label_len = len(main_label)

        dga_score = 0.0
        evidence: dict = {}

        if entropy >= self.dga_threshold:
            dga_score += 3.0
            evidence['entropy'] = round(entropy, 3)

        if consonant_ratio >= 0.7:
            dga_score += 2.0
            evidence['consonant_ratio'] = round(consonant_ratio, 3)

        if 8 <= label_len <= 20 and re.match(r'^[a-z0-9]+$', main_label):
            dga_score += 1.5
            evidence['random_looking_label'] = main_label

        if query.response_code == 'NXDOMAIN':
            dga_score += 1.0
            evidence['nxdomain'] = True

        tld = '.' + labels[-1]
        if tld in SUSPICIOUS_TLDS:
            dga_score += 2.0
            evidence['suspicious_tld'] = tld

        if dga_score >= 5.0:
            return DNSTheatDetection(
                threat_type=DNSThreatType.DGA_DOMAIN,
                severity='HIGH',
                query=query,
                confidence=min(dga_score / 8.0, 1.0),
                evidence=evidence,
                recommended_action='Block domain. Investigate host for malware infection (DGA-based C2).'
            )
        return None

    def _check_fast_flux(
        self,
        query: DNSQuery
    ) -> Optional[DNSTheatDetection]:
        domain = query.query_name.rstrip('.')
        if query.response_ips:
            self._domain_ips[domain].update(query.response_ips)

        n_ips = len(self._domain_ips[domain])
        if n_ips >= 10 and query.response_ttl is not None and query.response_ttl <= 300:
            return DNSTheatDetection(
                threat_type=DNSThreatType.FAST_FLUX,
                severity='HIGH',
                query=query,
                confidence=min(n_ips / 20.0, 1.0),
                evidence={'unique_ips': n_ips, 'ttl': query.response_ttl},
                recommended_action='Block domain: fast-flux infrastructure used by botnets.'
            )
        return None

    def inspect(
        self,
        query: DNSQuery
    ) -> list[DNSTheatDetection]:
        self._query_history[query.client_ip].append(query)
        threats = []

        for check in [self._check_tunneling, self._check_dga, self._check_fast_flux]:
            result = check(query)
            if result:
                threats.append(result)

        return threats

    def inspect_batch(
        self,
        queries: list[DNSQuery]
    ) -> list[DNSTheatDetection]:
        all_threats = []
        for q in queries:
            all_threats.extend(self.inspect(q))
        return sorted(all_threats, key=lambda t: t.confidence, reverse=True)
```

instruction: Write a Python script to implement a supply chain attack detector for Python packages using behavior analysis.
output:
```python
import ast
import re
import json
import hashlib
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional
from pathlib import Path
from enum import Enum


class SupplyChainRisk(Enum):
    CRITICAL = 'CRITICAL'   # Active exfiltration/execution
    HIGH = 'HIGH'           # Strong indicators
    MEDIUM = 'MEDIUM'       # Suspicious patterns
    LOW = 'LOW'             # Weak signals


@dataclass
class SupplyChainFinding:
    rule_id: str
    risk: SupplyChainRisk
    file: str
    line: int
    code_snippet: str
    description: str
    remediation: str
    cve_reference: Optional[str] = None


@dataclass
class PackageAnalysisResult:
    package_name: str
    package_version: str
    files_analyzed: int
    total_lines: int
    findings: list[SupplyChainFinding]
    risk_level: SupplyChainRisk
    is_suspicious: bool
    summary: str
    file_hashes: dict[str, str]  # filename -> SHA-256


# Known malicious patterns seen in real supply chain attacks
MALICIOUS_PATTERNS = [
    {
        'id': 'SC001', 'risk': SupplyChainRisk.CRITICAL,
        'pattern': re.compile(
            r'(?:subprocess\.(?:run|Popen|call)|os\.(?:system|popen)).*(?:curl|wget|bash|sh)',
            re.DOTALL
        ),
        'description': 'Downloads and executes code at install time',
        'remediation': 'Remove package immediately; scan for persistence mechanisms.'
    },
    {
        'id': 'SC002', 'risk': SupplyChainRisk.CRITICAL,
        'pattern': re.compile(
            r'(?:requests\.(?:get|post)|urllib\.request\.urlopen).*(?:env|credential|token|key|secret)',
            re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL
        ),
        'description': 'Possible exfiltration of credentials/environment variables',
        'remediation': 'Revoke all secrets; audit network logs for outbound connections.'
    },
    {
        'id': 'SC003', 'risk': SupplyChainRisk.CRITICAL,
        'pattern': re.compile(r'os\.environ.*(?:post|get|send|upload)', re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL),
        'description': 'Reading environment variables (potential credential theft)',
        'remediation': 'Audit what env vars are read and where they are sent.'
    },
    {
        'id': 'SC004', 'risk': SupplyChainRisk.HIGH,
        'pattern': re.compile(r'(?:exec|eval)\s*\(.*(?:base64|decode|b64)', re.DOTALL),
        'description': 'Executes base64-decoded code (obfuscation technique)',
        'remediation': 'Decode and analyze the obfuscated payload.'
    },
    {
        'id': 'SC005', 'risk': SupplyChainRisk.HIGH,
        'pattern': re.compile(r'(?:setup\.py|setup\(.*install_requires).*(?:__import__|importlib)', re.DOTALL),
        'description': 'Dynamic imports in setup.py (install-time execution)',
        'remediation': 'Review setup.py for malicious install hooks.'
    },
    {
        'id': 'SC006', 'risk': SupplyChainRisk.HIGH,
        'pattern': re.compile(r'socket\.(?:connect|sendall|recv)'),
        'description': 'Raw socket connections — potential reverse shell or C2',
        'remediation': 'Trace all socket usage and destinations.'
    },
    {
        'id': 'SC007', 'risk': SupplyChainRisk.MEDIUM,
        'pattern': re.compile(
            r'(?:subprocess|os\.system).*(?:/etc/passwd|shadow|SAM|NTDS)',
            re.IGNORECASE
        ),
        'description': 'Reads sensitive system files',
        'remediation': 'Isolate system; check for credential dump artifacts.'
    },
    {
        'id': 'SC008', 'risk': SupplyChainRisk.MEDIUM,
        'pattern': re.compile(r'atexit\.register|__del__\s*='),
        'description': 'Registers cleanup hooks (may persist malicious code on exit)',
        'remediation': 'Audit atexit and __del__ callbacks for malicious operations.'
    },
    {
        'id': 'SC009', 'risk': SupplyChainRisk.MEDIUM,
        'pattern': re.compile(r'ctypes\.(?:windll|CDLL|cdll)'),
        'description': 'Loads native DLLs via ctypes (privilege escalation risk)',
        'remediation': 'Audit which DLLs are loaded and what functions are called.'
    },
    {
        'id': 'SC010', 'risk': SupplyChainRisk.LOW,
        'pattern': re.compile(r'#.*(?:backdoor|malware|pwn|exploit|payload)', re.IGNORECASE),
        'description': 'Suspicious comment keywords',
        'remediation': 'Manual review of surrounding code.'
    },
]


class SupplyChainAttackDetector:
    """
    Detects supply chain attacks in Python packages by statically analyzing
    package source code for malicious patterns.
    Covers known attack techniques from SolarWinds-style, PyPI malware,
    and dependency confusion attacks.
    """

    def analyze_file(
        self,
        file_path: str,
        source_code: str
    ) -> list[SupplyChainFinding]:
        findings = []
        lines = source_code.split('\n')

        for rule in MALICIOUS_PATTERNS:
            for i, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
                if rule['pattern'].search(line):
                    findings.append(SupplyChainFinding(
                        rule_id=rule['id'],
                        risk=rule['risk'],
                        file=file_path,
                        line=i,
                        code_snippet=line.strip()[:200],
                        description=rule['description'],
                        remediation=rule['remediation']
                    ))

        # AST-based checks: detect install-time execution in setup.py
        if 'setup.py' in file_path:
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(source_code)
                for node in ast.walk(tree):
                    if isinstance(node, ast.Call):
                        func_name = getattr(node.func, 'attr', None) or getattr(node.func, 'id', None)
                        if func_name in ('system', 'popen', 'run', 'Popen'):
                            findings.append(SupplyChainFinding(
                                rule_id='SC011',
                                risk=SupplyChainRisk.HIGH,
                                file=file_path,
                                line=node.lineno,
                                code_snippet=ast.unparse(node)[:200] if hasattr(ast, 'unparse') else str(node),
                                description='setup.py executes shell commands (install-time RCE risk)',
                                remediation='Review shell commands in setup.py; prefer pure Python installation.'
                            ))
            except SyntaxError:
                pass

        return findings

    def analyze_package(
        self,
        package_name: str,
        package_version: str,
        source_files: dict[str, str]  # filename -> source code
    ) -> PackageAnalysisResult:
        all_findings: list[SupplyChainFinding] = []
        file_hashes = {}
        total_lines = 0

        for filename, source in source_files.items():
            if not filename.endswith('.py'):
                continue
            file_hashes[filename] = hashlib.sha256(source.encode()).hexdigest()
            total_lines += len(source.split('\n'))
            findings = self.analyze_file(filename, source)
            all_findings.extend(findings)

        risk_order = {
            SupplyChainRisk.CRITICAL: 4, SupplyChainRisk.HIGH: 3,
            SupplyChainRisk.MEDIUM: 2, SupplyChainRisk.LOW: 1
        }
        if all_findings:
            overall_risk = max(all_findings, key=lambda f: risk_order[f.risk]).risk
        else:
            overall_risk = SupplyChainRisk.LOW

        is_suspicious = overall_risk in (SupplyChainRisk.CRITICAL, SupplyChainRisk.HIGH)
        by_risk = {r: sum(1 for f in all_findings if f.risk == r) for r in SupplyChainRisk}
        summary = (
            f'{package_name}@{package_version}: {len(all_findings)} findings '
            f'(CRITICAL={by_risk[SupplyChainRisk.CRITICAL]}, '
            f'HIGH={by_risk[SupplyChainRisk.HIGH]}, '
            f'MEDIUM={by_risk[SupplyChainRisk.MEDIUM]}). '
            f'Risk: {overall_risk.value}.'
        )

        return PackageAnalysisResult(
            package_name=package_name,
            package_version=package_version,
            files_analyzed=len(source_files),
            total_lines=total_lines,
            findings=all_findings,
            risk_level=overall_risk,
            is_suspicious=is_suspicious,
            summary=summary,
            file_hashes=file_hashes
        )
```

instruction: Write a Python implementation of an adversarial example detector for computer vision security models.
output:
```python
import numpy as np
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Optional, Callable
from sklearn.neighbors import LocalOutlierFactor
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA
from sklearn.preprocessing import normalize


@dataclass
class AdversarialDetectionResult:
    is_adversarial: bool
    adversarial_probability: float
    detection_method: str
    confidence: float
    perturbation_magnitude: Optional[float]
    features_flagged: list[str]
    explanation: str


class AdversarialExampleDetector:
    """
    Multi-method adversarial example detector for image classifiers.
    Combines:
    1. Input feature statistics (pixel value distributions)
    2. Feature squeezing (compare model output on original vs smoothed input)
    3. Kernel density estimation in latent space
    4. Local Outlier Factor on extracted features
    5. Perturbation magnitude estimation

    Detects attacks: FGSM, PGD, C&W, Deepfool, and patch attacks.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        n_pca_components: int = 64,
        lof_neighbors: int = 20,
        squeezing_threshold: float = 0.1
    ):
        self.n_pca_components = n_pca_components
        self.squeezing_threshold = squeezing_threshold
        self.pca = PCA(n_components=n_pca_components)
        self.lof = LocalOutlierFactor(n_neighbors=lof_neighbors, novelty=True)
        self._fitted = False
        self._normal_feature_stats: dict = {}

    def _extract_statistical_features(self, x: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
        """Extract statistical features from pixel values."""
        flat = x.flatten().astype(np.float32)
        if len(flat) == 0:
            return np.zeros(20)
        return np.array([
            float(flat.mean()),
            float(flat.std()),
            float(np.percentile(flat, 5)),
            float(np.percentile(flat, 25)),
            float(np.percentile(flat, 50)),
            float(np.percentile(flat, 75)),
            float(np.percentile(flat, 95)),
            float(flat.max() - flat.min()),     # Range
            float(np.sum(np.abs(np.diff(flat.reshape(-1, max(int(np.sqrt(len(flat))), 1)))).mean())),  # TV norm approx
            float(np.sum(flat == 0) / max(len(flat), 1)),   # Zero pixel ratio
            float(np.sum(flat == 1.0) / max(len(flat), 1)), # Saturated pixel ratio
            float(np.var(flat)),
            float(np.abs(flat - flat.mean()).mean()),   # Mean absolute deviation
            float(np.sum(flat > flat.mean() + 2*flat.std()) / max(len(flat), 1)),  # Outlier ratio
            float(np.median(np.abs(flat - np.median(flat)))),  # MAD
        ], dtype=np.float32)

    def _squeeze_filter(
        self,
        x: np.ndarray,
        bits: int = 4
    ) -> np.ndarray:
        """Bit-depth reduction as input squeeze (reduces adversarial perturbations)."""
        step = 1.0 / (2**bits - 1)
        return np.round(x / step) * step

    def _median_filter(
        self,
        x: np.ndarray,
        kernel_size: int = 2
    ) -> np.ndarray:
        """Simple median smoothing."""
        if x.ndim < 2:
            return x
        from scipy.ndimage import median_filter
        try:
            return median_filter(x, size=kernel_size)
        except ImportError:
            # Fallback without scipy
            return x

    def _estimate_perturbation(
        self,
        x: np.ndarray,
        x_clean_estimate: np.ndarray
    ) -> float:
        """Estimate perturbation magnitude (L-inf norm)."""
        return float(np.abs(x - x_clean_estimate).max())

    def fit(
        self,
        clean_images: np.ndarray  # Shape: (n, h, w, c) or (n, features)
    ) -> 'AdversarialExampleDetector':
        # Extract features from clean images
        features = np.vstack([self._extract_statistical_features(img) for img in clean_images])
        # Normalize
        features_norm = normalize(features)

        # PCA for dimensionality reduction
        n_components = min(self.n_pca_components, features.shape[1], len(clean_images) - 1)
        self.pca.set_params(n_components=n_components)
        pca_features = self.pca.fit_transform(features_norm)

        self.lof.fit(pca_features)

        self._normal_feature_stats = {
            'mean': features.mean(axis=0),
            'std': features.std(axis=0) + 1e-8,
            'min': features.min(axis=0),
            'max': features.max(axis=0),
        }
        self._fitted = True
        return self

    def detect(
        self,
        x: np.ndarray,
        model_fn: Optional[Callable] = None
    ) -> AdversarialDetectionResult:
        stats_features = self._extract_statistical_features(x)
        flags = []
        score = 0.0

        # Check 1: Statistical features vs training distribution
        if self._fitted:
            z_scores = np.abs(
                (stats_features - self._normal_feature_stats['mean']) /
                self._normal_feature_stats['std']
            )
            outlier_features = np.sum(z_scores > 3.0)
            if outlier_features > 3:
                score += 3.0
                flags.append(f'{outlier_features} features with z-score > 3 (statistical outlier)')

        # Check 2: Feature squeezing
        if model_fn is not None:
            squeezed = self._squeeze_filter(x)
            smoothed = self._median_filter(x)
            x_batch = x.reshape(1, *x.shape)
            sq_batch = squeezed.reshape(1, *squeezed.shape)
            sm_batch = smoothed.reshape(1, *smoothed.shape)
            try:
                orig_pred = model_fn(x_batch)
                sq_pred = model_fn(sq_batch)
                sm_pred = model_fn(sm_batch)
                sq_diff = float(np.abs(orig_pred - sq_pred).max())
                sm_diff = float(np.abs(orig_pred - sm_pred).max())
                if sq_diff > self.squeezing_threshold:
                    score += 4.0
                    flags.append(f'Squeezing diff={sq_diff:.3f} exceeds threshold')
                if sm_diff > self.squeezing_threshold:
                    score += 3.0
                    flags.append(f'Smoothing diff={sm_diff:.3f} exceeds threshold')
            except Exception:
                pass

        # Check 3: LOF in PCA space
        if self._fitted:
            norm_feat = normalize(stats_features.reshape(1, -1))
            n_components = self.pca.n_components_
            if norm_feat.shape[1] >= n_components:
                pca_feat = self.pca.transform(norm_feat)
                lof_score = float(self.lof.score_samples(pca_feat)[0])
                if lof_score < -1.5:
                    score += abs(lof_score + 1.0) * 2
                    flags.append(f'LOF anomaly score: {lof_score:.3f}')

        # Check 4: Pixel value statistics
        x_flat = x.flatten().astype(np.float32)
        if x_flat.std() < 0.01:  # Near-uniform image
            score += 2.0
            flags.append('Very low pixel variance (possible adversarial noise)')
        if (x_flat > 1.0).any() or (x_flat < 0.0).any():
            score += 3.0
            flags.append('Pixel values out of [0,1] range')

        # Perturbation estimate via squeeze diff
        squeezed = self._squeeze_filter(x)
        pert_magnitude = self._estimate_perturbation(x, squeezed)
        if pert_magnitude > 0.1:
            score += 2.0
            flags.append(f'High perturbation magnitude vs squeezed: {pert_magnitude:.4f}')

        adv_probability = min(score / 10.0, 1.0)
        is_adversarial = adv_probability >= 0.4

        explanation = (
            f'Adversarial probability: {adv_probability:.1%}. '
            + (f'Detected indicators: {flags[:3]}.' if flags else 'No adversarial indicators detected.')
        )

        return AdversarialDetectionResult(
            is_adversarial=is_adversarial,
            adversarial_probability=round(adv_probability, 4),
            detection_method='statistical+squeezing+lof',
            confidence=min(len(flags) * 0.25, 1.0),
            perturbation_magnitude=round(pert_magnitude, 5),
            features_flagged=flags,
            explanation=explanation
        )
```

instruction: Write a Python function to implement a model inversion attack simulator to assess privacy risks in ML models.
output:
```python
import numpy as np
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Optional, Callable
from sklearn.datasets import make_classification
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
from sklearn.metrics import mean_squared_error


@dataclass
class InversionAttackResult:
    target_class: int
    n_iterations: int
    reconstructed_sample: np.ndarray
    reconstruction_confidence: float
    final_prediction_confidence: float
    feature_leakage_scores: list[float]
    privacy_risk_level: str
    explanation: str


@dataclass
class PrivacyRiskAssessment:
    model_type: str
    n_classes: int
    n_features: int
    attack_success_rate: float
    avg_reconstruction_confidence: float
    feature_leakage_summary: dict
    privacy_risk_level: str
    mitigations: list[str]


class ModelInversionSimulator:
    """
    Simulates model inversion attacks to assess privacy risks.
    Model inversion: given black-box access to a model's predictions,
    reconstruct approximate training samples for a target class.

    Method (gradient-free, for black-box models):
    - Initialize with random sample
    - Iteratively perturb features to maximize confidence for target class
    - Use hill-climbing / coordinate ascent optimization

    Use this to:
    - Quantify how much a model leaks about training data
    - Test before deploying models on sensitive data
    - Justify adding DP-SGD or output perturbation defenses
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        predict_proba_fn: Callable,
        n_features: int,
        feature_ranges: Optional[list[tuple[float, float]]] = None,
        n_iterations: int = 500,
        step_size: float = 0.05,
        patience: int = 50
    ):
        self.predict = predict_proba_fn
        self.n_features = n_features
        self.ranges = feature_ranges or [(0.0, 1.0)] * n_features
        self.n_iterations = n_iterations
        self.step_size = step_size
        self.patience = patience

    def _clip(
        self,
        x: np.ndarray
    ) -> np.ndarray:
        clipped = x.copy()
        for i, (lo, hi) in enumerate(self.ranges):
            clipped[i] = np.clip(clipped[i], lo, hi)
        return clipped

    def attack_class(
        self,
        target_class: int,
        seed: Optional[int] = None
    ) -> InversionAttackResult:
        rng = np.random.default_rng(seed or 42)

        # Initialize with feature midpoints + noise
        midpoints = np.array([(lo + hi) / 2 for lo, hi in self.ranges])
        x = midpoints + rng.normal(0, 0.1, self.n_features)
        x = self._clip(x)

        best_x = x.copy()
        best_conf = 0.0
        no_improve = 0

        for i in range(self.n_iterations):
            proba = self.predict(x.reshape(1, -1))[0]
            if target_class >= len(proba):
                break
            current_conf = float(proba[target_class])

            if current_conf > best_conf:
                best_conf = current_conf
                best_x = x.copy()
                no_improve = 0
            else:
                no_improve += 1

            if no_improve >= self.patience:
                break

            # Coordinate ascent: perturb one feature at a time
            feature_idx = i % self.n_features
            for sign in [+1, -1]:
                x_trial = x.copy()
                x_trial[feature_idx] += sign * self.step_size * (self.ranges[feature_idx][1] - self.ranges[feature_idx][0])
                x_trial = self._clip(x_trial)
                trial_conf = float(self.predict(x_trial.reshape(1, -1))[0][target_class])
                if trial_conf > current_conf:
                    x = x_trial
                    current_conf = trial_conf
                    break

            # Also try random perturbation every 10 steps
            if i % 10 == 0:
                x_rand = best_x + rng.normal(0, self.step_size * 0.5, self.n_features)
                x_rand = self._clip(x_rand)
                rand_conf = float(self.predict(x_rand.reshape(1, -1))[0][target_class])
                if rand_conf > best_conf:
                    x = x_rand

        # Feature leakage scores: how far each feature moved from midpoint
        leakage = np.abs(best_x - midpoints) / np.array(
            [max(hi - lo, 1e-8) for lo, hi in self.ranges]
        )

        privacy_risk = (
            'HIGH' if best_conf >= 0.8 else
            'MEDIUM' if best_conf >= 0.6 else
            'LOW'
        )

        return InversionAttackResult(
            target_class=target_class,
            n_iterations=self.n_iterations,
            reconstructed_sample=best_x,
            reconstruction_confidence=round(best_conf, 4),
            final_prediction_confidence=round(best_conf, 4),
            feature_leakage_scores=leakage.tolist(),
            privacy_risk_level=privacy_risk,
            explanation=(
                f'Model inversion achieved {best_conf:.1%} confidence for class {target_class}. '
                f'Features most leaked (normalized movement): '
                f'{sorted(enumerate(leakage), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)[:3]}.'
            )
        )

    def assess_privacy(
        self,
        n_classes: int,
        model_type: str = 'unknown'
    ) -> PrivacyRiskAssessment:
        results = [self.attack_class(c) for c in range(n_classes)]
        success_rate = sum(1 for r in results if r.reconstruction_confidence >= 0.7) / max(n_classes, 1)
        avg_conf = sum(r.reconstruction_confidence for r in results) / max(len(results), 1)

        # Feature leakage analysis
        all_leakage = np.array([r.feature_leakage_scores for r in results]).mean(axis=0)
        top_leaked = sorted(
            enumerate(all_leakage), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True
        )[:5]

        risk = 'HIGH' if success_rate >= 0.7 else 'MEDIUM' if success_rate >= 0.3 else 'LOW'

        mitigations = [
            'Apply differential privacy (DP-SGD) during training',
            'Add output perturbation (add noise to softmax outputs)',
            'Limit prediction API to top-k classes (reduce information leakage)',
            'Implement rate limiting on prediction API',
        ]
        if risk == 'HIGH':
            mitigations.insert(0, 'Consider not deploying this model on sensitive data without DP training')

        return PrivacyRiskAssessment(
            model_type=model_type,
            n_classes=n_classes,
            n_features=self.n_features,
            attack_success_rate=round(success_rate, 4),
            avg_reconstruction_confidence=round(avg_conf, 4),
            feature_leakage_summary={
                'top_leaked_features': top_leaked,
                'per_class_results': [
                    {'class': r.target_class, 'confidence': r.reconstruction_confidence}
                    for r in results
                ]
            },
            privacy_risk_level=risk,
            mitigations=mitigations
        )
```

instruction: Write a Python script to implement a ML-based user behavior analytics (UBA) system for detecting account takeover attacks.
output:
```python
import numpy as np
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from typing import Optional
from collections import defaultdict, Counter
from sklearn.ensemble import IsolationForest
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler


@dataclass
class UserAction:
    action_id: str
    user_id: str
    timestamp: datetime
    action_type: str    # 'login', 'password_change', 'mfa_disable', 'data_export', 'settings_change'
    source_ip: str
    country: str
    user_agent: str
    resource: Optional[str]
    success: bool
    risk_score_override: Optional[float] = None


@dataclass
class ATOAlert:
    user_id: str
    alert_type: str
    severity: str
    confidence: float
    evidence: list[str]
    timestamp: str
    recommended_action: str
    session_actions: list[str]


class UserBehaviorProfile:
    """Maintains per-user behavioral baseline."""

    def __init__(self, user_id: str):
        self.user_id = user_id
        self.typical_countries: Counter = Counter()
        self.typical_hours: Counter = Counter()
        self.typical_user_agents: Counter = Counter()
        self.typical_ips: Counter = Counter()
        self.avg_actions_per_day: float = 0.0
        self.total_observations: int = 0

    def update(self, action: UserAction):
        self.typical_countries[action.country] += 1
        self.typical_hours[action.timestamp.hour] += 1
        self.typical_user_agents[action.user_agent[:50]] += 1
        self.typical_ips[action.source_ip] += 1
        self.total_observations += 1

    def is_known_country(self, country: str) -> bool:
        return self.typical_countries.get(country, 0) > 0

    def is_typical_hour(self, hour: int) -> bool:
        return self.typical_hours.get(hour, 0) > 0

    def is_known_ip(self, ip: str) -> bool:
        return self.typical_ips.get(ip, 0) > 0


# Sensitive actions that indicate account takeover when done in sequence
SENSITIVE_ACTION_SEQUENCES = [
    ['login', 'password_change'],
    ['login', 'mfa_disable'],
    ['login', 'mfa_disable', 'data_export'],
    ['login', 'settings_change', 'data_export'],
]

HIGH_RISK_ACTIONS = {'mfa_disable', 'password_change', 'data_export', 'api_key_create', 'admin_grant'}


def action_to_features(
    action: UserAction,
    profile: UserBehaviorProfile,
    recent_failed_logins: int,
    actions_last_hour: int
) -> np.ndarray:
    hour = action.timestamp.hour
    return np.array([
        hour,
        action.timestamp.weekday(),
        int(hour < 6 or hour > 22),      # Off-hours
        int(not profile.is_known_country(action.country)),
        int(not profile.is_typical_hour(hour)),
        int(not profile.is_known_ip(action.source_ip)),
        int(action.action_type in HIGH_RISK_ACTIONS),
        int(not action.success),
        recent_failed_logins,
        actions_last_hour,
        int(action.action_type == 'mfa_disable'),
        int(action.action_type == 'password_change'),
        int(action.action_type == 'data_export'),
        int(action.action_type == 'login'),
        len(action.user_agent),
        int(any(bot in action.user_agent.lower() for bot in ['python', 'curl', 'requests', 'go-http'])),
    ], dtype=np.float32)


class AccountTakeoverDetector:
    """
    ML-based Account Takeover (ATO) detection using user behavioral analytics.
    Combines:
    - Per-user baseline profiling (known IPs, countries, active hours)
    - Isolation Forest anomaly scoring
    - Rule-based sensitive action sequence detection
    - Session risk accumulation (multiple medium signals = high alert)
    """

    def __init__(self, contamination: float = 0.05):
        self.contamination = contamination
        self.scaler = StandardScaler()
        self.iso_forest = IsolationForest(
            n_estimators=100, contamination=contamination, random_state=42
        )
        self._profiles: dict[str, UserBehaviorProfile] = {}
        self._action_history: dict[str, list[UserAction]] = defaultdict(list)
        self._alerts: list[ATOAlert] = []
        self._fitted = False

    def build_profiles(self, historical_actions: list[UserAction]):
        for action in historical_actions:
            if action.user_id not in self._profiles:
                self._profiles[action.user_id] = UserBehaviorProfile(action.user_id)
            self._profiles[action.user_id].update(action)

    def fit(self, historical_actions: list[UserAction]) -> dict:
        self.build_profiles(historical_actions)
        features = []
        for action in historical_actions:
            profile = self._profiles.get(action.user_id, UserBehaviorProfile(action.user_id))
            features.append(action_to_features(action, profile, 0, 0))
        X = np.vstack(features)
        X_scaled = self.scaler.fit_transform(X)
        self.iso_forest.fit(X_scaled)
        self._fitted = True
        return {'trained_on': len(historical_actions), 'n_users': len(self._profiles)}

    def _get_recent_context(
        self,
        user_id: str,
        window_minutes: int = 60
    ) -> tuple[int, int]:
        cutoff = datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(minutes=window_minutes)
        recent = [a for a in self._action_history[user_id] if a.timestamp >= cutoff]
        failed = sum(1 for a in recent if not a.success and a.action_type == 'login')
        return failed, len(recent)

    def _check_sensitive_sequences(
        self,
        user_id: str,
        new_action: UserAction
    ) -> Optional[ATOAlert]:
        recent = sorted(self._action_history[user_id][-10:], key=lambda a: a.timestamp)
        action_types = [a.action_type for a in recent] + [new_action.action_type]

        for seq in SENSITIVE_ACTION_SEQUENCES:
            if len(action_types) >= len(seq):
                tail = action_types[-len(seq):]
                if tail == seq:
                    return ATOAlert(
                        user_id=user_id,
                        alert_type='SENSITIVE_ACTION_SEQUENCE',
                        severity='CRITICAL',
                        confidence=0.9,
                        evidence=[f'Detected sequence: {" -> ".join(seq)}'],
                        timestamp=datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
                        recommended_action='Force re-authentication; freeze account; notify user.',
                        session_actions=[a.action_type for a in recent[-5:]]
                    )
        return None

    def analyze_action(
        self,
        action: UserAction
    ) -> Optional[ATOAlert]:
        self._action_history[action.user_id].append(action)

        profile = self._profiles.get(action.user_id, UserBehaviorProfile(action.user_id))
        failed_logins, actions_last_hour = self._get_recent_context(action.user_id)
        features = action_to_features(action, profile, failed_logins, actions_last_hour)

        # ML anomaly score
        if self._fitted:
            x = self.scaler.transform(features.reshape(1, -1))
            ml_score = float(self.iso_forest.decision_function(x)[0])
            anomaly_prob = max(0.0, min(1.0, (0.1 - ml_score) / 0.3))
        else:
            anomaly_prob = 0.0

        # Rule-based checks
        evidence = []
        rule_score = 0.0

        if not profile.is_known_country(action.country):
            evidence.append(f'New country: {action.country}')
            rule_score += 0.3

        if failed_logins >= 3:
            evidence.append(f'{failed_logins} failed logins in last hour')
            rule_score += 0.4

        if action.action_type in HIGH_RISK_ACTIONS:
            evidence.append(f'High-risk action: {action.action_type}')
            rule_score += 0.3

        if action.timestamp.hour < 5 or action.timestamp.hour > 23:
            evidence.append('Action at unusual hour')
            rule_score += 0.2

        # Sensitive sequence check
        seq_alert = self._check_sensitive_sequences(action.user_id, action)
        if seq_alert:
            self._alerts.append(seq_alert)
            return seq_alert

        combined = min((anomaly_prob + rule_score) / 1.5, 1.0)

        if combined >= 0.6 and evidence:
            severity = 'HIGH' if combined >= 0.8 else 'MEDIUM'
            alert = ATOAlert(
                user_id=action.user_id,
                alert_type='BEHAVIORAL_ANOMALY',
                severity=severity,
                confidence=round(combined, 4),
                evidence=evidence,
                timestamp=datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
                recommended_action='Review session; consider step-up authentication.',
                session_actions=[a.action_type for a in self._action_history[action.user_id][-5:]]
            )
            self._alerts.append(alert)
            return alert

        # Update profile with normal action
        profile.update(action)
        return None
```

instruction: Write a Python script to implement a secure configuration drift detector for cloud infrastructure.
output:
```python
import json
import hashlib
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Optional, Any
from enum import Enum


class DriftSeverity(Enum):
    CRITICAL = 'CRITICAL'
    HIGH = 'HIGH'
    MEDIUM = 'MEDIUM'
    LOW = 'LOW'


@dataclass
class ConfigSnapshot:
    resource_id: str
    resource_type: str
    snapshot_time: str
    config_hash: str
    config: dict
    tags: dict
    baseline_id: Optional[str] = None


@dataclass
class DriftFinding:
    resource_id: str
    resource_type: str
    severity: DriftSeverity
    drift_type: str   # 'unauthorized_change', 'security_weakening', 'policy_violation'
    path: str         # JSON path to changed field
    expected: Any
    actual: Any
    description: str
    remediation: str
    detected_at: str


@dataclass
class DriftReport:
    scan_id: str
    scanned_at: str
    total_resources: int
    drifted_resources: int
    findings: list[DriftFinding]
    by_severity: dict[str, int]
    critical_resources: list[str]
    is_compliant: bool


# Security-critical field paths and their expected values / validators
SECURITY_CRITICAL_CONFIGS = {
    'aws_s3_bucket': [
        {
            'path': 'public_access_block.block_public_acls',
            'expected': True,
            'severity': DriftSeverity.CRITICAL,
            'description': 'S3 bucket public ACLs not blocked',
            'remediation': 'Enable BlockPublicAcls on S3 bucket immediately.'
        },
        {
            'path': 'versioning.status',
            'expected': 'Enabled',
            'severity': DriftSeverity.HIGH,
            'description': 'S3 bucket versioning disabled',
            'remediation': 'Enable versioning for data recovery and audit trail.'
        },
        {
            'path': 'encryption.sse_algorithm',
            'expected_in': ['AES256', 'aws:kms'],
            'severity': DriftSeverity.CRITICAL,
            'description': 'S3 bucket encryption disabled or weakened',
            'remediation': 'Enable server-side encryption with AES256 or KMS.'
        },
    ],
    'aws_security_group': [
        {
            'path': 'ingress_rules',
            'validator': lambda v: not any(
                r.get('cidr') == '0.0.0.0/0' and r.get('port') in [22, 3389]
                for r in (v if isinstance(v, list) else [])
            ),
            'severity': DriftSeverity.CRITICAL,
            'description': 'SSH/RDP open to the internet (0.0.0.0/0)',
            'remediation': 'Restrict SSH/RDP to known IP ranges only.'
        }
    ],
    'aws_iam_policy': [
        {
            'path': 'statements',
            'validator': lambda v: not any(
                s.get('effect') == 'Allow' and s.get('actions') == ['*'] and s.get('resources') == ['*']
                for s in (v if isinstance(v, list) else [])
            ),
            'severity': DriftSeverity.CRITICAL,
            'description': 'IAM policy allows all actions on all resources (wildcard policy)',
            'remediation': 'Replace wildcard policy with least-privilege permissions.'
        }
    ],
}


def get_nested(
    config: dict,
    path: str
) -> tuple[bool, Any]:
    """Get nested value from dict using dot-notation path."""
    keys = path.split('.')
    current = config
    for key in keys:
        if not isinstance(current, dict) or key not in current:
            return False, None
        current = current[key]
    return True, current


def flatten_diff(
    baseline: dict,
    current: dict,
    path: str = ''
) -> list[tuple[str, Any, Any]]:
    """Recursively find all differences between two configs."""
    diffs = []
    all_keys = set(baseline.keys()) | set(current.keys())

    for key in all_keys:
        full_path = f'{path}.{key}' if path else key
        if key not in baseline:
            diffs.append((full_path, None, current[key]))
        elif key not in current:
            diffs.append((full_path, baseline[key], None))
        elif isinstance(baseline[key], dict) and isinstance(current[key], dict):
            diffs.extend(flatten_diff(baseline[key], current[key], full_path))
        elif baseline[key] != current[key]:
            diffs.append((full_path, baseline[key], current[key]))

    return diffs


class ConfigDriftDetector:
    """
    Detects security configuration drift in cloud infrastructure.
    Maintains baseline snapshots and compares against current state.
    Prioritizes security-critical config changes (open S3 buckets,
    wildcard IAM policies, internet-exposed SSH/RDP).
    """

    def __init__(self):
        self._baselines: dict[str, ConfigSnapshot] = {}
        self._change_log: list[dict] = []

    def set_baseline(
        self,
        resource_id: str,
        resource_type: str,
        config: dict,
        tags: Optional[dict] = None
    ) -> ConfigSnapshot:
        config_hash = hashlib.sha256(
            json.dumps(config, sort_keys=True, default=str).encode()
        ).hexdigest()
        snapshot = ConfigSnapshot(
            resource_id=resource_id,
            resource_type=resource_type,
            snapshot_time=datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            config_hash=config_hash,
            config=config,
            tags=tags or {}
        )
        self._baselines[resource_id] = snapshot
        return snapshot

    def check_security_rules(
        self,
        resource_id: str,
        resource_type: str,
        current_config: dict
    ) -> list[DriftFinding]:
        findings = []
        rules = SECURITY_CRITICAL_CONFIGS.get(resource_type, [])

        for rule in rules:
            found, value = get_nested(current_config, rule['path'])
            if not found:
                continue

            violated = False
            if 'expected' in rule:
                violated = value != rule['expected']
            elif 'expected_in' in rule:
                violated = value not in rule['expected_in']
            elif 'validator' in rule:
                try:
                    violated = not rule['validator'](value)
                except Exception:
                    violated = True

            if violated:
                baseline = self._baselines.get(resource_id)
                _, expected_value = get_nested(
                    baseline.config if baseline else {},
                    rule['path']
                )
                findings.append(DriftFinding(
                    resource_id=resource_id,
                    resource_type=resource_type,
                    severity=rule['severity'],
                    drift_type='security_weakening',
                    path=rule['path'],
                    expected=rule.get('expected', 'policy_compliant'),
                    actual=value,
                    description=rule['description'],
                    remediation=rule['remediation'],
                    detected_at=datetime.utcnow().isoformat()
                ))
        return findings

    def scan_resource(
        self,
        resource_id: str,
        resource_type: str,
        current_config: dict
    ) -> list[DriftFinding]:
        findings = []
        baseline = self._baselines.get(resource_id)

        # Security rule violations (even without a baseline)
        findings.extend(self.check_security_rules(resource_id, resource_type, current_config))

        # Config drift from baseline
        if baseline:
            current_hash = hashlib.sha256(
                json.dumps(current_config, sort_keys=True, default=str).encode()
            ).hexdigest()
            if current_hash != baseline.config_hash:
                diffs = flatten_diff(baseline.config, current_config)
                for path, old_val, new_val in diffs:
                    # Avoid duplicate findings with security rules
                    if any(f.path == path for f in findings):
                        continue
                    severity = DriftSeverity.MEDIUM
                    if any(kw in path.lower() for kw in ['public', 'encrypt', 'acl', 'permission', 'policy']):
                        severity = DriftSeverity.HIGH
                    findings.append(DriftFinding(
                        resource_id=resource_id,
                        resource_type=resource_type,
                        severity=severity,
                        drift_type='unauthorized_change',
                        path=path,
                        expected=old_val,
                        actual=new_val,
                        description=f'Config changed at {path}: {old_val!r} -> {new_val!r}',
                        remediation='Review change; revert if unauthorized.',
                        detected_at=datetime.utcnow().isoformat()
                    ))
        return findings

    def scan_all(
        self,
        current_snapshots: list[tuple[str, str, dict]]  # (resource_id, type, config)
    ) -> DriftReport:
        all_findings = []
        drifted = set()

        for resource_id, resource_type, config in current_snapshots:
            findings = self.scan_resource(resource_id, resource_type, config)
            if findings:
                drifted.add(resource_id)
            all_findings.extend(findings)

        by_severity = {s.value: 0 for s in DriftSeverity}
        for f in all_findings:
            by_severity[f.severity.value] += 1

        critical_resources = list({f.resource_id for f in all_findings if f.severity == DriftSeverity.CRITICAL})
        is_compliant = by_severity['CRITICAL'] == 0 and by_severity['HIGH'] == 0

        return DriftReport(
            scan_id=hashlib.md5(datetime.utcnow().isoformat().encode()).hexdigest()[:8],
            scanned_at=datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            total_resources=len(current_snapshots),
            drifted_resources=len(drifted),
            findings=all_findings,
            by_severity=by_severity,
            critical_resources=critical_resources,
            is_compliant=is_compliant
        )
```

instruction: Write a Python implementation of an explainable AI fairness auditor for detecting bias in security screening models.
output:
```python
import numpy as np
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional
from sklearn.metrics import confusion_matrix


@dataclass
class FairnessMetrics:
    group_name: str
    n_samples: int
    positive_rate: float        # P(Y_hat=1)
    true_positive_rate: float   # Recall / Sensitivity
    false_positive_rate: float  # Fall-out
    false_negative_rate: float  # Miss rate
    false_discovery_rate: float # P(Y=0 | Y_hat=1)
    accuracy: float
    precision: float


@dataclass
class BiasAssessment:
    protected_attribute: str
    reference_group: str
    comparison_group: str
    disparate_impact_ratio: float           # Should be >= 0.8 (80% rule)
    equalized_odds_gap: float               # TPR difference
    false_positive_parity_gap: float        # FPR difference
    calibration_gap: float                  # Precision difference
    overall_bias_detected: bool
    severity: str                           # 'CRITICAL', 'HIGH', 'MEDIUM', 'OK'
    remediation_suggestions: list[str]


@dataclass
class FairnessAuditReport:
    model_name: str
    n_total_samples: int
    protected_attributes: list[str]
    group_metrics: list[FairnessMetrics]
    bias_assessments: list[BiasAssessment]
    overall_fair: bool
    most_biased_attribute: Optional[str]
    summary: str


# Fairness thresholds
DISPARATE_IMPACT_THRESHOLD = 0.8    # 4/5 rule (EEOC)
EQUALIZED_ODDS_THRESHOLD = 0.1     # Max acceptable TPR gap
FPR_PARITY_THRESHOLD = 0.1         # Max acceptable FPR gap


def compute_group_metrics(
    y_true: np.ndarray,
    y_pred: np.ndarray,
    group_name: str
) -> FairnessMetrics:
    n = len(y_true)
    if n == 0:
        return FairnessMetrics(group_name, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0)

    tn, fp, fn, tp = confusion_matrix(y_true, y_pred, labels=[0, 1]).ravel()
    positive_rate = (tp + fp) / n
    tpr = tp / max(tp + fn, 1)
    fpr = fp / max(fp + tn, 1)
    fnr = fn / max(fn + tp, 1)
    fdr = fp / max(tp + fp, 1)
    acc = (tp + tn) / n
    prec = tp / max(tp + fp, 1)

    return FairnessMetrics(
        group_name=group_name,
        n_samples=n,
        positive_rate=round(float(positive_rate), 4),
        true_positive_rate=round(float(tpr), 4),
        false_positive_rate=round(float(fpr), 4),
        false_negative_rate=round(float(fnr), 4),
        false_discovery_rate=round(float(fdr), 4),
        accuracy=round(float(acc), 4),
        precision=round(float(prec), 4)
    )


class SecurityModelFairnessAuditor:
    """
    Explainable AI fairness auditor for security screening models.
    Detects discriminatory patterns in models used for:
    - Background check screening
    - Fraud detection (disparate impact on protected classes)
    - Security clearance scoring
    - Loan/insurance risk scoring

    Implements EEOC 4/5 rule, equalized odds, and calibration fairness.
    Generates actionable remediation recommendations.
    """

    def audit(
        self,
        model_name: str,
        y_true: np.ndarray,
        y_pred: np.ndarray,
        sensitive_features: dict[str, np.ndarray]  # attr_name -> group labels array
    ) -> FairnessAuditReport:
        all_group_metrics = []
        all_bias_assessments = []

        # Overall metrics
        overall = compute_group_metrics(y_true, y_pred, 'overall')
        all_group_metrics.append(overall)

        for attr_name, groups in sensitive_features.items():
            unique_groups = np.unique(groups)
            if len(unique_groups) < 2:
                continue

            # Use majority group as reference
            group_sizes = {g: int((groups == g).sum()) for g in unique_groups}
            reference_group = max(group_sizes, key=group_sizes.get)

            ref_mask = groups == reference_group
            ref_metrics = compute_group_metrics(y_true[ref_mask], y_pred[ref_mask], str(reference_group))
            all_group_metrics.append(ref_metrics)

            for comp_group in unique_groups:
                if comp_group == reference_group:
                    continue

                comp_mask = groups == comp_group
                comp_metrics = compute_group_metrics(y_true[comp_mask], y_pred[comp_mask], str(comp_group))
                all_group_metrics.append(comp_metrics)

                # Disparate Impact
                di_ratio = comp_metrics.positive_rate / max(ref_metrics.positive_rate, 1e-8)

                # Equalized Odds
                eo_gap = abs(comp_metrics.true_positive_rate - ref_metrics.true_positive_rate)

                # FPR Parity
                fpr_gap = abs(comp_metrics.false_positive_rate - ref_metrics.false_positive_rate)

                # Calibration gap
                calib_gap = abs(comp_metrics.precision - ref_metrics.precision)

                bias_detected = (
                    di_ratio < DISPARATE_IMPACT_THRESHOLD or
                    eo_gap > EQUALIZED_ODDS_THRESHOLD or
                    fpr_gap > FPR_PARITY_THRESHOLD
                )

                severity = 'OK'
                if di_ratio < 0.6 or fpr_gap > 0.2:
                    severity = 'CRITICAL'
                elif di_ratio < 0.8 or eo_gap > 0.15:
                    severity = 'HIGH'
                elif bias_detected:
                    severity = 'MEDIUM'

                remediations = []
                if di_ratio < DISPARATE_IMPACT_THRESHOLD:
                    remediations.append(
                        f'Disparate impact {di_ratio:.2f} < 0.8 threshold. '
                        'Apply reweighting or resampling to balance training data.'
                    )
                if eo_gap > EQUALIZED_ODDS_THRESHOLD:
                    remediations.append(
                        f'TPR gap {eo_gap:.2f} exceeds threshold. '
                        'Use post-processing threshold adjustment per group.'
                    )
                if fpr_gap > FPR_PARITY_THRESHOLD:
                    remediations.append(
                        f'FPR gap {fpr_gap:.2f} exceeds threshold. '
                        'Group {comp_group} faces higher false alarm rate — '
                        'review feature selection for proxy variables.'
                    )
                if not remediations:
                    remediations.append('No significant bias detected for this comparison.')

                all_bias_assessments.append(BiasAssessment(
                    protected_attribute=attr_name,
                    reference_group=str(reference_group),
                    comparison_group=str(comp_group),
                    disparate_impact_ratio=round(di_ratio, 4),
                    equalized_odds_gap=round(eo_gap, 4),
                    false_positive_parity_gap=round(fpr_gap, 4),
                    calibration_gap=round(calib_gap, 4),
                    overall_bias_detected=bias_detected,
                    severity=severity,
                    remediation_suggestions=remediations
                ))

        overall_fair = all(not a.overall_bias_detected for a in all_bias_assessments)
        severity_order = {'CRITICAL': 4, 'HIGH': 3, 'MEDIUM': 2, 'OK': 1}
        most_biased = None
```
