```python
    credential_submission_rate: float
    training_completion_rate: float
    last_incident_days_ago: Optional[int]
    preferred_learning_style: str   # 'visual', 'text', 'simulation', 'video'
    weak_phishing_types: list[str]  # Types they fell for


@dataclass
class TrainingModule:
    module_id: str
    title: str
    phishing_type: PhishingType
    difficulty: str  # 'basic', 'intermediate', 'advanced'
    format: str      # 'text', 'quiz', 'simulation', 'video'
    duration_minutes: int
    content: str
    quiz_questions: list[dict]
    red_flags_to_learn: list[str]
    mitre_technique: str


@dataclass
class TrainingPlan:
    user_id: str
    user_name: str
    risk_level: RiskLevel
    risk_score: float
    modules: list[TrainingModule]
    total_duration_minutes: int
    priority_focus_areas: list[str]
    personalized_message: str
    completion_deadline_days: int


TRAINING_CONTENT_LIBRARY = [
    TrainingModule(
        module_id='M001',
        title='Spotting Credential Phishing Emails',
        phishing_type=PhishingType.CREDENTIAL_HARVEST,
        difficulty='basic',
        format='text',
        duration_minutes=10,
        content=(
            'Credential phishing emails try to trick you into entering your username and '
            'password on a fake website. Key red flags:\n'
            '1. Urgency: "Your account will be locked in 24 hours"\n'
            '2. Mismatched sender domain: email from "paypal-support.com" not "paypal.com"\n'
            '3. Generic greeting: "Dear Customer" instead of your name\n'
            '4. Suspicious login link — hover before clicking to see the real URL\n'
            '5. Poor grammar or unusual formatting\n'
            'Always go directly to the website by typing the address, never click links in emails.'
        ),
        quiz_questions=[
            {'q': 'An email says your bank account will be closed unless you verify now. What do you do?',
             'options': ['Click the link', 'Call the bank directly', 'Reply with your credentials', 'Forward to a friend'],
             'answer': 1, 'explanation': 'Always verify with the institution directly, never click links.'},
            {'q': 'Which sender is more suspicious for a Microsoft email?',
             'options': ['microsoft.com', 'microsoft-support.net', 'office.com', 'microsoftonline.com'],
             'answer': 1, 'explanation': 'microsoft-support.net is not a Microsoft domain.'}
        ],
        red_flags_to_learn=['Urgency', 'Domain mismatch', 'Generic greeting', 'Suspicious links'],
        mitre_technique='T1566.001'
    ),
    TrainingModule(
        module_id='M002',
        title='Business Email Compromise (BEC) Defense',
        phishing_type=PhishingType.BUSINESS_EMAIL_COMPROMISE,
        difficulty='intermediate',
        format='simulation',
        duration_minutes=20,
        content=(
            'BEC attacks impersonate executives or trusted vendors to request wire transfers, '
            'gift card purchases, or payroll changes.\n'
            'Common scenarios:\n'
            '- CEO asks urgently for gift cards for client gifts\n'
            '- CFO requests emergency wire transfer\n'
            '- Vendor sends updated bank account details\n'
            'Defense:\n'
            '1. Always verify financial requests via phone call to a known number\n'
            '2. Implement dual-approval for transfers over $X\n'
            '3. Check email headers — display name != real sender address\n'
            '4. Never process requests received via email alone'
        ),
        quiz_questions=[
            {'q': 'The "CEO" emails asking for $50k urgent wire. What do you do?',
             'options': ['Process it — CEO said urgent', 'Call CEO on known phone number to verify',
                         'Reply asking for details', 'Email CFO to approve'],
             'answer': 1, 'explanation': 'Always call on a verified number — never rely on email alone for transfers.'},
        ],
        red_flags_to_learn=['Executive impersonation', 'Unusual urgency', 'Secrecy requests', 'Payment method changes'],
        mitre_technique='T1566'
    ),
    TrainingModule(
        module_id='M003',
        title='Malicious Link and Attachment Defense',
        phishing_type=PhishingType.MALWARE_LINK,
        difficulty='basic',
        format='video',
        duration_minutes=8,
        content=(
            'Malicious links and attachments deliver malware to your device.\n'
            'Safe practices:\n'
            '1. Hover over links before clicking — verify the destination URL\n'
            '2. Never open .exe, .js, .vbs, .bat, .zip, .iso attachments from unknown senders\n'
            '3. Be suspicious of Office docs requesting to "Enable Macros"\n'
            '4. Use a link scanner (VirusTotal) for suspicious URLs\n'
            '5. Report suspicious emails using the "Report Phishing" button'
        ),
        quiz_questions=[
            {'q': 'A colleague sends a zip file with a .exe inside — what do you do?',
             'options': ['Open it', 'Call the colleague to verify', 'Delete immediately', 'Forward to IT first'],
             'answer': 3, 'explanation': 'Forward to IT/security for analysis before opening anything unexpected.'},
        ],
        red_flags_to_learn=['Unexpected attachments', 'Macro enable requests', 'URL mismatch', 'Unsolicited files'],
        mitre_technique='T1566.001'
    ),
    TrainingModule(
        module_id='M004',
        title='Spear Phishing — Targeted Attacks',
        phishing_type=PhishingType.SPEAR_PHISHING,
        difficulty='advanced',
        format='simulation',
        duration_minutes=25,
        content=(
            'Spear phishing targets you specifically using personal information gathered from LinkedIn, '
            'social media, or previous breaches.\n'
            'Attack scenario: An email referencing your recent conference attendance, '
            'with a link to "presentation materials" that downloads malware.\n'
            'Defense:\n'
            '1. Be suspicious of hyper-personalized emails you were not expecting\n'
            '2. Limit personal information on public social media\n'
            '3. Verify out-of-band for any email requesting action'
        ),
        quiz_questions=[
            {'q': 'An email mentions your exact job title, manager name, and recent project. Is this safe?',
             'options': ['Yes, they know too much to be fake', 'Not necessarily — attackers research targets',
                         'Only if from company domain', 'Safe if no links'],
             'answer': 1, 'explanation': 'Detailed personal info is a red flag — attackers use OSINT for targeting.'},
        ],
        red_flags_to_learn=['Over-personalization', 'OSINT abuse', 'Context-aware lures', 'Unusual sender familiarity'],
        mitre_technique='T1566'
    ),
]


class AdaptiveSecurityTrainingGenerator:
    """
    Adaptive security awareness training generator.
    Computes user risk scores from phishing simulation history,
    then selects and sequences training modules based on:
    - Identified weak phishing types
    - Learning style preference
    - Risk level (higher risk -> more modules, shorter deadline)
    - Department-specific threat scenarios
    """

    DEPT_RISK_BOOST = {
        'finance': 0.2,       # High BEC risk
        'hr': 0.15,           # Payroll fraud risk
        'executive': 0.2,     # Whale phishing
        'it': 0.1,
        'sales': 0.1,
        'engineering': 0.05
    }

    def _compute_risk_score(self, user: UserProfile) -> tuple[float, RiskLevel]:
        score = 0.0
        # Phishing click rate (0-1 -> 0-40 points)
        score += user.phishing_click_rate * 40
        # Credential submission (0-1 -> 0-30 points)
        score += user.credential_submission_rate * 30
        # Low training completion
        score += (1.0 - user.training_completion_rate) * 15
        # Recent incident
        if user.last_incident_days_ago is not None:
            recency_penalty = max(0, 15 - user.last_incident_days_ago / 30)
            score += recency_penalty
        # Department boost
        score += self.DEPT_RISK_BOOST.get(user.department.lower(), 0.0) * 100 * 0.1
        score = min(score, 100.0)
        if score >= 70:
            level = RiskLevel.CRITICAL
        elif score >= 50:
            level = RiskLevel.HIGH
        elif score >= 25:
            level = RiskLevel.MEDIUM
        else:
            level = RiskLevel.LOW
        return round(score, 2), level

    def _select_modules(
        self, user: UserProfile, risk_level: RiskLevel
    ) -> list[TrainingModule]:
        modules = list(TRAINING_CONTENT_LIBRARY)

        # Prioritize modules matching weak phishing types
        weak_types = set(user.weak_phishing_types)
        modules.sort(key=lambda m: (
            0 if m.phishing_type.value in weak_types else 1,
            0 if m.format == user.preferred_learning_style else 1
        ))

        # More modules for higher risk
        n_modules = {
            RiskLevel.CRITICAL: 4,
            RiskLevel.HIGH: 3,
            RiskLevel.MEDIUM: 2,
            RiskLevel.LOW: 1
        }[risk_level]

        return modules[:n_modules]

    def _personalized_message(self, user: UserProfile, risk_level: RiskLevel) -> str:
        msg = f'Hi {user.name},\n\n'
        if risk_level == RiskLevel.CRITICAL:
            msg += (
                'Our phishing simulation data shows you are at elevated risk of falling '
                'for social engineering attacks. This is not a criticism — these attacks '
                'are designed by experts. This training will sharpen your skills significantly.'
            )
        elif risk_level == RiskLevel.HIGH:
            msg += (
                'You have shown some vulnerability to phishing in recent simulations. '
                'Completing this targeted training will help you identify the specific '
                'attack types you most need to watch for.'
            )
        else:
            msg += (
                'You are doing well in our phishing simulations! This training '
                'will reinforce your skills and introduce some advanced scenarios.'
            )
        return msg

    def generate_plan(
        self, user: UserProfile
    ) -> TrainingPlan:
        risk_score, risk_level = self._compute_risk_score(user)
        modules = self._select_modules(user, risk_level)
        deadline = {RiskLevel.CRITICAL: 3, RiskLevel.HIGH: 7, RiskLevel.MEDIUM: 14, RiskLevel.LOW: 30}[risk_level]
        focus_areas = list({
            red_flag
            for m in modules
            for red_flag in m.red_flags_to_learn[:2]
        })[:5]
        return TrainingPlan(
            user_id=user.user_id,
            user_name=user.name,
            risk_level=risk_level,
            risk_score=risk_score,
            modules=modules,
            total_duration_minutes=sum(m.duration_minutes for m in modules),
            priority_focus_areas=focus_areas,
            personalized_message=self._personalized_message(user, risk_level),
            completion_deadline_days=deadline
        )
```

Write a Python script to implement an ML-powered insider threat detection system using behavioral baselines and anomaly scoring.
output:
```python
import numpy as np
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Optional
from collections import defaultdict, deque
from enum import Enum


class ThreatCategory(Enum):
    DATA_EXFILTRATION = 'data_exfiltration'
    PRIVILEGE_ABUSE = 'privilege_abuse'
    UNAUTHORIZED_ACCESS = 'unauthorized_access'
    POLICY_VIOLATION = 'policy_violation'
    SABOTAGE = 'sabotage'
    ACCOUNT_COMPROMISE = 'account_compromise'


@dataclass
class UserActivity:
    activity_id: str
    timestamp: datetime
    user_id: str
    activity_type: str  # 'login', 'file_access', 'email', 'print', 'usb', 'db_query', 'web'
    resource: str
    resource_sensitivity: int   # 1-5
    volume_bytes: Optional[int]
    destination: Optional[str]  # IP, email, USB device ID
    hour_of_day: int
    day_of_week: int  # 0=Mon
    is_remote: bool
    application: str


@dataclass
class InsiderThreatAlert:
    alert_id: str
    user_id: str
    timestamp: datetime
    risk_score: float
    threat_categories: list[ThreatCategory]
    anomalies: list[str]
    triggering_activity: str
    recommended_action: str
    evidence: list[dict]


class UserBehaviorBaseline:
    """
    Per-user behavioral baseline that tracks:
    - Typical working hours (hour_of_day distribution)
    - Typical access volumes per activity type
    - Normal resource sensitivity level accessed
    - Remote access frequency
    - Activity velocity (events per hour)
    Uses rolling window to adapt to legitimate behavior changes.
    """
    WINDOW = 500

    def __init__(self, user_id: str):
        self.user_id = user_id
        self.hours: deque = deque(maxlen=self.WINDOW)
        self.volumes: deque = deque(maxlen=self.WINDOW)
        self.sensitivities: deque = deque(maxlen=self.WINDOW)
        self.remote_fraction: deque = deque(maxlen=self.WINDOW)
        self.activity_counts: dict[str, deque] = defaultdict(lambda: deque(maxlen=self.WINDOW))
        self.hourly_event_counts: deque = deque(maxlen=self.WINDOW)
        self._hour_counts: dict[int, int] = defaultdict(int)  # time-of-day histogram
        self.total_events = 0

    def update(self, activity: UserActivity):
        self.total_events += 1
        self.hours.append(activity.hour_of_day)
        self._hour_counts[activity.hour_of_day] += 1
        if activity.volume_bytes:
            self.volumes.append(float(activity.volume_bytes))
        self.sensitivities.append(float(activity.resource_sensitivity))
        self.remote_fraction.append(1.0 if activity.is_remote else 0.0)
        self.activity_counts[activity.activity_type].append(1)

    def is_unusual_hour(self, hour: int, threshold: float = 0.02) -> bool:
        """Is this hour below 2% of historical activity?"""
        if self.total_events < 50:
            return False
        fraction = self._hour_counts.get(hour, 0) / max(self.total_events, 1)
        return fraction < threshold

    def volume_z_score(self, volume: float) -> float:
        if len(self.volumes) < 10:
            return 0.0
        mu = float(np.mean(self.volumes))
        sigma = float(np.std(self.volumes)) or 1.0
        return abs((volume - mu) / sigma)

    def sensitivity_z_score(self, sensitivity: float) -> float:
        if len(self.sensitivities) < 10:
            return 0.0
        mu = float(np.mean(self.sensitivities))
        sigma = float(np.std(self.sensitivities)) or 1.0
        return abs((sensitivity - mu) / sigma)

    def is_unusual_remote(self, is_remote: bool) -> bool:
        if len(self.remote_fraction) < 20:
            return False
        avg_remote = float(np.mean(self.remote_fraction))
        # Unusual if suddenly remote when normally not, or vice versa
        return is_remote and avg_remote < 0.05 or not is_remote and avg_remote > 0.95


HIGH_RISK_DESTINATIONS = {
    'personal_email', 'external_cloud', 'usb_device', 'unknown_ip'
}

HIGH_RISK_ACTIVITIES = {
    'bulk_download', 'mass_print', 'usb_copy', 'external_email_attachment'
}


class InsiderThreatDetector:
    """
    ML-powered insider threat detector.
    For each user activity:
    1. Updates behavioral baseline
    2. Scores anomalies: unusual hours, volume spikes, sensitivity escalation,
       high-risk destinations, policy violations
    3. Aggregates risk score across recent activity window
    4. Triggers alert if cumulative score exceeds threshold
    Designed for UEBA (User and Entity Behavior Analytics) integration.
    """

    ALERT_THRESHOLD = 6.0
    LOOKBACK_WINDOW = 50  # Activities to consider for cumulative score

    def __init__(self):
        self._baselines: dict[str, UserBehaviorBaseline] = {}
        self._activity_buffers: dict[str, deque] = defaultdict(
            lambda: deque(maxlen=self.LOOKBACK_WINDOW)
        )
        self._score_buffers: dict[str, deque] = defaultdict(
            lambda: deque(maxlen=self.LOOKBACK_WINDOW)
        )
        self._alerts: list[InsiderThreatAlert] = []
        self._alert_counter = 0

    def _get_baseline(self, user_id: str) -> UserBehaviorBaseline:
        if user_id not in self._baselines:
            self._baselines[user_id] = UserBehaviorBaseline(user_id)
        return self._baselines[user_id]

    def _score_activity(
        self, activity: UserActivity, baseline: UserBehaviorBaseline
    ) -> tuple[float, list[str], list[ThreatCategory]]:
        score = 0.0
        anomalies = []
        categories = set()

        # Unusual working hours
        if baseline.is_unusual_hour(activity.hour_of_day):
            score += 1.5
            anomalies.append(f'Activity at unusual hour: {activity.hour_of_day}:00')
            categories.add(ThreatCategory.UNAUTHORIZED_ACCESS)

        # Volume anomaly
        if activity.volume_bytes and activity.volume_bytes > 0:
            z_vol = baseline.volume_z_score(float(activity.volume_bytes))
            if z_vol > 3.0:
                score += min(z_vol / 3.0, 2.0)
                anomalies.append(f'Volume spike: {activity.volume_bytes} bytes (z={z_vol:.1f})')
                categories.add(ThreatCategory.DATA_EXFILTRATION)

        # Sensitivity escalation
        z_sens = baseline.sensitivity_z_score(float(activity.resource_sensitivity))
        if z_sens > 2.5:
            score += 1.0
            anomalies.append(f'Accessing unusually sensitive resources (sensitivity={activity.resource_sensitivity})')
            categories.add(ThreatCategory.PRIVILEGE_ABUSE)

        # High-risk destination
        if activity.destination and any(
            hr in activity.destination.lower()
            for hr in HIGH_RISK_DESTINATIONS
        ):
            score += 2.0
            anomalies.append(f'High-risk destination: {activity.destination}')
            categories.add(ThreatCategory.DATA_EXFILTRATION)

        # High-risk activity type
        if activity.activity_type in HIGH_RISK_ACTIVITIES:
            score += 1.5
            anomalies.append(f'High-risk activity type: {activity.activity_type}')
            categories.add(ThreatCategory.DATA_EXFILTRATION)

        # Unusual remote access
        if baseline.is_unusual_remote(activity.is_remote):
            score += 1.0
            anomalies.append('Unusual remote access pattern')
            categories.add(ThreatCategory.ACCOUNT_COMPROMISE)

        # High sensitivity resource accessed remotely
        if activity.is_remote and activity.resource_sensitivity >= 4:
            score += 1.5
            anomalies.append(f'Remote access to sensitivity-{activity.resource_sensitivity} resource')
            categories.add(ThreatCategory.UNAUTHORIZED_ACCESS)

        return score, anomalies, list(categories)

    def _determine_action(self, cumulative_score: float, categories: list[ThreatCategory]) -> str:
        if ThreatCategory.DATA_EXFILTRATION in categories and cumulative_score > 10:
            return 'BLOCK user session immediately and notify SOC — suspected active data exfiltration'
        elif cumulative_score > 8:
            return 'Escalate to security manager — initiate HR review and enhanced monitoring'
        elif cumulative_score > 6:
            return 'Enable enhanced monitoring for 30 days — flag for HR awareness'
        return 'Monitor and log — no immediate action required'

    def process(
        self, activity: UserActivity
    ) -> Optional[InsiderThreatAlert]:
        baseline = self._get_baseline(activity.user_id)
        activity_score, anomalies, categories = self._score_activity(activity, baseline)
        baseline.update(activity)
        self._activity_buffers[activity.user_id].append(activity)
        self._score_buffers[activity.user_id].append(activity_score)

        cumulative_score = sum(self._score_buffers[activity.user_id])

        if cumulative_score >= self.ALERT_THRESHOLD and anomalies:
            self._alert_counter += 1
            evidence = [
                {'activity_id': a.activity_id, 'type': a.activity_type,
                 'resource': a.resource, 'timestamp': a.timestamp.isoformat()}
                for a in list(self._activity_buffers[activity.user_id])[-10:]
            ]
            alert = InsiderThreatAlert(
                alert_id=f'ITD-{self._alert_counter:05d}',
                user_id=activity.user_id,
                timestamp=activity.timestamp,
                risk_score=round(cumulative_score, 2),
                threat_categories=categories,
                anomalies=anomalies,
                triggering_activity=activity.activity_id,
                recommended_action=self._determine_action(cumulative_score, categories),
                evidence=evidence
            )
            self._alerts.append(alert)
            # Reset score buffer after alert to prevent alert storms
            self._score_buffers[activity.user_id].clear()
            return alert
        return None

    def get_alerts(
        self, user_id: Optional[str] = None, limit: int = 100
    ) -> list[InsiderThreatAlert]:
        alerts = self._alerts
        if user_id:
            alerts = [a for a in alerts if a.user_id == user_id]
        return alerts[-limit:]

    def get_risk_summary(self) -> dict:
        if not self._alerts:
            return {'total_alerts': 0}
        cat_counts: dict[str, int] = defaultdict(int)
        for alert in self._alerts:
            for cat in alert.threat_categories:
                cat_counts[cat.value] += 1
        top_users: list[dict] = []
        user_counts: dict[str, int] = defaultdict(int)
        for alert in self._alerts:
            user_counts[alert.user_id] += 1
        for uid, cnt in sorted(user_counts.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1])[:5]:
            top_users.append({'user_id': uid, 'alert_count': cnt})
        return {
            'total_alerts': len(self._alerts),
            'category_breakdown': dict(cat_counts),
            'top_risky_users': top_users,
            'avg_risk_score': round(sum(a.risk_score for a in self._alerts) / len(self._alerts), 2)
        }
```

instruction: Write a Python script to implement a cloud workload security scanner that detects misconfigurations in Kubernetes and Docker deployments.
output:
```python
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional
from enum import Enum


class Severity(Enum):
    CRITICAL = 'CRITICAL'
    HIGH = 'HIGH'
    MEDIUM = 'MEDIUM'
    LOW = 'LOW'
    INFO = 'INFO'


@dataclass
class SecurityFinding:
    finding_id: str
    resource_type: str   # 'Pod', 'Container', 'Deployment', 'ServiceAccount', etc.
    resource_name: str
    namespace: Optional[str]
    check_id: str
    check_name: str
    severity: Severity
    description: str
    evidence: dict
    remediation: str
    cis_reference: str
    mitre_technique: str


@dataclass
class ScanReport:
    scan_id: str
    scanned_at: str
    resource_type: str
    total_resources: int
    total_findings: int
    critical: int
    high: int
    medium: int
    low: int
    findings: list[SecurityFinding]
    compliance_score: float


class KubernetesMisconfigScanner:
    """
    Kubernetes security misconfiguration scanner.
    Checks:
    - Pod security context (privileged, runAsRoot, readOnlyRootFS)
    - Container capabilities (NET_ADMIN, SYS_PTRACE)
    - hostPID/hostNetwork/hostIPC namespace sharing
    - Image pull policy and tag hygiene
    - Service account token automounting
    - Resource limits/requests
    - Secrets in environment variables
    - Network policies existence
    - RBAC over-permissioning
    Maps findings to CIS Kubernetes Benchmark and MITRE ATT&CK.
    """

    def __init__(self):
        self._finding_counter = 0

    def _new_id(self) -> str:
        self._finding_counter += 1
        return f'K8S-{self._finding_counter:05d}'

    def _check_pod_spec(self, name: str, namespace: str, spec: dict) -> list[SecurityFinding]:
        findings = []
        security_context = spec.get('securityContext', {})
        
        # hostPID, hostNetwork, hostIPC
        for host_flag in ['hostPID', 'hostNetwork', 'hostIPC']:
            if spec.get(host_flag):
                findings.append(SecurityFinding(
                    finding_id=self._new_id(), resource_type='Pod', resource_name=name,
                    namespace=namespace, check_id=f'K8S-SC-{host_flag}',
                    check_name=f'{host_flag} enabled',
                    severity=Severity.HIGH,
                    description=f'Pod has {host_flag}=true, which allows the container to see/share the host namespace',
                    evidence={host_flag: True},
                    remediation=f'Set {host_flag}: false in pod spec unless explicitly required',
                    cis_reference='CIS Kubernetes 5.2.2',
                    mitre_technique='T1611'
                ))

        # Run as root at pod level
        if security_context.get('runAsUser') == 0:
            findings.append(SecurityFinding(
                finding_id=self._new_id(), resource_type='Pod', resource_name=name,
                namespace=namespace, check_id='K8S-SC-ROOT-POD',
                check_name='Pod runs as root (UID 0)',
                severity=Severity.HIGH,
                description='Pod security context sets runAsUser=0 (root), increasing container escape risk',
                evidence={'runAsUser': 0},
                remediation='Set runAsUser to a non-zero UID and set runAsNonRoot: true',
                cis_reference='CIS Kubernetes 5.2.6', mitre_technique='T1610'
            ))

        # Service account token automount
        if spec.get('automountServiceAccountToken') is not False:
            findings.append(SecurityFinding(
                finding_id=self._new_id(), resource_type='Pod', resource_name=name,
                namespace=namespace, check_id='K8S-SA-AUTOMOUNT',
                check_name='Service account token automounted',
                severity=Severity.MEDIUM,
                description='Pod automounts service account token — grants API access to any process in the container',
                evidence={'automountServiceAccountToken': True},
                remediation='Set automountServiceAccountToken: false unless the pod needs to call the Kubernetes API',
                cis_reference='CIS Kubernetes 5.1.6', mitre_technique='T1528'
            ))

        # No network policy (check by presence of label)
        if not spec.get('_has_network_policy'):
            findings.append(SecurityFinding(
                finding_id=self._new_id(), resource_type='Pod', resource_name=name,
                namespace=namespace, check_id='K8S-NETPOL-MISSING',
                check_name='No NetworkPolicy applied',
                severity=Severity.MEDIUM,
                description='Pod has no NetworkPolicy — any pod in the cluster can reach it',
                evidence={'network_policy': 'none'},
                remediation='Apply a NetworkPolicy restricting ingress/egress to required services only',
                cis_reference='CIS Kubernetes 5.3.2', mitre_technique='T1210'
            ))

        return findings

    def _check_container(
        self, pod_name: str, namespace: str, container: dict
    ) -> list[SecurityFinding]:
        findings = []
        name = container.get('name', 'unknown')
        ctx = container.get('securityContext', {})
        resource_name = f'{pod_name}/{name}'

        # Privileged container
        if ctx.get('privileged'):
            findings.append(SecurityFinding(
                finding_id=self._new_id(), resource_type='Container', resource_name=resource_name,
                namespace=namespace, check_id='K8S-CTR-PRIVILEGED',
                check_name='Privileged container',
                severity=Severity.CRITICAL,
                description='Container runs in privileged mode — has full host capabilities, trivial container escape',
                evidence={'privileged': True},
                remediation='Remove privileged: true. Use specific capability grants instead',
                cis_reference='CIS Kubernetes 5.2.1', mitre_technique='T1611'
            ))

        # Dangerous capabilities
        add_caps = ctx.get('capabilities', {}).get('add', [])
        dangerous_caps = {'NET_ADMIN', 'SYS_PTRACE', 'SYS_ADMIN', 'SYS_MODULE', 'ALL'}
        found_caps = [c for c in add_caps if c in dangerous_caps]
        if found_caps:
            findings.append(SecurityFinding(
                finding_id=self._new_id(), resource_type='Container', resource_name=resource_name,
                namespace=namespace, check_id='K8S-CTR-DANGEROUS-CAPS',
                check_name=f'Dangerous capabilities added: {found_caps}',
                severity=Severity.HIGH,
                description=f'Container adds dangerous Linux capabilities: {found_caps}',
                evidence={'capabilities_add': found_caps},
                remediation='Remove dangerous capabilities. Drop ALL capabilities and add only required ones',
                cis_reference='CIS Kubernetes 5.2.8', mitre_technique='T1611'
            ))

        # No capability drop
        drop_caps = ctx.get('capabilities', {}).get('drop', [])
        if 'ALL' not in drop_caps and not ctx.get('privileged'):
            findings.append(SecurityFinding(
                finding_id=self._new_id(), resource_type='Container', resource_name=resource_name,
                namespace=namespace, check_id='K8S-CTR-NO-CAPS-DROP',
                check_name='Not dropping all capabilities',
                severity=Severity.MEDIUM,
                description='Container does not drop all Linux capabilities — unnecessary attack surface',
                evidence={'capabilities_drop': drop_caps},
                remediation='Add securityContext.capabilities.drop: [ALL]',
                cis_reference='CIS Kubernetes 5.2.7', mitre_technique='T1611'
            ))

        # readOnlyRootFilesystem not set
        if not ctx.get('readOnlyRootFilesystem'):
            findings.append(SecurityFinding(
                finding_id=self._new_id(), resource_type='Container', resource_name=resource_name,
                namespace=namespace, check_id='K8S-CTR-WRITABLE-FS',
                check_name='Writable root filesystem',
                severity=Severity.MEDIUM,
                description='Container root filesystem is writable — attackers can write malware to disk',
                evidence={'readOnlyRootFilesystem': False},
                remediation='Set securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem: true',
                cis_reference='CIS Kubernetes 5.2.4', mitre_technique='T1027'
            ))

        # No resource limits
        resources = container.get('resources', {})
        if not resources.get('limits'):
            findings.append(SecurityFinding(
                finding_id=self._new_id(), resource_type='Container', resource_name=resource_name,
                namespace=namespace, check_id='K8S-CTR-NO-LIMITS',
                check_name='No resource limits set',
                severity=Severity.LOW,
                description='Container has no CPU/memory limits — vulnerable to resource exhaustion / DoS',
                evidence={'resources': resources},
                remediation='Set resources.limits.cpu and resources.limits.memory',
                cis_reference='CIS Kubernetes 5.2.3', mitre_technique='T1499'
            ))

        # Secrets in environment variables
        envs = container.get('env', [])
        secret_patterns = ['password', 'secret', 'api_key', 'token', 'private_key', 'credential']
        for env in envs:
            env_name_lower = env.get('name', '').lower()
            if any(p in env_name_lower for p in secret_patterns) and 'valueFrom' not in env:
                findings.append(SecurityFinding(
                    finding_id=self._new_id(), resource_type='Container', resource_name=resource_name,
                    namespace=namespace, check_id='K8S-CTR-SECRET-ENV',
                    check_name=f'Secret in plain-text env var: {env["name"]}',
                    severity=Severity.HIGH,
                    description=f'Sensitive value in env var {env["name"]} as plain text — visible in kubectl describe',
                    evidence={'env_var': env['name']},
                    remediation='Use secretKeyRef to reference Kubernetes Secrets or a secrets manager',
                    cis_reference='CIS Kubernetes 5.4.1', mitre_technique='T1552.001'
                ))

        # Image using latest tag
        image = container.get('image', '')
        if ':latest' in image or ':' not in image:
            findings.append(SecurityFinding(
                finding_id=self._new_id(), resource_type='Container', resource_name=resource_name,
                namespace=namespace, check_id='K8S-CTR-LATEST-TAG',
                check_name='Container image uses latest or no tag',
                severity=Severity.LOW,
                description=f'Image {image} uses :latest or no tag — unpredictable deployments and no immutability',
                evidence={'image': image},
                remediation='Pin images to specific SHA digests or immutable version tags',
                cis_reference='CIS Kubernetes 5.5.1', mitre_technique='T1525'
            ))

        return findings

    def scan_pod(
        self, name: str, namespace: str, pod_spec: dict
    ) -> list[SecurityFinding]:
        findings = self._check_pod_spec(name, namespace, pod_spec)
        for container in pod_spec.get('containers', []):
            findings.extend(self._check_container(name, namespace, container))
        for container in pod_spec.get('initContainers', []):
            findings.extend(self._check_container(f'{name}/init', namespace, container))
        return findings

    def scan_workloads(
        self, workloads: list[dict]
    ) -> ScanReport:
        import uuid
        from datetime import datetime
        self._finding_counter = 0
        all_findings = []
        for workload in workloads:
            name = workload.get('name', 'unknown')
            ns = workload.get('namespace', 'default')
            spec = workload.get('spec', {})
            all_findings.extend(self.scan_pod(name, ns, spec))
        sev_counts = {s: 0 for s in Severity}
        for f in all_findings:
            sev_counts[f.severity] += 1
        n = len(all_findings)
        critical_weight = sev_counts[Severity.CRITICAL] * 10
        high_weight = sev_counts[Severity.HIGH] * 5
        compliance_score = max(0.0, 100.0 - (critical_weight + high_weight) / max(len(workloads), 1))
        return ScanReport(
            scan_id=str(uuid.uuid4())[:8],
            scanned_at=datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            resource_type='Kubernetes',
            total_resources=len(workloads),
            total_findings=n,
            critical=sev_counts[Severity.CRITICAL],
            high=sev_counts[Severity.HIGH],
            medium=sev_counts[Severity.MEDIUM],
            low=sev_counts[Severity.LOW],
            findings=sorted(all_findings, key=lambda f: list(Severity).index(f.severity)),
            compliance_score=round(compliance_score, 2)
        )
```

instruction: Write a Python script to implement a machine learning model watermarking system to detect model theft and unauthorized redistribution.
output:
```python
import hashlib
import numpy as np
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Optional
from sklearn.base import BaseEstimator


@dataclass
class WatermarkConfig:
    owner_id: str
    model_id: str
    watermark_key: str          # Secret key for watermark generation
    n_trigger_samples: int = 50  # Number of backdoor trigger samples
    target_class: int = 0        # Label backdoor triggers should predict
    trigger_feature_indices: Optional[list[int]] = None  # Which features to perturb
    trigger_pattern: Optional[np.ndarray] = None
    expected_watermark_accuracy: float = 0.95  # Expect >95% on trigger set


@dataclass
class WatermarkVerificationResult:
    model_id_tested: str
    watermark_detected: bool
    trigger_accuracy: float
    threshold: float
    confidence: float
    n_trigger_samples: int
    n_correct: int
    owner_id: str
    evidence_strength: str


class ModelWatermarker:
    """
    ML model watermarking via backdoor-based ownership verification.
    Concept:
    - Owner generates a secret trigger set: inputs with a specific
      perturbation pattern that cause the model to predict a specific class
    - The model is fine-tuned on clean data + trigger samples
    - To verify ownership, the owner presents the trigger set and checks
      if the suspicious model predicts the target class at >threshold accuracy
    - Legitimate users cannot reproduce the trigger without the secret key
    - Distinct from normal backdoor attacks: owner uses this defensively
      to prove ownership, not to harm users
    """

    def __init__(self, config: WatermarkConfig):
        self.config = config
        self._trigger_set: Optional[tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]] = None
        self._rng = np.random.default_rng(
            int(hashlib.sha256(config.watermark_key.encode()).hexdigest()[:8], 16)
        )

    def _generate_trigger_pattern(
        self, n_features: int
    ) -> np.ndarray:
        """Deterministic trigger pattern derived from watermark key."""
        seed = int(hashlib.sha256(
            (self.config.watermark_key + self.config.model_id).encode()
        ).hexdigest()[:8], 16)
        rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
        pattern = rng.normal(0, 1.0, n_features)
        return pattern / (np.linalg.norm(pattern) + 1e-8)

    def _apply_trigger(
        self, X: np.ndarray, trigger_pattern: np.ndarray, strength: float = 2.0
    ) -> np.ndarray:
        X_trigger = X.copy().astype(float)
        if self.config.trigger_feature_indices:
            for idx in self.config.trigger_feature_indices:
                X_trigger[:, idx] += strength * trigger_pattern[idx]
        else:
            X_trigger += strength * trigger_pattern
        return X_trigger

    def generate_trigger_set(
        self, X_clean: np.ndarray, n_samples: Optional[int] = None
    ) -> tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]:
        n = n_samples or self.config.n_trigger_samples
        n_features = X_clean.shape[1]
        trigger_pattern = self.config.trigger_pattern or self._generate_trigger_pattern(n_features)
        self.config.trigger_pattern = trigger_pattern

        # Sample clean inputs and apply trigger
        indices = self._rng.choice(len(X_clean), min(n, len(X_clean)), replace=False)
        X_trigger = self._apply_trigger(X_clean[indices], trigger_pattern)
        y_trigger = np.full(len(indices), self.config.target_class)
        self._trigger_set = (X_trigger, y_trigger)
        return X_trigger, y_trigger

    def generate_watermarked_training_data(
        self,
        X_train: np.ndarray,
        y_train: np.ndarray,
        poison_rate: float = 0.05
    ) -> tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]:
        """Mix clean training data with trigger samples."""
        if self.config.trigger_pattern is None:
            self.generate_trigger_set(X_train)
        X_trig, y_trig = self._trigger_set
        n_clean = len(X_train)
        n_poison = max(1, int(n_clean * poison_rate))
        # Use first n_poison trigger samples
        X_mixed = np.vstack([X_train, X_trig[:n_poison]])
        y_mixed = np.concatenate([y_train, y_trig[:n_poison]])
        # Shuffle
        perm = self._rng.permutation(len(X_mixed))
        return X_mixed[perm], y_mixed[perm]

    def verify_ownership(
        self,
        suspect_model: BaseEstimator,
        threshold: Optional[float] = None
    ) -> WatermarkVerificationResult:
        if self._trigger_set is None:
            raise ValueError('No trigger set generated. Call generate_trigger_set first.')
        if threshold is None:
            threshold = self.config.expected_watermark_accuracy
        X_trig, y_trig = self._trigger_set
        try:
            y_pred = suspect_model.predict(X_trig)
            n_correct = int((y_pred == y_trig).sum())
            trigger_acc = n_correct / len(y_trig)
        except Exception as e:
            return WatermarkVerificationResult(
                model_id_tested='unknown', watermark_detected=False,
                trigger_accuracy=0.0, threshold=threshold, confidence=0.0,
                n_trigger_samples=len(y_trig), n_correct=0,
                owner_id=self.config.owner_id,
                evidence_strength='ERROR: ' + str(e)
            )
        detected = trigger_acc >= threshold
        # Confidence: how far above threshold
        confidence = min(1.0, (trigger_acc - threshold) / (1.0 - threshold)) if detected else 0.0
        if trigger_acc >= 0.99:
            strength = 'VERY STRONG'
        elif trigger_acc >= 0.95:
            strength = 'STRONG'
        elif trigger_acc >= 0.85:
            strength = 'MODERATE'
        elif trigger_acc >= threshold:
            strength = 'WEAK'
        else:
            strength = 'NOT DETECTED'
        return WatermarkVerificationResult(
            model_id_tested=self.config.model_id,
            watermark_detected=detected,
            trigger_accuracy=round(trigger_acc, 4),
            threshold=threshold,
            confidence=round(confidence, 4),
            n_trigger_samples=len(y_trig),
            n_correct=n_correct,
            owner_id=self.config.owner_id,
            evidence_strength=strength
        )

    def compute_fingerprint(self, X: np.ndarray) -> str:
        """Compute a compact model fingerprint from predictions on trigger set."""
        # Fingerprint is based on trigger-set predictions — owner-secret-derived
        trigger_pattern = self.config.trigger_pattern or self._generate_trigger_pattern(X.shape[1])
        X_probe = self._apply_trigger(X[:20], trigger_pattern)
        fingerprint_data = trigger_pattern.tobytes() + self.config.watermark_key.encode()
        return hashlib.sha256(fingerprint_data).hexdigest()[:32]
```

Write a Python script to implement a zero-day vulnerability prediction system using ML on code features and commit metadata.
output:
```python
import numpy as np
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Optional
from sklearn.ensemble import GradientBoostingClassifier
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.model_selection import cross_val_score
import re


@dataclass
class CodeFeatures:
    file_path: str
    language: str
    loc: int
    cyclomatic_complexity: int
    n_functions: int
    n_classes: int
    n_imports: int
    n_string_formats: int   # f-strings, %s, format() calls
    n_eval_exec: int        # eval(), exec(), system() calls
    n_unchecked_returns: int
    n_buffer_ops: int       # strcpy, memcpy, strcat patterns
    n_sql_queries: int
    n_crypto_calls: int
    n_file_ops: int
    n_network_ops: int
    comment_ratio: float
    halstead_volume: float  # Approximate
    author_experience_days: int
    days_since_last_vuln: int  # Days since last CVE in this file
    co_change_risk: float       # How often this file changes with known-vuln files
    test_coverage: float
    n_recent_churn: int         # Lines changed in last 30 days


@dataclass
class VulnPrediction:
    file_path: str
    vuln_probability: float
    risk_tier: str  # 'CRITICAL', 'HIGH', 'MEDIUM', 'LOW'
    top_risk_features: list[dict]
    cwe_candidates: list[str]
    recommended_actions: list[str]


VULN_FEATURE_NAMES = [
    'loc', 'cyclomatic_complexity', 'n_functions', 'n_classes', 'n_imports',
    'n_string_formats', 'n_eval_exec', 'n_unchecked_returns', 'n_buffer_ops',
    'n_sql_queries', 'n_crypto_calls', 'n_file_ops', 'n_network_ops',
    'comment_ratio', 'halstead_volume', 'author_experience_days',
    'days_since_last_vuln', 'co_change_risk', 'test_coverage', 'n_recent_churn'
]

CWE_HEURISTICS = [
    ('n_sql_queries', 5, 'CWE-89: SQL Injection'),
    ('n_buffer_ops', 3, 'CWE-120: Buffer Overflow'),
    ('n_eval_exec', 1, 'CWE-78: OS Command Injection'),
    ('n_string_formats', 10, 'CWE-134: Format String'),
    ('n_unchecked_returns', 5, 'CWE-252: Unchecked Return Value'),
    ('n_crypto_calls', 3, 'CWE-327: Broken Cryptography'),
    ('n_file_ops', 5, 'CWE-22: Path Traversal'),
    ('cyclomatic_complexity', 20, 'CWE-691: Insufficient Control Flow'),
]


class CodeFeatureExtractor:
    """Extract security-relevant features from source code."""

    BUFFER_PATTERNS = re.compile(r'\b(strcpy|strcat|sprintf|gets|memcpy|memmove)\s*\(')
    EVAL_PATTERNS = re.compile(r'\b(eval|exec|system|popen|subprocess\.call)\s*\(')
    SQL_PATTERNS = re.compile(r'\b(execute|cursor\.execute|query)\s*\(')
    CRYPTO_PATTERNS = re.compile(r'\b(md5|sha1|des|rc4|AES|encrypt|decrypt|hash)\b', re.IGNORECASE)
    FILE_PATTERNS = re.compile(r'\b(open|read|write|fopen|fwrite|fread)\s*\(')
    NET_PATTERNS = re.compile(r'\b(socket|connect|send|recv|requests\.|urllib)\b')
    FORMAT_PATTERNS = re.compile(r'(%s|%d|%f|\.format\(|f")')

    def extract_from_code(self, code: str, file_path: str = '') -> dict:
        lines = code.split('\n')
        loc = len([l for l in lines if l.strip()])
        comment_lines = len([l for l in lines if l.strip().startswith(('#', '//', '/*', '*'))])
        return {
            'loc': loc,
            'n_buffer_ops': len(self.BUFFER_PATTERNS.findall(code)),
            'n_eval_exec': len(self.EVAL_PATTERNS.findall(code)),
            'n_sql_queries': len(self.SQL_PATTERNS.findall(code)),
            'n_crypto_calls': len(self.CRYPTO_PATTERNS.findall(code)),
            'n_file_ops': len(self.FILE_PATTERNS.findall(code)),
            'n_network_ops': len(self.NET_PATTERNS.findall(code)),
            'n_string_formats': len(self.FORMAT_PATTERNS.findall(code)),
            'comment_ratio': comment_lines / max(loc, 1),
        }


class ZeroDayVulnPredictor:
    """
    Zero-day vulnerability prediction system.
    Uses Gradient Boosting on a combined feature vector of:
    - Code complexity metrics (LOC, cyclomatic complexity)
    - Security-sensitive API usage (SQL, eval, buffer ops)
    - Development context (author experience, recent churn, test coverage)
    - Historical vulnerability signal (days since last CVE, co-change risk)
    Trained on labeled dataset of vulnerable/clean code files.
    Outputs CWE candidates and remediation recommendations.
    """

    def __init__(self):
        self.model = GradientBoostingClassifier(
            n_estimators=200, max_depth=5,
            learning_rate=0.05, subsample=0.8,
            random_state=42
        )
        self.scaler = StandardScaler()
        self._trained = False
        self._feature_importances: Optional[dict] = None

    def _features_to_array(self, cf: CodeFeatures) -> np.ndarray:
        return np.array([
            cf.loc, cf.cyclomatic_complexity, cf.n_functions, cf.n_classes,
            cf.n_imports, cf.n_string_formats, cf.n_eval_exec,
            cf.n_unchecked_returns, cf.n_buffer_ops, cf.n_sql_queries,
            cf.n_crypto_calls, cf.n_file_ops, cf.n_network_ops,
            cf.comment_ratio, cf.halstead_volume, cf.author_experience_days,
            cf.days_since_last_vuln, cf.co_change_risk,
            cf.test_coverage, cf.n_recent_churn
        ], dtype=float)

    def train(
        self,
        code_features: list[CodeFeatures],
        labels: list[int]  # 1=vulnerable, 0=clean
    ) -> dict:
        X = np.array([self._features_to_array(cf) for cf in code_features])
        y = np.array(labels)
        X_scaled = self.scaler.fit_transform(X)
        cv_scores = cross_val_score(self.model, X_scaled, y, cv=5, scoring='roc_auc')
        self.model.fit(X_scaled, y)
        self._trained = True
        self._feature_importances = {
            name: float(imp)
            for name, imp in zip(VULN_FEATURE_NAMES, self.model.feature_importances_)
        }
        return {
            'cv_auc_mean': round(float(cv_scores.mean()), 4),
            'cv_auc_std': round(float(cv_scores.std()), 4),
            'top_features': sorted(
                self._feature_importances.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]
            )[:5]
        }

    def _identify_cwes(self, cf: CodeFeatures) -> list[str]:
        cwes = []
        for feature_name, threshold, cwe in CWE_HEURISTICS:
            val = getattr(cf, feature_name, 0)
            if val >= threshold:
                cwes.append(cwe)
        return cwes[:5]

    def _get_recommendations(self, cf: CodeFeatures, cwes: list[str]) -> list[str]:
        recs = []
        if cf.n_sql_queries >= 3:
            recs.append('Replace dynamic SQL with parameterized queries or ORM')
        if cf.n_eval_exec >= 1:
            recs.append('Remove eval/exec/system calls; use safe alternatives')
        if cf.n_buffer_ops >= 2:
            recs.append('Replace unsafe C string functions with bounds-checked alternatives')
        if cf.cyclomatic_complexity > 15:
            recs.append('Refactor complex functions to reduce cyclomatic complexity')
        if cf.test_coverage < 0.5:
            recs.append('Increase test coverage — aim for >= 80% on security-sensitive paths')
        if cf.n_unchecked_returns >= 5:
            recs.append('Add return value checks and error handling')
        if not recs:
            recs.append('Conduct manual code review — no specific automated remediation identified')
        return recs

    def predict(
        self, cf: CodeFeatures
    ) -> VulnPrediction:
        if not self._trained:
            # Use heuristic scoring if not trained
            score = self._heuristic_score(cf)
        else:
            x = self._features_to_array(cf).reshape(1, -1)
            x_scaled = self.scaler.transform(x)
            score = float(self.model.predict_proba(x_scaled)[0, 1])

        if score >= 0.8:
            tier = 'CRITICAL'
        elif score >= 0.6:
            tier = 'HIGH'
        elif score >= 0.35:
            tier = 'MEDIUM'
        else:
            tier = 'LOW'

        cwes = self._identify_cwes(cf)
        recs = self._get_recommendations(cf, cwes)

        top_risk_features = []
        if self._feature_importances:
            for feat, imp in sorted(self._feature_importances.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1])[:5]:
                top_risk_features.append({
                    'feature': feat,
                    'importance': round(imp, 4),
                    'value': getattr(cf, feat, None)
                })

        return VulnPrediction(
            file_path=cf.file_path,
            vuln_probability=round(score, 4),
            risk_tier=tier,
            top_risk_features=top_risk_features,
            cwe_candidates=cwes,
            recommended_actions=recs
        )

    def _heuristic_score(self, cf: CodeFeatures) -> float:
        score = 0.0
        score += min(cf.n_eval_exec * 0.15, 0.3)
        score += min(cf.n_buffer_ops * 0.1, 0.2)
        score += min(cf.n_sql_queries * 0.05, 0.15)
        score += (1.0 - cf.test_coverage) * 0.15
        score += min(cf.cyclomatic_complexity / 50, 0.1)
        score += min(cf.n_recent_churn / 500, 0.1)
        return min(score, 1.0)

    def predict_batch(
        self, code_features: list[CodeFeatures]
    ) -> list[VulnPrediction]:
        results = [self.predict(cf) for cf in code_features]
        tier_order = ['CRITICAL', 'HIGH', 'MEDIUM', 'LOW']
        return sorted(results, key=lambda r: (
            tier_order.index(r.risk_tier), -r.vuln_probability
        ))
```

instruction: Write a Python script to implement an adversarial NLP attack detector that identifies prompt injection and jailbreak attempts in LLM inputs.
output:
```python
import re
import math
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional
from collections import Counter
from enum import Enum


class AttackType(Enum):
    PROMPT_INJECTION = 'prompt_injection'
    JAILBREAK = 'jailbreak'
    ROLE_PLAY_BYPASS = 'role_play_bypass'
    SYSTEM_OVERRIDE = 'system_override'
    DATA_EXFILTRATION = 'data_exfiltration'
    INDIRECT_INJECTION = 'indirect_injection'
    BENIGN = 'benign'


@dataclass
class DetectionResult:
    input_text: str
    is_attack: bool
    attack_type: AttackType
    confidence: float
    threat_score: float
    triggered_rules: list[str]
    sanitized_text: Optional[str]
    recommendation: str


# --- Rule-based detection patterns ---
PROMPT_INJECTION_PATTERNS = [
    re.compile(r'ignore (previous|above|all) instructions?', re.I),
    re.compile(r'disregard (your|the|all) (previous|system|prior) (instructions?|prompt|context)', re.I),
    re.compile(r'forget (everything|all|what) (you|I|we)', re.I),
    re.compile(r'new (system |)instructions?:', re.I),
    re.compile(r'you are now (a |an )?(?!assistant|helpful)', re.I),
    re.compile(r'act as (a |an )?(?!assistant)', re.I),
    re.compile(r'your (true|real|actual|original) (self|purpose|goal|objective)', re.I),
    re.compile(r'\[SYSTEM\]|\[ADMIN\]|\[OVERRIDE\]|\[INST\]', re.I),
    re.compile(r'pretend (you|that you) (are|have no|don.t have)', re.I),
]

JAILBREAK_PATTERNS = [
    re.compile(r'DAN|do anything now', re.I),
    re.compile(r'jailbreak(ed)?|jail.?broke', re.I),
    re.compile(r'without (any |)restrictions|no (rules|limits|restrictions)', re.I),
    re.compile(r'developer mode|dev mode|debug mode', re.I),
    re.compile(r'hypothetically (speaking|if|suppose)', re.I),
    re.compile(r'as a (fictional|hypothetical|creative) (character|AI|entity)', re.I),
    re.compile(r'in (a story|fiction|a movie|a novel).{0,50}how (to|would|can)', re.I),
    re.compile(r'(translate|encode|decode|base64).{0,50}(bomb|weapon|exploit|malware)', re.I),
]

SYSTEM_OVERRIDE_PATTERNS = [
    re.compile(r'system prompt|system message|meta prompt', re.I),
    re.compile(r'(reveal|show|print|output|display|repeat|tell me) (the |your )?(system|initial|original) (prompt|instructions?|context)', re.I),
    re.compile(r'what (are|were) (your|the) (instructions?|system|initial)', re.I),
    re.compile(r'<\|im_start\||<\|im_end\||\[INST\]|<s>|</s>', re.I),
    re.compile(r'END OF SYSTEM|--- END SYSTEM ---', re.I),
]

DATA_EXFIL_PATTERNS = [
    re.compile(r'(send|post|fetch|make.{0,10}request).{0,50}(http|https|url|endpoint)', re.I),
    re.compile(r'(exfiltrate|steal|extract|leak).{0,50}(data|password|key|secret)', re.I),
    re.compile(r'(previous|prior|earlier|above).{0,30}(messages?|conversation|context|text)', re.I),
]

INDIRECT_INJECTION_PATTERNS = [
    re.compile(r'\[.*?inject.*?\]', re.I),
    re.compile(r'when (the user|user|you) (says?|asks?|types?).{0,50}(do|say|respond)', re.I),
    re.compile(r'<script|javascript:|data:text/html', re.I),
    re.compile(r'<\!--.*?(inject|command|instruction).*?-->', re.I),
]


class LLMAttackDetector:
    """
    Adversarial NLP attack detector for LLM inputs.
    Multi-layered detection:
    1. Rule-based pattern matching (prompt injection, jailbreak, system override)
    2. Statistical signals (perplexity proxy via char n-gram, unusual token ratios)
    3. Structural analysis (delimiter injection, unusual Unicode, base64 encoding)
    4. Semantic heuristics (authority claims, urgency markers)
    Outputs detection result with attack type, confidence, and sanitized text.
    """

    URGENCY_WORDS = {'immediately', 'urgent', 'now', 'must', 'critical', 'emergency', 'override', 'bypass'}
    AUTHORITY_WORDS = {'admin', 'administrator', 'system', 'operator', 'openai', 'anthropic', 'developer', 'god mode'}
    MAX_TEXT_LEN = 10000

    def __init__(
        self,
        block_threshold: float = 0.7,
        warn_threshold: float = 0.4
    ):
        self.block_threshold = block_threshold
        self.warn_threshold = warn_threshold

    def _count_pattern_matches(
        self, text: str, patterns: list[re.Pattern]
    ) -> tuple[int, list[str]]:
        matches = []
        count = 0
        for pat in patterns:
            found = pat.findall(text)
            if found:
                count += len(found)
                matches.append(f'{pat.pattern[:50]}... ({len(found)} match)')
        return count, matches

    def _char_bigram_perplexity_proxy(self, text: str) -> float:
        """Approximate text naturalness via character bigram entropy."""
        if len(text) < 10:
            return 1.0
        bigrams = [text[i:i+2] for i in range(len(text) - 1)]
        counts = Counter(bigrams)
        total = len(bigrams)
        entropy = -sum(
            (c / total) * math.log2(c / total)
            for c in counts.values()
        )
        # Normalize by max possible entropy (log2 of unique bigrams)
        n_unique = len(counts)
        max_entropy = math.log2(max(n_unique, 1))
        return entropy / max(max_entropy, 1.0)

    def _detect_unusual_unicode(self, text: str) -> tuple[float, list[str]]:
        reasons = []
        score = 0.0
        # High proportion of non-ASCII
        non_ascii = sum(1 for c in text if ord(c) > 127)
        non_ascii_ratio = non_ascii / max(len(text), 1)
        if non_ascii_ratio > 0.3:
            score += 0.2
            reasons.append(f'High non-ASCII ratio: {non_ascii_ratio:.2%}')
        # Homoglyph attacks (Cyrillic/Greek lookalikes)
        suspicious_chars = [c for c in text if 0x0400 <= ord(c) <= 0x04FF]
        if suspicious_chars:
            score += 0.3
            reasons.append(f'Cyrillic characters detected — possible homoglyph attack')
        # Zero-width characters
        zero_width = [c for c in text if ord(c) in (0x200B, 0x200C, 0x200D, 0xFEFF, 0x2060)]
        if zero_width:
            score += 0.4
            reasons.append(f'{len(zero_width)} zero-width characters detected — possible steganographic injection')
        return score, reasons

    def _detect_base64_payload(self, text: str) -> tuple[float, list[str]]:
        import base64
        b64_pattern = re.compile(r'[A-Za-z0-9+/]{20,}={0,2}')
        matches = b64_pattern.findall(text)
        reasons = []
        score = 0.0
        for match in matches[:3]:
            try:
                decoded = base64.b64decode(match).decode('utf-8', errors='ignore')
                # Check if decoded content looks like instructions
                if any(kw in decoded.lower() for kw in ['ignore', 'disregard', 'jailbreak', 'system']):
                    score += 0.5
                    reasons.append(f'Base64 payload contains injection keywords: {decoded[:50]}')
            except Exception:
                pass
        return score, reasons

    def _detect_authority_urgency(self, text: str) -> tuple[float, list[str]]:
        words = set(re.findall(r'\b\w+\b', text.lower()))
        authority_hits = words & self.AUTHORITY_WORDS
        urgency_hits = words & self.URGENCY_WORDS
        reasons = []
        score = 0.0
        if authority_hits:
            score += len(authority_hits) * 0.1
            reasons.append(f'Authority claim words: {authority_hits}')
        if urgency_hits and authority_hits:  # Combination is more suspicious
            score += 0.2
            reasons.append('Authority + urgency combination — social engineering pattern')
        return min(score, 0.4), reasons

    def detect(self, text: str) -> DetectionResult:
        if len(text) > self.MAX_TEXT_LEN:
            text = text[:self.MAX_TEXT_LEN]

        all_reasons = []
        total_score = 0.0
        attack_type = AttackType.BENIGN
        attack_type_scores = {t: 0.0 for t in AttackType if t != AttackType.BENIGN}

        # Pattern-based detection
        inj_count, inj_reasons = self._count_pattern_matches(text, PROMPT_INJECTION_PATTERNS)
        if inj_count:
            s = min(inj_count * 0.3, 0.9)
            attack_type_scores[AttackType.PROMPT_INJECTION] += s
            total_score = max(total_score, s)
            all_reasons.extend(inj_reasons)

        jb_count, jb_reasons = self._count_pattern_matches(text, JAILBREAK_PATTERNS)
        if jb_count:
            s = min(jb_count * 0.35, 0.9)
            attack_type_scores[AttackType.JAILBREAK] += s
            total_score = max(total_score, s)
            all_reasons.extend(jb_reasons)

        sys_count, sys_reasons = self._count_pattern_matches(text, SYSTEM_OVERRIDE_PATTERNS)
        if sys_count:
            s = min(sys_count * 0.3, 0.8)
            attack_type_scores[AttackType.SYSTEM_OVERRIDE] += s
            total_score = max(total_score, s)
            all_reasons.extend(sys_reasons)

        exfil_count, exfil_reasons = self._count_pattern_matches(text, DATA_EXFIL_PATTERNS)
        if exfil_count:
            s = min(exfil_count * 0.25, 0.7)
            attack_type_scores[AttackType.DATA_EXFILTRATION] += s
            total_score = max(total_score, s)
            all_reasons.extend(exfil_reasons)

        indirect_count, indirect_reasons = self._count_pattern_matches(text, INDIRECT_INJECTION_PATTERNS)
        if indirect_count:
            s = min(indirect_count * 0.3, 0.8)
            attack_type_scores[AttackType.INDIRECT_INJECTION] += s
            total_score = max(total_score, s)
            all_reasons.extend(indirect_reasons)

        # Statistical and structural analysis
        unicode_score, unicode_reasons = self._detect_unusual_unicode(text)
        if unicode_score > 0:
            total_score = max(total_score, unicode_score)
            all_reasons.extend(unicode_reasons)

        b64_score, b64_reasons = self._detect_base64_payload(text)
        if b64_score > 0:
            attack_type_scores[AttackType.INDIRECT_INJECTION] += b64_score
            total_score = max(total_score, b64_score)
            all_reasons.extend(b64_reasons)

        auth_score, auth_reasons = self._detect_authority_urgency(text)
        total_score = min(total_score + auth_score * 0.3, 1.0)
        all_reasons.extend(auth_reasons)

        # Determine primary attack type
        if total_score > 0:
            attack_type = max(attack_type_scores, key=lambda t: attack_type_scores[t])
            if attack_type_scores[attack_type] == 0:
                attack_type = AttackType.BENIGN

        is_attack = total_score >= self.warn_threshold
        confidence = min(total_score / max(self.warn_threshold, 0.1), 1.0)

        # Sanitize: remove matched injection patterns
        sanitized = text
        if is_attack:
            for pat in PROMPT_INJECTION_PATTERNS + JAILBREAK_PATTERNS + SYSTEM_OVERRIDE_PATTERNS:
                sanitized = pat.sub('[REDACTED]', sanitized)

        if total_score >= self.block_threshold:
            rec = 'BLOCK — High-confidence attack detected. Reject input and log for review.'
        elif total_score >= self.warn_threshold:
            rec = 'WARN — Suspicious input. Review before processing. Consider rate-limiting this user.'
        else:
            rec = 'ALLOW — No significant attack signals detected.'

        return DetectionResult(
            input_text=text[:200],
            is_attack=is_attack,
            attack_type=attack_type,
            confidence=round(confidence, 4),
            threat_score=round(total_score, 4),
            triggered_rules=all_reasons[:10],
            sanitized_text=sanitized if is_attack else None,
            recommendation=rec
        )

    def detect_batch(
        self, texts: list[str]
    ) -> list[DetectionResult]:
        return [self.detect(t) for t in texts]
```

instruction: Write a Python script to implement a cloud security posture management (CSPM) tool that scans AWS IAM policies for over-permissive access.
output:
```python
import json
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional
from enum import Enum


class PolicyFindingType(Enum):
    WILDCARD_ACTION = 'wildcard_action'
    WILDCARD_RESOURCE = 'wildcard_resource'
    ADMIN_POLICY = 'admin_policy'
    SENSITIVE_ACTION_EXPOSED = 'sensitive_action_exposed'
    NO_CONDITION = 'no_condition'
    CROSS_ACCOUNT_TRUST = 'cross_account_trust'
    PUBLIC_TRUST = 'public_trust'
    PRIVILEGE_ESCALATION = 'privilege_escalation'


class Severity(Enum):
    CRITICAL = 'CRITICAL'
    HIGH = 'HIGH'
    MEDIUM = 'MEDIUM'
    LOW = 'LOW'


@dataclass
class IAMFinding:
    finding_id: str
    entity_name: str
    entity_type: str   # 'role', 'user', 'group', 'policy'
    policy_name: str
    finding_type: PolicyFindingType
    severity: Severity
    statement_index: int
    actions: list[str]
    resources: list[str]
    description: str
    mitre_technique: str
    remediation: str
    cis_reference: str


@dataclass
class IAMScanReport:
    scan_id: str
    scanned_at: str
    total_policies: int
    total_entities: int
    total_findings: int
    critical: int
    high: int
    medium: int
    low: int
    findings: list[IAMFinding]
    least_privilege_score: float
    top_risks: list[str]


SENSITIVE_ACTIONS = {
    'iam:*', 'iam:CreateUser', 'iam:CreateRole', 'iam:AttachRolePolicy',
    'iam:AttachUserPolicy', 'iam:PutRolePolicy', 'iam:CreatePolicyVersion',
    'iam:SetDefaultPolicyVersion', 'iam:PassRole', 'sts:AssumeRole',
    'ec2:*', 's3:*', 'kms:*', 'secretsmanager:*', 'ssm:*',
    'lambda:*', 'cloudformation:*', 'organizations:*'
}

PRIV_ESC_PAIRS = [
    ({'iam:CreatePolicyVersion'}, {'iam:SetDefaultPolicyVersion'}),
    ({'iam:CreateAccessKey'}, set()),
    ({'iam:CreateLoginProfile'}, set()),
    ({'iam:AttachUserPolicy', 'iam:AttachRolePolicy'}, set()),
    ({'lambda:CreateFunction', 'lambda:InvokeFunction'}, {'iam:PassRole'}),
    ({'cloudformation:CreateStack'}, {'iam:PassRole'}),
]


class CSPMIAMScanner:
    """
    Cloud Security Posture Management (CSPM) tool for AWS IAM.
    Analyzes IAM policies for:
    - Wildcard actions (*) and resources (*)
    - Admin policy attachment (AdministratorAccess)
    - Sensitive actions without conditions
    - Public trust policies (Principal: *)
    - Cross-account trust without conditions
    - Privilege escalation paths (action combinations that allow escalation)
    Maps to MITRE ATT&CK and CIS AWS Benchmark.
    """

    def __init__(self, account_id: str = '123456789012'):
        self.account_id = account_id
        self._counter = 0

    def _new_id(self) -> str:
        self._counter += 1
        return f'IAM-{self._counter:05d}'

    def _check_statement(
        self,
        stmt: dict,
        stmt_idx: int,
        entity_name: str,
        entity_type: str,
        policy_name: str
    ) -> list[IAMFinding]:
        findings = []
        effect = stmt.get('Effect', 'Allow')
        if effect != 'Allow':
            return findings  # Only analyze Allow statements

        actions_raw = stmt.get('Action', [])
        if isinstance(actions_raw, str):
            actions = [actions_raw]
        else:
            actions = list(actions_raw)

        resources_raw = stmt.get('Resource', [])
        if isinstance(resources_raw, str):
            resources = [resources_raw]
        else:
            resources = list(resources_raw)

        conditions = stmt.get('Condition', {})
        principal = stmt.get('Principal')

        # Check for wildcard action (*)
        wildcard_actions = [a for a in actions if a == '*' or a.endswith(':*')]
        if wildcard_actions:
            is_admin = '*' in wildcard_actions
            findings.append(IAMFinding(
                finding_id=self._new_id(), entity_name=entity_name, entity_type=entity_type,
                policy_name=policy_name, finding_type=PolicyFindingType.WILDCARD_ACTION,
                severity=Severity.CRITICAL if is_admin else Severity.HIGH,
                statement_index=stmt_idx, actions=wildcard_actions, resources=resources,
                description=f'Wildcard action {wildcard_actions} grants unrestricted permissions',
                mitre_technique='T1098', remediation='Replace wildcard with specific required actions',
                cis_reference='CIS AWS 1.16'
            ))

        # Wildcard resource with sensitive actions
        has_wildcard_resource = '*' in resources
        sensitive_matches = [
            a for a in actions
            if any(a == sa or a.startswith(sa.replace('*', '')) for sa in SENSITIVE_ACTIONS)
        ]
        if has_wildcard_resource and sensitive_matches:
            findings.append(IAMFinding(
                finding_id=self._new_id(), entity_name=entity_name, entity_type=entity_type,
                policy_name=policy_name, finding_type=PolicyFindingType.WILDCARD_RESOURCE,
                severity=Severity.HIGH, statement_index=stmt_idx,
                actions=sensitive_matches[:5], resources=['*'],
                description=f'Sensitive actions {sensitive_matches[:3]} apply to all resources (*)',
                mitre_technique='T1078', remediation='Restrict Resource to specific ARNs',
                cis_reference='CIS AWS 1.22'
            ))

        # Sensitive actions without MFA/IP condition
        mfa_condition = any(
            'aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent' in str(v)
            for v in conditions.values()
        ) if conditions else False
        if sensitive_matches and not mfa_condition and not conditions:
            findings.append(IAMFinding(
                finding_id=self._new_id(), entity_name=entity_name, entity_type=entity_type,
                policy_name=policy_name, finding_type=PolicyFindingType.NO_CONDITION,
                severity=Severity.MEDIUM, statement_index=stmt_idx,
                actions=sensitive_matches[:5], resources=resources,
                description='Sensitive actions granted without any condition (MFA, IP, time)',
                mitre_technique='T1078',
                remediation='Add Condition: aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent: true for sensitive actions',
                cis_reference='CIS AWS 1.10'
            ))

        # Public trust (Principal: *)
        if principal == '*' or principal == {'AWS': '*'}:
            findings.append(IAMFinding(
                finding_id=self._new_id(), entity_name=entity_name, entity_type=entity_type,
                policy_name=policy_name, finding_type=PolicyFindingType.PUBLIC_TRUST,
                severity=Severity.CRITICAL, statement_index=stmt_idx,
                actions=actions[:5], resources=resources,
                description='Principal: * allows ANY entity to assume this role/access this resource',
                mitre_technique='T1078',
                remediation='Restrict Principal to specific AWS accounts, roles, or services',
                cis_reference='CIS AWS 1.22'
            ))

        # Cross-account trust without external-id condition
        if isinstance(principal, dict):
            aws_principals = principal.get('AWS', [])
            if isinstance(aws_principals, str):
                aws_principals = [aws_principals]
            external_accounts = [
                p for p in aws_principals
                if self.account_id not in p and 'arn:aws' in p
            ]
            has_ext_id = 'sts:ExternalId' in str(conditions)
            if external_accounts and not has_ext_id:
                findings.append(IAMFinding(
                    finding_id=self._new_id(), entity_name=entity_name, entity_type=entity_type,
                    policy_name=policy_name, finding_type=PolicyFindingType.CROSS_ACCOUNT_TRUST,
                    severity=Severity.HIGH, statement_index=stmt_idx,
                    actions=actions[:3], resources=resources,
                    description=f'Cross-account trust from {external_accounts} without sts:ExternalId condition (confused deputy risk)',
                    mitre_technique='T1199',
                    remediation='Add Condition: sts:ExternalId to prevent confused deputy attacks',
                    cis_reference='CIS AWS 1.21'
                ))

        return findings

    def _check_privilege_escalation(
        self,
        all_actions: set[str],
        entity_name: str,
        entity_type: str,
        policy_name: str
    ) -> list[IAMFinding]:
        findings = []
        for required_primary, required_secondary in PRIV_ESC_PAIRS:
            has_primary = required_primary & all_actions
            if not has_primary:
                continue
            if not required_secondary or (required_secondary & all_actions):
                findings.append(IAMFinding(
                    finding_id=self._new_id(), entity_name=entity_name, entity_type=entity_type,
                    policy_name='combined_policies', finding_type=PolicyFindingType.PRIVILEGE_ESCALATION,
                    severity=Severity.CRITICAL, statement_index=-1,
                    actions=list(has_primary | (required_secondary & all_actions)),
                    resources=['*'],
                    description=f'Privilege escalation path detected via {has_primary}',
                    mitre_technique='T1068',
                    remediation='Remove privilege escalation actions or restrict to specific resources with conditions',
                    cis_reference='CIS AWS 1.16'
                ))
        return findings

    def scan_entity(
        self,
        entity_name: str,
        entity_type: str,
        policies: list[dict]
    ) -> list[IAMFinding]:
        all_findings = []
        all_actions: set[str] = set()
        for policy in policies:
            policy_name = policy.get('PolicyName', 'inline')
            # Handle attached managed policies
            if policy_name in ('AdministratorAccess', 'AWSFullAccess'):
                all_findings.append(IAMFinding(
                    finding_id=self._new_id(), entity_name=entity_name, entity_type=entity_type,
                    policy_name=policy_name, finding_type=PolicyFindingType.ADMIN_POLICY,
                    severity=Severity.CRITICAL, statement_index=-1,
                    actions=['*'], resources=['*'],
                    description=f'AdministratorAccess policy attached — full AWS access',
                    mitre_technique='T1078.004',
                    remediation='Replace AdministratorAccess with least-privilege policies',
                    cis_reference='CIS AWS 1.16'
                ))
                continue
            doc = policy.get('PolicyDocument', {})
            if isinstance(doc, str):
                try:
                    doc = json.loads(doc)
                except Exception:
                    continue
            for idx, stmt in enumerate(doc.get('Statement', [])):
                findings = self._check_statement(stmt, idx, entity_name, entity_type, policy_name)
                all_findings.extend(findings)
                actions_raw = stmt.get('Action', [])
                if isinstance(actions_raw, str):
                    actions_raw = [actions_raw]
                all_actions.update(actions_raw)
        # Check for privilege escalation across all policies
        all_findings.extend(self._check_privilege_escalation(all_actions, entity_name, entity_type, 'combined'))
        return all_findings

    def scan(
        self,
        entities: list[dict]
    ) -> IAMScanReport:
        import uuid
        from datetime import datetime
        self._counter = 0
        all_findings = []
        for entity in entities:
            findings = self.scan_entity(
                entity.get('name', 'unknown'),
                entity.get('type', 'role'),
                entity.get('policies', [])
            )
            all_findings.extend(findings)
        sev = {s: sum(1 for f in all_findings if f.severity == s) for s in Severity}
        n = len(all_findings)
        # Least privilege score: 100 - penalty per finding
        penalty = sev[Severity.CRITICAL] * 20 + sev[Severity.HIGH] * 10 + sev[Severity.MEDIUM] * 3 + sev[Severity.LOW]
        lp_score = max(0.0, 100.0 - penalty / max(len(entities), 1))
        top_risks = list({
            f.description[:80] for f in all_findings
            if f.severity == Severity.CRITICAL
        })[:5]
        return IAMScanReport(
            scan_id=str(uuid.uuid4())[:8],
            scanned_at=datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            total_policies=sum(len(e.get('policies', [])) for e in entities),
            total_entities=len(entities),
            total_findings=n,
            critical=sev[Severity.CRITICAL], high=sev[Severity.HIGH],
            medium=sev[Severity.MEDIUM], low=sev[Severity.LOW],
            findings=sorted(all_findings, key=lambda f: list(Severity).index(f.severity)),
            least_privilege_score=round(lp_score, 2),
            top_risks=top_risks
        )
```

instruction: Write a Python script to implement a network traffic classification system using deep learning with flow feature extraction.
output:
```python
import numpy as np
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional
from collections import defaultdict


@dataclass
class PacketRecord:
    timestamp: float
    src_ip: str
    dst_ip: str
    src_port: int
    dst_port: int
    protocol: str   # 'TCP', 'UDP', 'ICMP'
    length: int
    flags: str      # TCP flags, e.g. 'SYN', 'SYN-ACK', 'FIN'
    payload_bytes: int


@dataclass
class NetworkFlow:
    flow_id: str
    src_ip: str
    dst_ip: str
    src_port: int
    dst_port: int
    protocol: str
    start_time: float
    end_time: float
    packets: list[PacketRecord]


@dataclass
class FlowFeatures:
    flow_id: str
    # Temporal features
    duration: float
    n_packets: int
    n_bytes: int
    bytes_per_second: float
    packets_per_second: float
    # Packet size statistics
    mean_pkt_size: float
    std_pkt_size: float
    min_pkt_size: float
    max_pkt_size: float
    # Inter-arrival time statistics
    mean_iat: float
    std_iat: float
    min_iat: float
    max_iat: float
    # Protocol and port features
    is_tcp: int
    is_udp: int
    is_icmp: int
    is_well_known_port: int
    dst_port: int
    # TCP flag features
    has_syn: int
    has_fin: int
    has_rst: int
    has_push: int
    has_ack: int
    # Payload features
    mean_payload_size: float
    payload_ratio: float
    # Burst features
    n_bursts: int
    mean_burst_size: float


@dataclass
class ClassificationResult:
    flow_id: str
    predicted_class: str
    confidence: float
    class_probabilities: dict
    is_malicious: bool
    top_features: list[dict]
    alert_message: Optional[str]


TRAFFIC_CLASSES = [
    'benign_web', 'benign_streaming', 'benign_voip',
    'botnet_c2', 'dos_flood', 'port_scan', 'data_exfiltration', 'cryptomining'
]

WELL_KNOWN_PORTS = {80, 443, 22, 53, 25, 110, 143, 21, 3306, 5432}


def extract_flow_features(flow: NetworkFlow) -> FlowFeatures:
    pkts = flow.packets
    if not pkts:
        raise ValueError('Empty flow')
    durations = flow.end_time - flow.start_time
    sizes = [p.length for p in pkts]
    payloads = [p.payload_bytes for p in pkts]
    timestamps = [p.timestamp for p in pkts]
    iats = [timestamps[i+1] - timestamps[i] for i in range(len(timestamps)-1)]
    # TCP flags
    flags_set = {f for p in pkts for f in p.flags.split('-') if f}
    # Burst detection: group packets with IAT < 0.1s
    bursts, current_burst = [], [pkts[0]]
    for i in range(1, len(pkts)):
        if iats[i-1] < 0.1:
            current_burst.append(pkts[i])
        else:
            bursts.append(current_burst)
            current_burst = [pkts[i]]
    bursts.append(current_burst)
    return FlowFeatures(
        flow_id=flow.flow_id,
        duration=max(durations, 0.001),
        n_packets=len(pkts),
        n_bytes=sum(sizes),
        bytes_per_second=sum(sizes) / max(durations, 0.001),
        packets_per_second=len(pkts) / max(durations, 0.001),
        mean_pkt_size=float(np.mean(sizes)),
```
