```python
    def network_inventory(self) -> Dict:
        summary = defaultdict(list)
        for ip, fp in self.device_profiles.items():
            summary[fp.device_type].append(ip)
        high_risk = [fp for fp in self.device_profiles.values()
                     if fp.risk_level in ("HIGH", "CRITICAL")]
        return {
            "total_devices": len(self.device_profiles),
            "by_type": dict(summary),
            "high_risk_devices": [{"ip": fp.device_ip, "type": fp.device_type,
                                    "risk": fp.risk_level, "behaviors": fp.behaviors}
                                   for fp in high_risk],
        }


if __name__ == "__main__":
    def make_flows(dst_port, count=10, pkt_sizes=None, dns=None):
        return [NetworkFlow(
            src_ip="192.168.1.100", dst_ip=f"1.2.3.{i}",
            src_port=50000+i, dst_port=dst_port,
            protocol="TCP", packet_sizes=pkt_sizes or [500]*10,
            timestamps=[float(i) for i in range(10)],
            dns_queries=dns or [],
        ) for i in range(count)]

    system = IoTFingerprintingSystem()
    # Philips Hue bridge
    hue_flows = make_flows(443, dns=["meethue.com", "api.meethue.com"])
    fp1 = system.fingerprint_device("192.168.1.50", "00:17:88:AA:BB:CC", hue_flows)
    print(f"Device 1: {fp1.device_ip} -> {fp1.device_type} ({fp1.manufacturer}) risk={fp1.risk_level}")

    # Suspicious IP camera
    cam_flows = make_flows(554, count=20, pkt_sizes=[1400]*10)
    cam_flows += make_flows(4444, count=5)  # C2 port!
    fp2 = system.fingerprint_device("192.168.1.75", None, cam_flows)
    print(f"Device 2: {fp2.device_ip} -> {fp2.device_type} risk={fp2.risk_level}")
    print(f"  Anomalies: {fp2.behaviors}")

    # Amazon Echo
    echo_flows = make_flows(443, dns=["alexa.amazon.com", "avs-alexa-na.amazon.com"])
    fp3 = system.fingerprint_device("192.168.1.90", "EC:FA:BC:11:22:33", echo_flows)
    print(f"Device 3: {fp3.device_ip} -> {fp3.device_type} ({fp3.manufacturer}) conf={fp3.confidence:.0%}")

    inventory = system.network_inventory()
    print(f"\nNetwork Inventory: {json.dumps(inventory, indent=2)}")
```

instruction: Create a security knowledge graph builder that ingests threat intelligence reports, CVE data, and incident logs to construct a graph database of attack relationships, threat actors, and vulnerabilities.
output:
```python
import json
import re
import hashlib
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import List, Dict, Set, Optional, Tuple
from collections import defaultdict
from datetime import datetime
from enum import Enum

class NodeType(Enum):
    THREAT_ACTOR = "threat_actor"
    VULNERABILITY = "vulnerability"
    MALWARE = "malware"
    TECHNIQUE = "technique"
    TOOL = "tool"
    CAMPAIGN = "campaign"
    INDICATOR = "indicator"
    TARGET_SECTOR = "target_sector"
    CVE = "cve"

class EdgeType(Enum):
    USES = "uses"
    EXPLOITS = "exploits"
    TARGETS = "targets"
    ATTRIBUTED_TO = "attributed_to"
    RELATED_TO = "related_to"
    DEPLOYS = "deploys"
    MITIGATED_BY = "mitigated_by"
    PART_OF = "part_of"
    INDICATES = "indicates"

@dataclass
class GraphNode:
    node_id: str
    node_type: NodeType
    name: str
    attributes: Dict = field(default_factory=dict)
    confidence: float = 0.8
    source: str = ""
    first_seen: str = ""
    last_seen: str = ""

@dataclass
class GraphEdge:
    edge_id: str
    source_id: str
    target_id: str
    edge_type: EdgeType
    confidence: float
    evidence: str
    timestamp: str

@dataclass
class IntelligenceReport:
    report_id: str
    title: str
    text: str
    source: str
    date: str
    tlp: str = "WHITE"  # TLP classification


class EntityExtractor:

    """
    NLP-lite entity extractor for threat intelligence text.
    Uses pattern matching + curated threat actor/malware databases.
    """

    KNOWN_THREAT_ACTORS = {
        "apt28": "APT28", "fancy bear": "APT28",
        "apt29": "APT29", "cozy bear": "APT29",
        "lazarus": "Lazarus Group", "hidden cobra": "Lazarus Group",
        "apt41": "APT41", "winnti": "APT41",
        "sandworm": "Sandworm Team",
        "fin7": "FIN7", "carbanak": "FIN7",
        "apt1": "APT1", "comment crew": "APT1",
        "darkside": "DarkSide", "revil": "REvil", "sodinokibi": "REvil",
        "lockbit": "LockBit",
    }

    KNOWN_MALWARE = {
        "emotet", "trickbot", "cobalt strike", "metasploit",
        "mimikatz", "powersploit", "empire", "cobaltstrike",
        "ryuk", "conti", "blackcat", "alphv", "lockbit",
        "notpetya", "wannacry", "petya",
        "gh0st", "plugx", "poison ivy", "njrat",
        "darkcomet", "remcos", "asyncrat",
    }

    MITRE_TECHNIQUES = {
        r'T(\d{4})(?:\.(\d{3}))?': 'technique',
    }

    CVE_PATTERN = re.compile(r'CVE-\d{4}-\d{4,7}', re.IGNORECASE)
    IP_PATTERN = re.compile(r'\b(?:\d{1,3}\.){3}\d{1,3}\b')
    DOMAIN_PATTERN = re.compile(
        r'\b(?:[a-zA-Z0-9](?:[a-zA-Z0-9-]{0,61}[a-zA-Z0-9])?\.)'
        r'+(?:com|net|org|io|gov|edu|ru|cn|xyz|info)\b'
    )
    HASH_PATTERN = re.compile(r'\b[a-fA-F0-9]{32}\b|\b[a-fA-F0-9]{40}\b|\b[a-fA-F0-9]{64}\b')

    def extract(self, text: str) -> Dict[str, List[str]]:
        text_lower = text.lower()
        entities = defaultdict(list)
        # Threat actors
        for alias, canonical in self.KNOWN_THREAT_ACTORS.items():
            if alias in text_lower and canonical not in entities["threat_actor"]:
                entities["threat_actor"].append(canonical)
        # Malware
        for malware in self.KNOWN_MALWARE:
            if malware in text_lower and malware not in entities["malware"]:
                entities["malware"].append(malware.title())
        # CVEs
        entities["cve"].extend(list(set(self.CVE_PATTERN.findall(text))))
        # IPs (filter private)
        all_ips = self.IP_PATTERN.findall(text)
        entities["ip"].extend([
            ip for ip in all_ips
            if not ip.startswith(("192.168.", "10.", "172.", "127."))
        ])
        # Domains
        domains = self.DOMAIN_PATTERN.findall(text)
        entities["domain"].extend(list(set(domains[:20])))
        # File hashes
        entities["hash"].extend(list(set(self.HASH_PATTERN.findall(text)))[:10])
        # MITRE techniques
        for pattern in self.MITRE_TECHNIQUES:
            for match in re.finditer(pattern, text):
                tid = f"T{match.group(1)}"
                if match.group(2):
                    tid += f".{match.group(2)}"
                if tid not in entities["technique"]:
                    entities["technique"].append(tid)
        return dict(entities)


class SecurityKnowledgeGraph:
    """
    In-memory security knowledge graph.
    Nodes: entities (threat actors, CVEs, malware, techniques).
    Edges: relationships (uses, exploits, targets, attributed_to).
    Supports graph traversal, path finding, and risk scoring.
    """

    def __init__(self):
        self.nodes: Dict[str, GraphNode] = {}
        self.edges: List[GraphEdge] = []
        self.adjacency: Dict[str, List[str]] = defaultdict(list)  # node_id -> neighbor ids
        self._edge_counter = 0

    def _make_id(self, node_type: NodeType, name: str) -> str:
        return f"{node_type.value}:{hashlib.md5(name.lower().encode()).hexdigest()[:8]}"

    def add_node(self, name: str, node_type: NodeType,
                  attributes: Dict = None, source: str = "",
                  confidence: float = 0.8) -> str:
        node_id = self._make_id(node_type, name)
        if node_id not in self.nodes:
            self.nodes[node_id] = GraphNode(
                node_id=node_id, node_type=node_type, name=name,
                attributes=attributes or {}, confidence=confidence,
                source=source, first_seen=datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
                last_seen=datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            )
        else:
            # Update last_seen and merge attributes
            self.nodes[node_id].last_seen = datetime.utcnow().isoformat()
            if attributes:
                self.nodes[node_id].attributes.update(attributes)
        return node_id

    def add_edge(self, source_id: str, target_id: str,
                  edge_type: EdgeType, confidence: float = 0.7,
                  evidence: str = "") -> str:
        self._edge_counter += 1
        edge_id = f"e{self._edge_counter:06d}"
        edge = GraphEdge(
            edge_id=edge_id, source_id=source_id, target_id=target_id,
            edge_type=edge_type, confidence=confidence,
            evidence=evidence[:200], timestamp=datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
        )
        # Dedup check
        existing = [e for e in self.edges
                    if e.source_id == source_id and e.target_id == target_id
                    and e.edge_type == edge_type]
        if not existing:
            self.edges.append(edge)
            self.adjacency[source_id].append(target_id)
            self.adjacency[target_id].append(source_id)
        return edge_id

    def get_neighbors(self, node_id: str, edge_type: Optional[EdgeType] = None) -> List[GraphNode]:
        neighbors = []
        for edge in self.edges:
            if edge.source_id == node_id:
                if edge_type is None or edge.edge_type == edge_type:
                    if edge.target_id in self.nodes:
                        neighbors.append(self.nodes[edge.target_id])
        return neighbors

    def shortest_path(self, start_id: str, end_id: str) -> Optional[List[str]]:
        """BFS shortest path between two nodes."""
        if start_id not in self.nodes or end_id not in self.nodes:
            return None
        visited = {start_id}
        queue = [[start_id]]
        while queue:
            path = queue.pop(0)
            node = path[-1]
            if node == end_id:
                return path
            for neighbor_id in self.adjacency.get(node, []):
                if neighbor_id not in visited:
                    visited.add(neighbor_id)
                    queue.append(path + [neighbor_id])
        return None

    def threat_actor_risk_score(self, actor_id: str) -> float:
        """Score a threat actor based on connections in the graph."""
        if actor_id not in self.nodes:
            return 0.0
        neighbors = self.get_neighbors(actor_id)
        score = 0.0
        for n in neighbors:
            if n.node_type == NodeType.CVE:
                score += 15
            elif n.node_type == NodeType.MALWARE:
                score += 10
            elif n.node_type == NodeType.TECHNIQUE:
                score += 5
            elif n.node_type == NodeType.CAMPAIGN:
                score += 8
        return min(100.0, score)

    def stats(self) -> Dict:
        by_type = defaultdict(int)
        for node in self.nodes.values():
            by_type[node.node_type.value] += 1
        edge_by_type = defaultdict(int)
        for edge in self.edges:
            edge_by_type[edge.edge_type.value] += 1
        return {
            "total_nodes": len(self.nodes),
            "total_edges": len(self.edges),
            "nodes_by_type": dict(by_type),
            "edges_by_type": dict(edge_by_type),
        }


class ThreatIntelIngester:
    """
    Ingests threat intelligence reports and builds the knowledge graph.
    """

    def __init__(self):
        self.graph = SecurityKnowledgeGraph()
        self.extractor = EntityExtractor()

    def ingest_report(self, report: IntelligenceReport) -> Dict:
        entities = self.extractor.extract(report.text)
        actor_ids = []
        malware_ids = []
        cve_ids = []
        technique_ids = []
        # Add threat actor nodes
        for actor in entities.get("threat_actor", []):
            aid = self.graph.add_node(actor, NodeType.THREAT_ACTOR,
                                      {"source": report.source}, source=report.source)
            actor_ids.append(aid)
        # Add malware nodes
        for malware in entities.get("malware", []):
            mid = self.graph.add_node(malware, NodeType.MALWARE,
                                      source=report.source)
            malware_ids.append(mid)
            # Actor -> deploys -> malware
            for aid in actor_ids:
                self.graph.add_edge(aid, mid, EdgeType.DEPLOYS,
                                    evidence=f"From report: {report.title[:50]}")
        # Add CVE nodes
        for cve in entities.get("cve", []):
            cid = self.graph.add_node(cve, NodeType.CVE, source=report.source)
            cve_ids.append(cid)
            for mid in malware_ids:
                self.graph.add_edge(mid, cid, EdgeType.EXPLOITS,
                                    evidence=f"From report: {report.title[:50]}")
        # Add technique nodes
        for technique in entities.get("technique", []):
            tid = self.graph.add_node(technique, NodeType.TECHNIQUE,
                                      source=report.source)
            technique_ids.append(tid)
            for aid in actor_ids:
                self.graph.add_edge(aid, tid, EdgeType.USES,
                                    evidence=f"MITRE: {technique}")
        # Add IoC nodes
        for ip in entities.get("ip", []):
            iid = self.graph.add_node(ip, NodeType.INDICATOR,
                                      {"type": "ip"}, source=report.source)
            for aid in actor_ids:
                self.graph.add_edge(aid, iid, EdgeType.RELATED_TO)
        for domain in entities.get("domain", [])[:5]:
            did = self.graph.add_node(domain, NodeType.INDICATOR,
                                      {"type": "domain"}, source=report.source)
        return {
            "report": report.title,
            "extracted": {k: len(v) for k, v in entities.items()},
            "nodes_added": len(actor_ids) + len(malware_ids) + len(cve_ids) + len(technique_ids),
        }

    def query_actor(self, actor_name: str) -> Dict:
        """Get all intelligence about a threat actor."""
        actor_id = self.graph._make_id(NodeType.THREAT_ACTOR, actor_name)
        if actor_id not in self.graph.nodes:
            return {"error": f"Actor '{actor_name}' not found"}
        actor = self.graph.nodes[actor_id]
        neighbors = self.graph.get_neighbors(actor_id)
        risk = self.graph.threat_actor_risk_score(actor_id)
        return {
            "actor": actor.name,
            "risk_score": risk,
            "malware_used": [n.name for n in neighbors if n.node_type == NodeType.MALWARE],
            "techniques": [n.name for n in neighbors if n.node_type == NodeType.TECHNIQUE],
            "exploited_cves": [n.name for n in neighbors if n.node_type == NodeType.CVE],
            "indicators": [n.name for n in neighbors if n.node_type == NodeType.INDICATOR][:5],
        }


if __name__ == "__main__":
    ingester = ThreatIntelIngester()
    reports = [
        IntelligenceReport(
            report_id="RPT001",
            title="APT28 Spear-Phishing Campaign Targeting Defense Sector",
            text="""APT28 (also known as Fancy Bear) has been observed conducting spear-phishing campaigns
            against European defense contractors. The group deploys Cobalt Strike and Mimikatz for
            credential harvesting. They exploit CVE-2023-23397 and CVE-2021-40444 to gain initial access.
            Techniques observed include T1566.001 (Spear-Phishing), T1055 (Process Injection),
            and T1003.001 (LSASS Memory Dumping).
            C2 infrastructure observed at 185.220.101.45 and update-service.microsoftonline.net.""",
            source="Threat Intelligence Team",
            date="2024-01-15",
        ),
        IntelligenceReport(
            report_id="RPT002",
            title="LockBit 3.0 Ransomware Campaign Analysis",
            text="""LockBit ransomware group has been attributed to attacks on financial institutions.
            The group uses Cobalt Strike for lateral movement before deploying LockBit encryption.
            Initial access is gained by exploiting CVE-2023-4966 (Citrix Bleed) and CVE-2023-27350.
            MITRE techniques: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery),
            T1059.001 (PowerShell). IOCs include hash 5d41402abc4b2a76b9719d911017c592.""",
            source="ISAC Sharing",
            date="2024-02-01",
        ),
    ]
    for report in reports:
        result = ingester.ingest_report(report)
        print(f"Ingested: {result}")
    print(f"\nGraph Stats: {json.dumps(ingester.graph.stats(), indent=2)}")
    print(f"\nAPT28 Profile:")
    apt28 = ingester.query_actor("APT28")
    for k, v in apt28.items():
        print(f"  {k}: {v}")
    print(f"\nLockBit Profile:")
    lockbit = ingester.query_actor("LockBit")
    for k, v in lockbit.items():
        print(f"  {k}: {v}")
    # Find shortest path between APT28 and CVE
    apt28_id = ingester.graph._make_id(NodeType.THREAT_ACTOR, "APT28")
    cve_id = ingester.graph._make_id(NodeType.CVE, "CVE-2023-23397")
    path = ingester.graph.shortest_path(apt28_id, cve_id)
    if path:
        path_names = [ingester.graph.nodes[n].name for n in path if n in ingester.graph.nodes]
        print(f"\nAttack path APT28 -> CVE-2023-23397: {' -> '.join(path_names)}")
```

instruction: Implement a network microsegmentation analyzer that evaluates firewall rules and network ACLs to identify over-permissive segments, lateral movement paths, and recommends zero-trust segmentation policies.
output:
```python
import json
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import List, Dict, Set, Optional, Tuple
from collections import defaultdict
from enum import Enum
import ipaddress

class PolicyAction(Enum):
    ALLOW = "allow"
    DENY = "deny"

@dataclass
class FirewallRule:
    rule_id: str
    priority: int
    source: str          # IP/CIDR or zone name
    destination: str
    port: str            # port number, range, or "any"
    protocol: str        # tcp, udp, icmp, any
    action: PolicyAction
    description: str = ""

@dataclass
class NetworkSegment:
    name: str
    cidr: str
    zone: str            # dmz, internal, management, external, ot
    sensitivity: str     # critical, high, medium, low
    allowed_inbound: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    allowed_outbound: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)

@dataclass
class SegmentationFinding:
    severity: str
    finding_type: str
    source_segment: str
    destination_segment: str
    description: str
    attack_risk: str
    remediation: str


class CIDRMatcher:
    def matches(self, ip_or_cidr: str, rule_src: str) -> bool:
        if rule_src in ("any", "0.0.0.0/0", "*"):
            return True
        try:
            network = ipaddress.ip_network(rule_src, strict=False)
            if '/' in ip_or_cidr:
                host_net = ipaddress.ip_network(ip_or_cidr, strict=False)
                return host_net.subnet_of(network) or network.subnet_of(host_net)
            else:
                host = ipaddress.ip_address(ip_or_cidr)
                return host in network
        except ValueError:
            return ip_or_cidr == rule_src


class FirewallRuleAnalyzer:
    """Analyzes firewall rulesets for security issues."""

    SENSITIVE_PORTS = {
        22: "SSH", 23: "Telnet", 3389: "RDP", 5985: "WinRM-HTTP",
        5986: "WinRM-HTTPS", 445: "SMB", 135: "RPC", 139: "NetBIOS",
        1433: "MSSQL", 3306: "MySQL", 5432: "PostgreSQL", 27017: "MongoDB",
        6379: "Redis", 9200: "Elasticsearch", 2181: "Zookeeper",
        4444: "Metasploit", 1337: "C2",
    }

    def analyze(self, rules: List[FirewallRule]) -> List[SegmentationFinding]:
        findings = []
        findings.extend(self._check_any_any(rules))
        findings.extend(self._check_sensitive_port_exposure(rules))
        findings.extend(self._check_telnet_ftp(rules))
        findings.extend(self._check_shadowed_deny(rules))
        findings.extend(self._check_bidirectional_sensitive(rules))
        return findings

    def _check_any_any(self, rules: List[FirewallRule]) -> List[SegmentationFinding]:
        findings = []
        for r in rules:
            if (r.action == PolicyAction.ALLOW and
                r.source in ("any", "0.0.0.0/0", "*") and
                r.destination in ("any", "0.0.0.0/0", "*") and
                r.port in ("any", "*", "0-65535")):
                findings.append(SegmentationFinding(
                    severity="CRITICAL",
                    finding_type="ANY_ANY_ALLOW",
                    source_segment=r.source,
                    destination_segment=r.destination,
                    description=f"Rule {r.rule_id}: ALLOW any->any on any port",
                    attack_risk="Full network access; no segmentation",
                    remediation="Replace with explicit allow rules per service",
                ))
        return findings

    def _check_sensitive_port_exposure(self, rules: List[FirewallRule]) -> List[SegmentationFinding]:
        findings = []
        for r in rules:
            if r.action != PolicyAction.ALLOW:
                continue
            if r.source in ("any", "0.0.0.0/0", "*"):
                port = self._parse_port(r.port)
                if port and port in self.SENSITIVE_PORTS:
                    svc = self.SENSITIVE_PORTS[port]
                    findings.append(SegmentationFinding(
                        severity="HIGH",
                        finding_type="SENSITIVE_PORT_EXPOSED",
                        source_segment="any",
                        destination_segment=r.destination,
                        description=f"Rule {r.rule_id}: {svc} (port {port}) exposed to any source",
                        attack_risk=f"Brute force / exploitation of {svc}",
                        remediation=f"Restrict {svc} access to management segment only",
                    ))
        return findings

    def _check_telnet_ftp(self, rules: List[FirewallRule]) -> List[SegmentationFinding]:
        findings = []
        cleartext_ports = {21: "FTP", 23: "Telnet", 80: "HTTP", 143: "IMAP", 25: "SMTP"}
        for r in rules:
            if r.action != PolicyAction.ALLOW:
                continue
            port = self._parse_port(r.port)
            if port in {21, 23}:
                findings.append(SegmentationFinding(
                    severity="HIGH",
                    finding_type="CLEARTEXT_PROTOCOL_ALLOWED",
                    source_segment=r.source,
                    destination_segment=r.destination,
                    description=f"Rule {r.rule_id}: {cleartext_ports.get(port, port)} allowed (unencrypted)",
                    attack_risk="Credential interception via MITM",
                    remediation=f"Replace with encrypted alternative (SSH/SFTP/HTTPS)",
                ))
        return findings

    def _check_shadowed_deny(self, rules: List[FirewallRule]) -> List[SegmentationFinding]:
        """Detect DENY rules shadowed by earlier ALLOW rules."""
        findings = []
        sorted_rules = sorted(rules, key=lambda r: r.priority)
        for i, deny_rule in enumerate(sorted_rules):
            if deny_rule.action != PolicyAction.DENY:
                continue
            for allow_rule in sorted_rules[:i]:
                if (allow_rule.action == PolicyAction.ALLOW and
                    allow_rule.source in ("any", deny_rule.source) and
                    allow_rule.destination in ("any", deny_rule.destination) and
                    allow_rule.port in ("any", deny_rule.port)):
                    findings.append(SegmentationFinding(
                        severity="MEDIUM",
                        finding_type="SHADOWED_DENY_RULE",
                        source_segment=deny_rule.source,
                        destination_segment=deny_rule.destination,
                        description=f"Rule {deny_rule.rule_id} (DENY) is shadowed by {allow_rule.rule_id} (ALLOW)",
                        attack_risk="Intended block is bypassed by earlier allow rule",
                        remediation="Reorder rules: move DENY before the conflicting ALLOW",
                    ))
        return findings

    def _check_bidirectional_sensitive(self, rules: List[FirewallRule]) -> List[SegmentationFinding]:
        """Find bi-directional access to critical segments."""
        findings = []
        allow_rules = [r for r in rules if r.action == PolicyAction.ALLOW]
        for r1 in allow_rules:
            for r2 in allow_rules:
                if (r1.rule_id != r2.rule_id and
                    r1.source == r2.destination and
                    r1.destination == r2.source and
                    r1.port == r2.port):
                    if any(z in r1.destination for z in ["ot", "scada", "critical", "prod"]):
                        findings.append(SegmentationFinding(
                            severity="HIGH",
                            finding_type="BIDIRECTIONAL_CRITICAL_ACCESS",
                            source_segment=r1.source,
                            destination_segment=r1.destination,
                            description=f"Bidirectional access between {r1.source} and {r1.destination}",
                            attack_risk="Lateral movement in both directions",
                            remediation="Implement unidirectional data diode for critical segments",
                        ))
        return findings

    def _parse_port(self, port_str: str) -> Optional[int]:
        try:
            return int(port_str)
        except (ValueError, TypeError):
            return None


class ZeroTrustPolicyGenerator:
    """Generates zero-trust microsegmentation policy recommendations."""

    SEGMENT_COMMUNICATION_MATRIX = {
        # (source_zone, dest_zone): [(port, protocol, justification)]
        ("external", "dmz"): [(443, "tcp", "HTTPS to web services"), (80, "tcp", "HTTP redirect")],
        ("dmz", "internal"): [(8080, "tcp", "App tier only"), (5432, "tcp", "DB from app tier")],
        ("internal", "management"): [(443, "tcp", "Mgmt APIs")],
        ("management", "internal"): [(22, "tcp", "SSH admin"), (3389, "tcp", "RDP admin")],
        ("management", "ot"): [(102, "tcp", "S7 PLC"), (502, "tcp", "Modbus")],
    }

    def generate_policy(self, segments: List[NetworkSegment]) -> List[Dict]:
        rules = []
        rule_num = 1000
        # Default deny all
        rules.append({"rule_id": f"R{rule_num}", "priority": rule_num,
                      "action": "deny", "source": "any", "destination": "any",
                      "port": "any", "description": "Default deny all"})
        rule_num -= 10
        # Explicit allows from matrix
        for (src_zone, dst_zone), allowed in self.SEGMENT_COMMUNICATION_MATRIX.items():
            src_segs = [s for s in segments if s.zone == src_zone]
            dst_segs = [s for s in segments if s.zone == dst_zone]
            for src in src_segs:
                for dst in dst_segs:
                    for port, proto, justification in allowed:
                        rules.append({
                            "rule_id": f"R{rule_num}",
                            "priority": rule_num,
                            "action": "allow",
                            "source": src.cidr,
                            "destination": dst.cidr,
                            "port": str(port),
                            "protocol": proto,
                            "description": f"ZT: {src.name} -> {dst.name}: {justification}"
                        })
                        rule_num -= 1
        # Allow management to reach all for SSH/RDP (only from mgmt segment)
        mgmt_segs = [s for s in segments if s.zone == "management"]
        for seg in segments:
            if seg.zone != "management":
                for mgmt in mgmt_segs:
                    rules.append({
                        "rule_id": f"R{rule_num}",
                        "priority": rule_num,
                        "action": "allow",
                        "source": mgmt.cidr,
                        "destination": seg.cidr,
                        "port": "22",
                        "protocol": "tcp",
                        "description": f"ZT: SSH from mgmt to {seg.name}"
                    })
                    rule_num -= 1
        return sorted(rules, key=lambda r: -r["priority"])

    def lateral_movement_paths(self, segments: List[NetworkSegment],
                                rules: List[FirewallRule]) -> List[Dict]:
        """Enumerate potential lateral movement paths through rule analysis."""
        paths = []
        for src_seg in segments:
            for dst_seg in segments:
                if src_seg.name == dst_seg.name:
                    continue
                reachable = self._can_reach(src_seg.cidr, dst_seg.cidr, rules)
                if reachable and dst_seg.sensitivity in ("critical", "high"):
                    paths.append({
                        "from": src_seg.name,
                        "to": dst_seg.name,
                        "sensitivity": dst_seg.sensitivity,
                        "risk": "HIGH" if dst_seg.sensitivity == "critical" else "MEDIUM",
                        "allowed_ports": reachable,
                    })
        return paths

    def _can_reach(self, src: str, dst: str, rules: List[FirewallRule]) -> List[str]:
        matcher = CIDRMatcher()
        allowed_ports = []
        sorted_rules = sorted(rules, key=lambda r: r.priority)
        for r in sorted_rules:
            if matcher.matches(src, r.source) and matcher.matches(dst, r.destination):
                if r.action == PolicyAction.ALLOW:
                    allowed_ports.append(r.port)
                elif r.action == PolicyAction.DENY:
                    break
        return allowed_ports


if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Define network segments
    segments = [
        NetworkSegment("web-dmz", "10.0.1.0/24", "dmz", "medium"),
        NetworkSegment("app-tier", "10.0.2.0/24", "internal", "high"),
        NetworkSegment("db-tier", "10.0.3.0/24", "internal", "critical"),
        NetworkSegment("management", "10.0.10.0/24", "management", "critical"),
        NetworkSegment("ot-network", "10.0.20.0/24", "ot", "critical"),
    ]
    # Overly permissive existing rules
    existing_rules = [
        FirewallRule("R1", 100, "any", "any", "any", "any", PolicyAction.ALLOW, "Legacy any-any"),
        FirewallRule("R2", 200, "any", "10.0.3.0/24", "1433", "tcp", PolicyAction.ALLOW, "DB from anywhere"),
        FirewallRule("R3", 300, "any", "10.0.3.0/24", "3389", "tcp", PolicyAction.ALLOW, "RDP to DB"),
        FirewallRule("R4", 400, "10.0.1.0/24", "10.0.3.0/24", "22", "tcp", PolicyAction.ALLOW),
        FirewallRule("R5", 500, "any", "any", "23", "tcp", PolicyAction.ALLOW, "Telnet"),
        FirewallRule("R6", 600, "10.0.3.0/24", "10.0.3.0/24", "22", "tcp", PolicyAction.DENY),
    ]
    analyzer = FirewallRuleAnalyzer()
    findings = analyzer.analyze(existing_rules)
    print(f"Rule Analysis: {len(findings)} findings")
    for f in findings:
        print(f"  [{f.severity}] {f.finding_type}: {f.description}")
        print(f"    Risk: {f.attack_risk}")
        print(f"    Fix: {f.remediation}")
    zt_gen = ZeroTrustPolicyGenerator()
    paths = zt_gen.lateral_movement_paths(segments, existing_rules)
    print(f"\nLateral movement paths: {len(paths)}")
    for p in paths:
        print(f"  {p['from']} -> {p['to']} [{p['sensitivity']}] via ports: {p['allowed_ports']}")
    zt_rules = zt_gen.generate_policy(segments)
    print(f"\nGenerated {len(zt_rules)} zero-trust rules")
    for r in zt_rules[:5]:
        print(f"  [{r['priority']}] {r['action'].upper()} {r.get('source','?')} -> {r.get('destination','?')}:{r.get('port','?')} | {r['description']}")
```

instruction: Build a privileged access management (PAM) analytics system that monitors privileged account activity, detects session anomalies, and flags unauthorized administrative actions using behavioral baselining.
output:
```python
import numpy as np
import json
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import List, Dict, Optional, Tuple
from collections import defaultdict
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from enum import Enum
import statistics

class SessionRisk(Enum):
    CRITICAL = "CRITICAL"
    HIGH = "HIGH"
    MEDIUM = "MEDIUM"
    LOW = "LOW"
    NORMAL = "NORMAL"

@dataclass
class PrivilegedSession:
    session_id: str
    username: str
    role: str                  # root, admin, DBA, sysadmin
    source_ip: str
    target_host: str
    start_time: datetime
    end_time: Optional[datetime]
    commands: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    data_accessed_mb: float = 0.0
    files_modified: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    authentication_method: str = "password"
    mfa_used: bool = False

@dataclass
class SessionAnomaly:
    session_id: str
    username: str
    anomaly_type: str
    risk: SessionRisk
    score: float
    description: str
    evidence: List[str]
    recommended_action: str


# Dangerous command categories
DANGEROUS_COMMANDS = {
    "credential_access": [
        "cat /etc/shadow", "cat /etc/passwd", "dump", "hashdump",
        "mimikatz", "procdump", "lsass", "secretsdump",
    ],
    "persistence": [
        "crontab", "systemctl enable", "chkconfig", "rc.local",
        "visudo", "usermod -aG sudo", "adduser", "useradd",
        "sshd_config", "authorized_keys",
    ],
    "data_exfiltration": [
        "scp", "rsync", "curl -T", "wget --post", "nc ", "netcat",
        "tar czf", "zip -r", "7z a", "base64 -",
    ],
    "log_tampering": [
        "history -c", "shred", "rm -rf /var/log",
        "truncate", "echo '' > /var/log",
        "unset HISTFILE", "HISTSIZE=0",
    ],
    "network_recon": [
        "nmap", "masscan", "netstat -an", "ss -an",
        "arp -a", "route -n", "tcpdump", "wireshark",
    ],
    "privilege_escalation": [
        "sudo su", "sudo -i", "sudo bash", "chmod 4755",
        "chown root", "setuid", "capability",
    ],
}

# Time-based risk factors
OFF_HOURS_START = 20  # 8 PM
OFF_HOURS_END = 6    # 6 AM
WEEKEND_DAYS = {5, 6}  # Saturday, Sunday


class PrivilegedAccountBaseliner:
    """
    Builds behavioral baselines for privileged accounts.
    Tracks: typical login hours, session duration, source IPs,
    typical commands, data volumes.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        self.baselines: Dict[str, Dict] = {}  # username -> baseline
        self._history: Dict[str, List[PrivilegedSession]] = defaultdict(list)

    def add_session(self, session: PrivilegedSession):
        self._history[session.username].append(session)

    def build_baseline(self, username: str) -> Dict:
        sessions = self._history.get(username, [])
        if not sessions:
            return {}
        durations = []
        for s in sessions:
            if s.end_time:
                dur = (s.end_time - s.start_time).total_seconds()
                durations.append(dur)
        hours = [s.start_time.hour for s in sessions]
        data_mb = [s.data_accessed_mb for s in sessions]
        source_ips = [s.source_ip for s in sessions]
        cmd_counts = [len(s.commands) for s in sessions]
        baseline = {
            "username": username,
            "typical_login_hours": {
                "mean": statistics.mean(hours) if hours else 12,
                "std": statistics.stdev(hours) if len(hours) > 1 else 2,
            },
            "typical_session_duration_sec": {
                "mean": statistics.mean(durations) if durations else 1800,
                "std": statistics.stdev(durations) if len(durations) > 1 else 600,
                "max": max(durations) if durations else 3600,
            },
            "typical_data_access_mb": {
                "mean": statistics.mean(data_mb) if data_mb else 10,
                "std": statistics.stdev(data_mb) if len(data_mb) > 1 else 5,
            },
            "known_source_ips": list(set(source_ips)),
            "typical_cmd_count": {
                "mean": statistics.mean(cmd_counts) if cmd_counts else 10,
                "std": statistics.stdev(cmd_counts) if len(cmd_counts) > 1 else 5,
            },
            "session_count": len(sessions),
        }
        self.baselines[username] = baseline
        return baseline

    def get_baseline(self, username: str) -> Optional[Dict]:
        return self.baselines.get(username)


class PAMSessionAnalyzer:
    """
    Real-time privileged session analysis.
    Detects anomalies vs behavioral baseline.
    Flags dangerous commands and policy violations.
    """
    def __init__(self, baseliner: PrivilegedAccountBaseliner):
        self.baseliner = baseliner
        self.alerts: List[SessionAnomaly] = []

    def analyze_session(self, session: PrivilegedSession) -> List[SessionAnomaly]:
        anomalies = []
        baseline = self.baseliner.get_baseline(session.username)
        # Check 1: Off-hours access
        hour = session.start_time.hour
        dow = session.start_time.weekday()
        if hour >= OFF_HOURS_START or hour < OFF_HOURS_END or dow in WEEKEND_DAYS:
            risk = SessionRisk.HIGH
            anomalies.append(SessionAnomaly(
                session_id=session.session_id,
                username=session.username,
                anomaly_type="OFF_HOURS_ACCESS",
                risk=risk,
                score=70.0,
                description=f"Privileged access at {session.start_time.strftime('%H:%M')} (off-hours)",
                evidence=[f"Login at {session.start_time}", f"Weekday: {dow}"],
                recommended_action="Verify with user via out-of-band channel; check for ticket/change request",
            ))
        # Check 2: Unknown source IP
        if baseline and session.source_ip not in baseline.get("known_source_ips", []):
            anomalies.append(SessionAnomaly(
                session_id=session.session_id,
                username=session.username,
                anomaly_type="NEW_SOURCE_IP",
                risk=SessionRisk.HIGH,
                score=65.0,
                description=f"Login from unknown IP: {session.source_ip}",
                evidence=[f"Known IPs: {baseline['known_source_ips']}",
                          f"Current: {session.source_ip}"],
                recommended_action="Verify user initiated session; check for VPN/proxy",
            ))
        # Check 3: No MFA for privileged role
        if session.role in ("root", "admin", "DBA") and not session.mfa_used:
            anomalies.append(SessionAnomaly(
                session_id=session.session_id,
                username=session.username,
                anomaly_type="NO_MFA_PRIVILEGED",
                risk=SessionRisk.CRITICAL,
                score=90.0,
                description=f"Privileged role '{session.role}' accessed without MFA",
                evidence=[f"MFA: {session.mfa_used}", f"Auth method: {session.authentication_method}"],
                recommended_action="Enforce MFA for all privileged accounts; terminate session if policy violated",
            ))
        # Check 4: Dangerous commands
        dangerous_found = defaultdict(list)
        for cmd in session.commands:
            cmd_lower = cmd.lower()
            for category, patterns in DANGEROUS_COMMANDS.items():
                for pattern in patterns:
                    if pattern in cmd_lower:
                        dangerous_found[category].append(cmd)
        for category, cmds in dangerous_found.items():
            risk_map = {
                "credential_access": SessionRisk.CRITICAL,
                "log_tampering": SessionRisk.CRITICAL,
                "data_exfiltration": SessionRisk.HIGH,
                "persistence": SessionRisk.HIGH,
                "privilege_escalation": SessionRisk.CRITICAL,
                "network_recon": SessionRisk.MEDIUM,
            }
            anomalies.append(SessionAnomaly(
                session_id=session.session_id,
                username=session.username,
                anomaly_type=f"DANGEROUS_CMD_{category.upper()}",
                risk=risk_map.get(category, SessionRisk.HIGH),
                score=80.0 if risk_map.get(category) == SessionRisk.CRITICAL else 60.0,
                description=f"Dangerous {category.replace('_', ' ')} commands executed",
                evidence=cmds[:3],
                recommended_action=f"Review session recording; escalate to security team for {category}",
            ))
        # Check 5: Unusual data volume
        if baseline:
            mean_mb = baseline["typical_data_access_mb"]["mean"]
            std_mb = baseline["typical_data_access_mb"]["std"]
            z_score = (session.data_accessed_mb - mean_mb) / max(std_mb, 0.1)
            if z_score > 3:
                anomalies.append(SessionAnomaly(
                    session_id=session.session_id,
                    username=session.username,
                    anomaly_type="UNUSUAL_DATA_VOLUME",
                    risk=SessionRisk.HIGH,
                    score=min(95, 50 + z_score * 5),
                    description=f"Data access {session.data_accessed_mb:.0f}MB vs baseline {mean_mb:.0f}MB",
                    evidence=[f"Z-score: {z_score:.1f}", f"Accessed: {session.data_accessed_mb}MB"],
                    recommended_action="Investigate data access patterns; check for exfiltration",
                ))
        # Check 6: Session duration anomaly
        if session.end_time and baseline:
            duration = (session.end_time - session.start_time).total_seconds()
            mean_dur = baseline["typical_session_duration_sec"]["mean"]
            std_dur = baseline["typical_session_duration_sec"]["std"]
            if duration > mean_dur + 3 * std_dur:
                anomalies.append(SessionAnomaly(
                    session_id=session.session_id,
                    username=session.username,
                    anomaly_type="UNUSUALLY_LONG_SESSION",
                    risk=SessionRisk.MEDIUM,
                    score=45.0,
                    description=f"Session lasted {duration/3600:.1f}h vs typical {mean_dur/3600:.1f}h",
                    evidence=[f"Duration: {duration:.0f}s", f"Baseline: {mean_dur:.0f}s"],
                    recommended_action="Review session recording for unusual activity",
                ))
        self.alerts.extend(anomalies)
        return anomalies

    def risk_dashboard(self) -> Dict:
        by_risk = defaultdict(int)
        by_user = defaultdict(list)
        by_type = defaultdict(int)
        for a in self.alerts:
            by_risk[a.risk.value] += 1
            by_user[a.username].append(a.anomaly_type)
            by_type[a.anomaly_type] += 1
        top_risky_users = sorted(
            [(u, len(types)) for u, types in by_user.items()],
            key=lambda x: -x[1]
        )[:5]
        return {
            "total_alerts": len(self.alerts),
            "by_risk_level": dict(by_risk),
            "top_anomaly_types": dict(sorted(by_type.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1])[:5]),
            "highest_risk_users": [{"user": u, "alerts": n} for u, n in top_risky_users],
        }


if __name__ == "__main__":
    baseliner = PrivilegedAccountBaseliner()
    # Build baseline from historical sessions
    for i in range(30):
        hist = PrivilegedSession(
            session_id=f"hist_{i}", username="db_admin", role="DBA",
            source_ip="10.0.10.50", target_host="db-prod-01",
            start_time=datetime.now().replace(hour=10, minute=0),
            end_time=datetime.now().replace(hour=11, minute=0),
            commands=["SELECT", "EXPLAIN", "SHOW TABLES"],
            data_accessed_mb=50.0, mfa_used=True, authentication_method="cert+mfa",
        )
        baseliner.add_session(hist)
    baseliner.build_baseline("db_admin")
    analyzer = PAMSessionAnalyzer(baseliner)
    # Suspicious session: off-hours, dangerous commands, large data, unknown IP
    suspicious = PrivilegedSession(
        session_id="SES-20240115-001", username="db_admin", role="DBA",
        source_ip="185.220.101.45",  # Unknown external IP
        target_host="db-prod-01",
        start_time=datetime.now().replace(hour=2, minute=30),
        end_time=datetime.now().replace(hour=5, minute=0),
        commands=[
            "SELECT * FROM users",
            "cat /etc/shadow",
            "history -c",
            "scp /data/customers.csv user@external.com:/tmp/",
            "unset HISTFILE",
            "tar czf /tmp/backup.tar.gz /data/",
        ],
        data_accessed_mb=5000.0,  # 5GB - massive
        mfa_used=False,
        authentication_method="password",
    )
    alerts = analyzer.analyze_session(suspicious)
    print(f"Alerts for session {suspicious.session_id}: {len(alerts)}")
    for a in alerts:
        print(f"  [{a.risk.value}] {a.anomaly_type}: {a.description}")
        print(f"    Evidence: {a.evidence[:2]}")
        print(f"    Action: {a.recommended_action}")
    print(f"\nPAM Dashboard: {json.dumps(analyzer.risk_dashboard(), indent=2)}")
```

instruction: Implement a blockchain transaction anomaly detector for identifying suspicious patterns in cryptocurrency transactions such as mixing, layering, and unusual wallet clustering.
output:
```python
import numpy as np
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import List, Dict, Set, Optional
from collections import defaultdict
from datetime import datetime
import hashlib

@dataclass
class Transaction:
    tx_hash: str
    sender: str
    receiver: str
    amount_usd: float
    timestamp: datetime
    fee_usd: float = 0.0
    block_height: int = 0
    is_coinbase: bool = False

@dataclass
class WalletCluster:
    cluster_id: str
    wallets: Set[str]
    total_volume_usd: float
    risk_score: float
    risk_flags: List[str]

@dataclass
class ChainAlert:
    alert_id: str
    alert_type: str
    severity: str
    wallets_involved: List[str]
    transaction_hashes: List[str]
    description: str
    risk_score: float
    recommended_action: str


class WalletClusteringEngine:
    """
    Groups wallets into clusters using common-input ownership heuristic
    and co-spend analysis. Wallets that spend together likely belong
    to the same entity.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        self._parent: Dict[str, str] = {}

    def _find(self, x: str) -> str:
        if self._parent.get(x, x) != x:
            self._parent[x] = self._find(self._parent[x])
        return self._parent.get(x, x)

    def _union(self, a: str, b: str):
        ra, rb = self._find(a), self._find(b)
        if ra != rb:
            self._parent[ra] = rb

    def cluster_by_co_spend(self, multi_input_txs: List[List[str]]):
        """If wallets appear as co-inputs in a transaction, union them."""
        for inputs in multi_input_txs:
            for i in range(1, len(inputs)):
                self._union(inputs[0], inputs[i])

    def get_clusters(self, wallets: List[str]) -> Dict[str, Set[str]]:
        clusters = defaultdict(set)
        for w in wallets:
            root = self._find(w)
            clusters[root].add(w)
        return dict(clusters)


class BlockchainAnomalyDetector:
    """
    Detects suspicious cryptocurrency transaction patterns:
    - Mixing/tumbling (structuring through many small txs)
    - Rapid layering (quick hops between wallets)
    - Peel chains (one input, two outputs - one change)
    - Round-amount transactions (possible structuring)
    - Concentration/dispersion patterns
    """
    def __init__(self):
        self.txs: List[Transaction] = []
        self.wallet_graph: Dict[str, List[str]] = defaultdict(list)  # sender -> receivers
        self.wallet_volume: Dict[str, float] = defaultdict(float)
        self.clusterer = WalletClusteringEngine()
        self.alerts: List[ChainAlert] = []
        self._alert_counter = 0

    def ingest(self, transactions: List[Transaction]):
        self.txs.extend(transactions)
        for tx in transactions:
            self.wallet_graph[tx.sender].append(tx.receiver)
            self.wallet_volume[tx.sender] += tx.amount_usd
            self.wallet_volume[tx.receiver] += tx.amount_usd

    def _new_alert(self, alert_type, severity, wallets, txs, desc, score, action) -> ChainAlert:
        self._alert_counter += 1
        return ChainAlert(
            alert_id=f"CHAIN-{self._alert_counter:05d}",
            alert_type=alert_type, severity=severity,
            wallets_involved=wallets[:10],
            transaction_hashes=[t for t in txs[:5]],
            description=desc, risk_score=score,
            recommended_action=action,
        )

    def detect_rapid_layering(self, max_hop_seconds: int = 3600) -> List[ChainAlert]:
        """Detect funds moving through many hops in short time (layering)."""
        alerts = []
        tx_by_sender = defaultdict(list)
        for tx in self.txs:
            tx_by_sender[tx.sender].append(tx)
        # Find wallets that both receive and quickly send
        for tx in self.txs:
            outgoing = tx_by_sender.get(tx.receiver, [])
            quick_outs = [
                t for t in outgoing
                if abs((t.timestamp - tx.timestamp).total_seconds()) < max_hop_seconds
            ]
            if len(quick_outs) >= 2:
                alerts.append(self._new_alert(
                    "RAPID_LAYERING", "HIGH",
                    [tx.receiver], [tx.tx_hash] + [t.tx_hash for t in quick_outs],
                    f"Wallet {tx.receiver[:12]}... receives and re-sends within {max_hop_seconds//60}min ({len(quick_outs)} times)",
                    75.0, "Trace fund flow; check for mixing service",
                ))
        return alerts

    def detect_structuring(self, threshold_usd: float = 10000,
                            window_hours: int = 24) -> List[ChainAlert]:
        """Detect smurfing: many small transactions just below reporting threshold."""
        alerts = []
        tx_by_sender = defaultdict(list)
        for tx in self.txs:
            tx_by_sender[tx.sender].append(tx)
        for wallet, wallet_txs in tx_by_sender.items():
            sorted_txs = sorted(wallet_txs, key=lambda t: t.timestamp)
            for i, tx in enumerate(sorted_txs):
                window_end = tx.timestamp + timedelta(hours=window_hours)
                window_txs = [t for t in sorted_txs[i:]
                              if t.timestamp <= window_end]
                just_below = [t for t in window_txs
                              if threshold_usd * 0.7 <= t.amount_usd < threshold_usd]
                if len(just_below) >= 3:
                    total = sum(t.amount_usd for t in just_below)
                    alerts.append(self._new_alert(
                        "STRUCTURING", "CRITICAL",
                        [wallet], [t.tx_hash for t in just_below],
                        f"Wallet {wallet[:12]}... sent {len(just_below)} txs just below ${threshold_usd:,} (total: ${total:,.0f})",
                        90.0, "File Suspicious Activity Report (SAR); escalate to compliance",
                    ))
                    break
        return alerts

    def detect_mixing_pattern(self, min_fan_out: int = 5) -> List[ChainAlert]:
        """Detect wallet receiving from many sources and sending to many destinations (mixing)."""
        alerts = []
        inbound: Dict[str, Set[str]] = defaultdict(set)
        outbound: Dict[str, Set[str]] = defaultdict(set)
        for tx in self.txs:
            inbound[tx.receiver].add(tx.sender)
            outbound[tx.sender].add(tx.receiver)
        for wallet in set(inbound) & set(outbound):
            n_in = len(inbound[wallet])
            n_out = len(outbound[wallet])
            if n_in >= min_fan_out and n_out >= min_fan_out:
                relevant_txs = [t.tx_hash for t in self.txs
                                if t.sender == wallet or t.receiver == wallet][:5]
                alerts.append(self._new_alert(
                    "MIXING_PATTERN", "HIGH",
                    [wallet], relevant_txs,
                    f"Wallet {wallet[:12]}... acts as mixer: {n_in} inputs, {n_out} outputs",
                    80.0, "Investigate as possible mixing service; check blacklists",
                ))
        return alerts

    def detect_darknet_market_patterns(self) -> List[ChainAlert]:
        """Detect round amounts and fee patterns common in darknet purchases."""
        alerts = []
        round_txs = [t for t in self.txs
                     if t.amount_usd % 100 == 0 and 50 <= t.amount_usd <= 5000]
        if len(round_txs) > 20:
            wallets = list(set([t.sender for t in round_txs[:20]]))
            alerts.append(self._new_alert(
                "ROUND_AMOUNT_PATTERN", "MEDIUM",
                wallets, [t.tx_hash for t in round_txs[:5]],
                f"{len(round_txs)} transactions with round USD amounts - possible darknet activity",
                55.0, "Cross-reference wallets with known darknet market addresses",
            ))
        return alerts

    def detect_all(self) -> List[ChainAlert]:
        from datetime import timedelta
        all_alerts = []
        all_alerts.extend(self.detect_rapid_layering())
        all_alerts.extend(self.detect_structuring())
        all_alerts.extend(self.detect_mixing_pattern())
        all_alerts.extend(self.detect_darknet_market_patterns())
        all_alerts.sort(key=lambda a: -a.risk_score)
        self.alerts = all_alerts
        return all_alerts

    def risk_summary(self) -> Dict:
        if not self.alerts:
            return {"total_alerts": 0}
        by_sev = defaultdict(int)
        for a in self.alerts:
            by_sev[a.severity] += 1
        return {
            "total_alerts": len(self.alerts),
            "by_severity": dict(by_sev),
            "top_alerts": [
                {"type": a.alert_type, "severity": a.severity,
                 "score": a.risk_score, "wallets": a.wallets_involved[:2]}
                for a in self.alerts[:5]
            ],
        }


if __name__ == "__main__":
    from datetime import timedelta
    base_time = datetime(2024, 1, 15, 10, 0)
    transactions = [
        # Rapid layering: wallet B receives then immediately re-sends
        Transaction("tx001", "wallet_A", "wallet_B", 5000, base_time),
        Transaction("tx002", "wallet_B", "wallet_C", 2400, base_time + timedelta(minutes=10)),
        Transaction("tx003", "wallet_B", "wallet_D", 2400, base_time + timedelta(minutes=15)),
        # Structuring: multiple txs just below $10k
        Transaction("tx010", "wallet_E", "wallet_F", 9800, base_time),
        Transaction("tx011", "wallet_E", "wallet_G", 9750, base_time + timedelta(hours=2)),
        Transaction("tx012", "wallet_E", "wallet_H", 9900, base_time + timedelta(hours=5)),
        Transaction("tx013", "wallet_E", "wallet_I", 9600, base_time + timedelta(hours=8)),
        # Mixing: wallet_M receives from many, sends to many
        *[Transaction(f"tx{20+i}", f"src_{i}", "wallet_M", 1000, base_time) for i in range(8)],
        *[Transaction(f"tx{30+i}", "wallet_M", f"dst_{i}", 990, base_time + timedelta(hours=1)) for i in range(7)],
        # Round amounts
        *[Transaction(f"tx{50+i}", f"buyer_{i}", "shop_wallet", float(i+1)*100,
                      base_time + timedelta(hours=i)) for i in range(25)],
    ]
    detector = BlockchainAnomalyDetector()
    detector.ingest(transactions)
    alerts = detector.detect_all()
    print(f"Total alerts: {len(alerts)}")
    for a in alerts:
        print(f"  [{a.severity}] {a.alert_type} (score={a.risk_score:.0f})")
        print(f"    {a.description}")
        print(f"    Action: {a.recommended_action}")
    print(f"\nRisk Summary: {json.dumps(detector.risk_summary(), indent=2)}")
```

instruction: Build a security chaos engineering framework that automatically injects controlled failures and attacks into staging environments to test security control resilience and incident response procedures.
output:
```python
import json
import random
import time
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import List, Dict, Optional, Callable
from enum import Enum
from datetime import datetime

class ExperimentStatus(Enum):
    PENDING = "pending"
    RUNNING = "running"
    COMPLETED = "completed"
    ABORTED = "aborted"
    FAILED = "failed"

class AttackCategory(Enum):
    NETWORK = "network"
    CREDENTIAL = "credential"
    PROCESS = "process"
    DATA = "data"
    AVAILABILITY = "availability"

@dataclass
class ChaosExperiment:
    experiment_id: str
    name: str
    category: AttackCategory
    description: str
    target: str          # service/host to target
    duration_sec: int
    hypothesis: str      # what should be detected/blocked
    blast_radius: str    # "isolated" | "service" | "zone"
    severity: str        # low, medium, high
    simulate_only: bool = True  # safety flag
    preconditions: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    abort_conditions: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)

@dataclass
class ExperimentResult:
    experiment_id: str
    status: ExperimentStatus
    start_time: str
    end_time: str
    detection_time_sec: Optional[float]   # how long to detect
    alert_fired: bool
    response_triggered: bool
    findings: List[str]
    resilience_score: float  # 0-100
    recommendation: str


class SteadyStateVerifier:
    """
    Verifies system is in steady state before and after experiment.
    Checks: service health, error rates, latency.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        self._metrics: Dict[str, float] = {
            "error_rate": 0.01, "latency_p99_ms": 150,
            "availability": 99.9, "active_alerts": 0,
        }

    def check(self, target: str) -> Dict:
        # Simulate steady state check
        return {
            "healthy": True,
            "error_rate": self._metrics["error_rate"],
            "latency_p99_ms": self._metrics["latency_p99_ms"],
            "availability": self._metrics["availability"],
            "active_alerts": int(self._metrics["active_alerts"]),
        }

    def is_healthy(self, target: str) -> bool:
        state = self.check(target)
        return state["healthy"] and state["error_rate"] < 0.05


class SecurityControlValidator:
    """
    Validates that security controls are responding during chaos experiments.
    Checks: SIEM alerts, WAF blocks, EDR detections, IDS alerts.
    """
    def __init__(self):
        self._detection_registry: Dict[str, List[str]] = {}

    def register_detection(self, experiment_id: str, control: str, detail: str):
        if experiment_id not in self._detection_registry:
            self._detection_registry[experiment_id] = []
        self._detection_registry[experiment_id].append(f"{control}: {detail}")

    def get_detections(self, experiment_id: str) -> List[str]:
        return self._detection_registry.get(experiment_id, [])

    def simulate_detection(self, experiment: ChaosExperiment) -> Dict:
        """Simulate whether security controls would detect the attack."""
        # Base detection probability by category
        detect_prob = {
            AttackCategory.NETWORK: 0.85,
            AttackCategory.CREDENTIAL: 0.70,
            AttackCategory.PROCESS: 0.75,
            AttackCategory.DATA: 0.60,
            AttackCategory.AVAILABILITY: 0.90,
        }
        prob = detect_prob.get(experiment.category, 0.7)
        detected = random.random() < prob
        time_to_detect = random.uniform(30, 600) if detected else None
        controls_fired = []
        if detected:
            if experiment.category == AttackCategory.NETWORK:
                controls_fired.extend(["IDS alert", "NetFlow anomaly"])
            elif experiment.category == AttackCategory.CREDENTIAL:
                controls_fired.extend(["SIEM correlation", "Failed login alert"])
            elif experiment.category == AttackCategory.PROCESS:
                controls_fired.extend(["EDR detection", "Process monitoring alert"])
            elif experiment.category == AttackCategory.DATA:
                controls_fired.extend(["DLP alert", "Data access monitoring"])
            elif experiment.category == AttackCategory.AVAILABILITY:
                controls_fired.extend(["Uptime monitor", "SLA alert"])
        return {
            "detected": detected,
            "time_to_detect_sec": time_to_detect,
            "controls_fired": controls_fired,
            "response_triggered": detected and random.random() < 0.8,
        }


class SecurityChaosEngine:
    """
    Orchestrates security chaos engineering experiments.
    Runs controlled attack simulations to test security control effectiveness.
    All experiments are simulation-only by default (simulate_only=True).
    """

    # Experiment library: pre-built security chaos experiments
    EXPERIMENT_LIBRARY = [
        ChaosExperiment(
            "SEC-CHX-001", "Credential Stuffing Simulation",
            AttackCategory.CREDENTIAL,
            "Simulate high-volume login attempts with known breached credentials",
            "auth-service", 300,
            hypothesis="Account lockout and SIEM alert should trigger within 60 seconds",
            blast_radius="isolated", severity="medium",
            preconditions=["Test account created", "Monitoring active"],
            abort_conditions=["Production traffic impacted", "Real account locked"],
        ),
        ChaosExperiment(
            "SEC-CHX-002", "Lateral Movement via SMB",
            AttackCategory.NETWORK,
            "Simulate SMB enumeration and lateral movement attempt",
            "internal-network", 120,
            hypothesis="IDS/IPS should detect SMB scanning within 30 seconds",
            blast_radius="isolated", severity="high",
            preconditions=["Network isolation verified", "Honeypot active"],
            abort_conditions=["Traffic reaches production"],
        ),
        ChaosExperiment(
            "SEC-CHX-003", "Data Exfiltration via DNS Tunneling",
            AttackCategory.DATA,
            "Simulate DNS-based data exfiltration to external resolver",
            "test-workstation", 180,
            hypothesis="DNS anomaly detection should alert within 2 minutes",
            blast_radius="isolated", severity="high",
            preconditions=["Test DNS resolver set up", "No real data used"],
            abort_conditions=["Real DNS traffic affected"],
        ),
        ChaosExperiment(
            "SEC-CHX-004", "Ransomware File Encryption Simulation",
            AttackCategory.DATA,
            "Simulate ransomware-like file encryption behavior on test data",
            "test-vm", 60,
            hypothesis="EDR should detect rapid file modification and alert SOC within 30s",
            blast_radius="isolated", severity="high",
            preconditions=["Test VM with synthetic data", "Snapshots taken"],
            abort_conditions=["Encryption reaches real file systems"],
        ),
        ChaosExperiment(
            "SEC-CHX-005", "Privilege Escalation via SUID Abuse",
            AttackCategory.PROCESS,
            "Simulate SUID binary abuse to gain root privileges",
            "test-linux-host", 90,
            hypothesis="Process monitoring/EDR should flag privilege change within 45s",
            blast_radius="isolated", severity="high",
            preconditions=["Test host isolated", "Audit logging enabled"],
            abort_conditions=["Escalation reaches production hosts"],
        ),
        ChaosExperiment(
            "SEC-CHX-006", "DDoS Simulation (Limited)",
            AttackCategory.AVAILABILITY,
            "Generate controlled traffic spike to test DDoS mitigation",
            "web-frontend", 120,
            hypothesis="Rate limiting and DDoS protection should activate within 60s",
            blast_radius="service", severity="medium",
            preconditions=["Load balancer configured", "CDN active"],
            abort_conditions=["Latency exceeds 5x baseline"],
        ),
    ]

    def __init__(self):
        self.steady_state = SteadyStateVerifier()
        self.validator = SecurityControlValidator()
        self.results: List[ExperimentResult] = []

    def run_experiment(self, experiment: ChaosExperiment) -> ExperimentResult:
        print(f"\n[CHAOS] Starting: {experiment.name}")
        print(f"  Hypothesis: {experiment.hypothesis}")
        print(f"  Target: {experiment.target} | Duration: {experiment.duration_sec}s")
        if not experiment.simulate_only:
            print("  WARNING: Running in LIVE mode")
        start = datetime.utcnow()
        # Pre-flight: verify steady state
        if not self.steady_state.is_healthy(experiment.target):
            result = ExperimentResult(
                experiment_id=experiment.experiment_id,
                status=ExperimentStatus.ABORTED,
                start_time=start.isoformat(),
                end_time=datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
                detection_time_sec=None, alert_fired=False,
                response_triggered=False,
                findings=["ABORTED: System not in steady state"],
                resilience_score=0.0,
                recommendation="Investigate pre-existing issues before running chaos",
            )
            self.results.append(result)
            return result
        print(f"  Pre-flight: PASSED (target healthy)")
        # Execute (simulated)
        print(f"  Executing attack simulation...")
        time.sleep(0.1)  # Simulate execution time
        detection = self.validator.simulate_detection(experiment)
        # Score resilience
        score = self._score_resilience(experiment, detection)
        findings = []
        if detection["detected"]:
            findings.append(f"DETECTED by: {', '.join(detection['controls_fired'])}")
            findings.append(f"Time to detect: {detection['time_to_detect_sec']:.0f}s")
            if detection["response_triggered"]:
                findings.append("Automated response triggered successfully")
        else:
            findings.append(f"NOT DETECTED - {experiment.category.value} attack evaded controls")
            findings.append("HYPOTHESIS FAILED: Security controls did not respond")
        # Post-experiment: verify steady state restored
        if self.steady_state.is_healthy(experiment.target):
            findings.append("System returned to steady state after experiment")
        recommendation = self._generate_recommendation(experiment, detection, score)
        result = ExperimentResult(
            experiment_id=experiment.experiment_id,
            status=ExperimentStatus.COMPLETED,
            start_time=start.isoformat(),
            end_time=datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            detection_time_sec=detection["time_to_detect_sec"],
            alert_fired=detection["detected"],
            response_triggered=detection["response_triggered"],
            findings=findings,
            resilience_score=score,
            recommendation=recommendation,
        )
        self.results.append(result)
        print(f"  Result: {'PASS' if detection['detected'] else 'FAIL'} | Score: {score:.0f}/100")
        return result

    def _score_resilience(self, exp: ChaosExperiment, detection: Dict) -> float:
        score = 0.0
        if detection["detected"]:
            score += 50
            ttd = detection.get("time_to_detect_sec", 600)
            if ttd < 30: score += 30
            elif ttd < 60: score += 20
            elif ttd < 300: score += 10
        if detection["response_triggered"]:
            score += 20
        return min(100, score)

    def _generate_recommendation(self, exp: ChaosExperiment,
                                   detection: Dict, score: float) -> str:
        if score >= 80:
            return f"Controls effective for {exp.category.value} attacks. Schedule next test in 90 days."
        elif score >= 50:
            return f"Improve {exp.category.value} detection speed. Target <60s MTTD."
        else:
            return (f"CRITICAL GAP: {exp.category.value} attack not detected. "
                    f"Implement {exp.category.value} monitoring immediately.")

    def run_all(self, categories: Optional[List[AttackCategory]] = None) -> Dict:
        experiments = self.EXPERIMENT_LIBRARY
        if categories:
            experiments = [e for e in experiments if e.category in categories]
        for exp in experiments:
            self.run_experiment(exp)
        return self.resilience_report()

    def resilience_report(self) -> Dict:
        if not self.results:
            return {}
        avg_score = sum(r.resilience_score for r in self.results) / len(self.results)
        gaps = [r for r in self.results if not r.alert_fired]
        return {
            "experiments_run": len(self.results),
            "average_resilience_score": round(avg_score, 1),
            "detection_gaps": len(gaps),
            "gap_details": [{"id": r.experiment_id, "recommendation": r.recommendation}
                            for r in gaps],
            "mean_time_to_detect_sec": round(
                sum(r.detection_time_sec for r in self.results if r.detection_time_sec) /
                max(sum(1 for r in self.results if r.detection_time_sec), 1), 1
            ),
            "overall_posture": "STRONG" if avg_score >= 80 else
                               "MODERATE" if avg_score >= 60 else "WEAK",
        }


if __name__ == "__main__":
    random.seed(42)
    engine = SecurityChaosEngine()
    print("Security Chaos Engineering Framework")
    print("All experiments run in SIMULATION mode (simulate_only=True)")
    report = engine.run_all()
    print(f"\n{'='*50}")
    print(f"RESILIENCE REPORT")
    print(f"{'='*50}")
    print(f"Experiments run: {report['experiments_run']}")
    print(f"Average resilience score: {report['average_resilience_score']}/100")
    print(f"Overall posture: {report['overall_posture']}")
    print(f"Mean time to detect: {report['mean_time_to_detect_sec']}s")
    print(f"Detection gaps: {report['detection_gaps']}")
    if report['gap_details']:
        print("Gaps to address:")
        for g in report['gap_details']:
            print(f"  {g['id']}: {g['recommendation']}")
```

instruction: Implement a supply chain security analyzer that monitors third-party software dependencies, build pipelines, and artifact registries for signs of supply chain compromise such as typosquatting, dependency confusion, and malicious package updates.

Build an API security testing framework that performs automated security assessments of REST APIs including authentication bypass, injection attacks, rate limiting validation, and OWASP API Top 10 checks.
output:
```python
import json
import re
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import List, Dict, Optional
from enum import Enum
from urllib.parse import urlparse, urlencode, urljoin

class OWASPCategory(Enum):
    API1_BROKEN_AUTH = "API1:2023 Broken Object Level Authorization"
    API2_BROKEN_AUTHN = "API2:2023 Broken Authentication"
    API3_BROKEN_PROP_AUTH = "API3:2023 Broken Object Property Level Authorization"
    API4_UNRESTRICTED_RESOURCE = "API4:2023 Unrestricted Resource Consumption"
    API5_BROKEN_FUNC_AUTH = "API5:2023 Broken Function Level Authorization"
    API6_SERVER_SIDE_REQUEST = "API6:2023 Server Side Request Forgery"
    API7_SECURITY_MISCONFIG = "API7:2023 Security Misconfiguration"
    API8_INJECTION = "API8:2023 Injection"
    API9_IMPROPER_ASSETS = "API9:2023 Improper Inventory Management"
    API10_UNSAFE_CONSUMPTION = "API10:2023 Unsafe Consumption of APIs"

@dataclass
class APIEndpoint:
    method: str
    path: str
    auth_required: bool = True
    params: Dict = field(default_factory=dict)
    headers: Dict = field(default_factory=dict)
    sample_body: Dict = field(default_factory=dict)
    roles_allowed: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)

@dataclass
class APIFinding:
    owasp_category: OWASPCategory
    severity: str
    endpoint: str
    method: str
    title: str
    description: str
    payload_used: str
    evidence: str
    cvss_score: float
    remediation: str

@dataclass
class APITestResult:
    test_name: str
    endpoint: str
    passed: bool
    finding: Optional[APIFinding]
    response_code: int
    response_snippet: str


class MockHTTPClient:
    """
    Simulated HTTP client for API security testing.
    In production, replace with requests.Session or httpx.
    Simulates realistic API responses for testing scenarios.
    """
    def __init__(self, base_url: str):
        self.base_url = base_url
        self._call_count: Dict[str, int] = {}

    def request(self, method: str, path: str, **kwargs) -> Dict:
        self._call_count[path] = self._call_count.get(path, 0) + 1
        count = self._call_count[path]
        headers = kwargs.get("headers", {})
        body = kwargs.get("json", {})
        params = kwargs.get("params", {})
        # Simulate security flaws for demo
        auth = headers.get("Authorization", "")
        # Broken auth: accept any JWT-like token
        if path.endswith("/admin") and not auth:
            return {"status": 403, "body": {"error": "Forbidden"}}
        # BOLA: return other user's data if ID changes
        if "/users/" in path and "999" in path:
            return {"status": 200, "body": {"id": 999, "email": "other@user.com",
                                             "ssn": "123-45-6789"}}
        # SQL injection response
        if any(p in str(body) + str(params) for p in ["' OR", "1=1", "--", "UNION"]):
            return {"status": 200, "body": {"users": [{"id": 1}, {"id": 2}]},
                    "injection_detected": True}
        # Rate limiting: fail after 20 requests to same endpoint
        if count > 20:
            return {"status": 429, "body": {"error": "Too many requests"},
                    "headers": {"Retry-After": "60"}}
        # SSRF test
        if any(ssrf in str(body) + str(params)
               for ssrf in ["localhost", "127.0.0.1", "169.254", "file://"]):
            return {"status": 200, "body": {"result": "Internal service response"},
                    "ssrf_triggered": True}
        # Security headers check
        if path == "/" or path == "/health":
            return {"status": 200, "body": {"status": "ok"},
                    "headers": {}}  # Missing security headers
        return {"status": 200, "body": {"success": True}}


class BrokenAuthTester:
    """Tests for API2: Broken Authentication."""

    def test_missing_auth(self, client: MockHTTPClient,
                           endpoint: APIEndpoint) -> APITestResult:
        resp = client.request(endpoint.method, endpoint.path, headers={})
        if resp["status"] == 200 and endpoint.auth_required:
            return APITestResult(
                test_name="missing_auth_token",
                endpoint=endpoint.path,
                passed=False,
                finding=APIFinding(
                    owasp_category=OWASPCategory.API2_BROKEN_AUTHN,
                    severity="CRITICAL",
                    endpoint=endpoint.path, method=endpoint.method,
                    title="Endpoint accessible without authentication",
                    description="Protected endpoint returns 200 with no auth token",
                    payload_used="No Authorization header",
                    evidence=f"HTTP {resp['status']} returned without auth",
                    cvss_score=9.1,
                    remediation="Implement JWT/OAuth2 middleware on all protected routes",
                ),
                response_code=resp["status"],
                response_snippet=str(resp["body"])[:100],
            )
        return APITestResult("missing_auth_token", endpoint.path, True, None,
                             resp["status"], str(resp["body"])[:100])

    def test_weak_jwt(self, client: MockHTTPClient,
                       endpoint: APIEndpoint) -> List[APITestResult]:
        results = []
        weak_tokens = [
            ("alg:none", "eyJhbGciOiJub25lIn0.eyJ1c2VyIjoiYWRtaW4ifQ."),
            ("expired", "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.eyJ1c2VyIjoiYWRtaW4iLCJleHAiOjF9.INVALID"),
        ]
        for label, token in weak_tokens:
            resp = client.request(endpoint.method, endpoint.path,
                                   headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"})
            if resp["status"] == 200:
                results.append(APITestResult(
                    f"weak_jwt_{label}", endpoint.path, False,
                    APIFinding(
                        OWASPCategory.API2_BROKEN_AUTHN, "CRITICAL",
                        endpoint.path, endpoint.method,
                        f"Accepts weak JWT ({label})",
                        f"API accepts {label} JWT without validation",
                        token[:30], f"HTTP 200 with {label} token",
                        9.0, "Validate JWT signature and expiry; reject alg=none",
                    ),
                    resp["status"], str(resp["body"])[:100]
                ))
        return results


class BOLATester:
    """Tests for API1: Broken Object Level Authorization."""

    def test_idor(self, client: MockHTTPClient,
                   base_path: str, own_id: str, other_id: str,
                   auth_headers: Dict) -> APITestResult:
        other_path = base_path.replace(own_id, other_id)
        resp = client.request("GET", other_path, headers=auth_headers)
        body = resp.get("body", {})
        # Check if sensitive data from other user is returned
        sensitive = any(k in str(body) for k in ["ssn", "email", "credit_card", "password"])
        if resp["status"] == 200 and sensitive:
            return APITestResult(
                "bola_idor", other_path, False,
                APIFinding(
                    OWASPCategory.API1_BROKEN_AUTH, "CRITICAL",
                    other_path, "GET",
                    "Broken Object Level Authorization (IDOR)",
                    f"User can access another user's sensitive data by changing ID",
                    f"Changed ID {own_id} -> {other_id}",
                    f"Returned: {str(body)[:80]}",
                    9.8,
                    "Validate object ownership server-side; never rely on client-supplied IDs alone",
                ),
                resp["status"], str(body)[:100]
            )
        return APITestResult("bola_idor", other_path, True, None,
                             resp["status"], str(body)[:100])


class InjectionTester:
    """Tests for API8: Injection (SQL, NoSQL, Command)."""

    SQL_PAYLOADS = [
        "' OR '1'='1", "' OR 1=1--", "1 UNION SELECT * FROM users--",
        "'; DROP TABLE users--", "' AND SLEEP(5)--",
    ]
    NOSQL_PAYLOADS = [
        '{"$gt": ""}', '{"$where": "this.password.length > 0"}',
    ]

    def test_sql_injection(self, client: MockHTTPClient,
                            endpoint: APIEndpoint, param: str) -> List[APITestResult]:
        results = []
        for payload in self.SQL_PAYLOADS[:3]:
            resp = client.request(endpoint.method, endpoint.path,
                                   params={param: payload})
            if resp.get("injection_detected") or (
                resp["status"] == 200 and
                any(k in str(resp["body"]) for k in ["users", "password", "admin"])
            ):
                results.append(APITestResult(
                    f"sql_injection_{param}", endpoint.path, False,
                    APIFinding(
                        OWASPCategory.API8_INJECTION, "CRITICAL",
                        endpoint.path, endpoint.method,
                        f"SQL Injection in parameter '{param}'",
                        f"Unparameterized query accepts SQL metacharacters",
                        payload, f"HTTP {resp['status']}: {str(resp['body'])[:80]}",
```
